title: W2: olean codec read — three validation levels over the extracted contract
description:
Implement the olean codec, read side (fln-olean, §7.2): header parsing (magic, version fields, base address), region relocation via the shared compacted-region machinery, ModuleData traversal into Grimoire environment structures, and the three validation levels — trust-the-producer (mmap speed, the Reference posture), structural audit (tag/bounds/reachability sweep, still fast), full recheck (Crucible over every constant — the G1 mode).

The layout inventory comes from OLEAN_CONTRACT.md (W1 extraction) with per-field provenance and a versioning note per Reference release in the supported epoch set. Hostile inputs are a first-class threat (R18): every decode path runs under resource budgets; a malformed olean yields typed ArtifactCorrupt / ArtifactEpochMismatch, never a panic and never unbounded work.

Acceptance: the full mathlib olean set at the pin loads at every validation level; read throughput meets the PG-8 floor (>= Reference load throughput) on the baseline profile; epoch-mismatch inputs produce the typed error with the correct epoch fields. Tests: the C3 artifact-archaeology corpus (valid + mutated oleans, every offset and tag targeted); decode-budget exhaustion fixtures; differential load (same olean into Reference and FrankenLean, environment statement-diff empty).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: fln-olean: preserve all pinned Import fields in ModuleData decoding
description:
Problem: the generated OLEAN contract at the v4.32.0 pin defines Lean.Import as four observable fields (module, importAll, isExported, isMeta), and Reference import closure explicitly uses all three flags to compute visibility/data/IR propagation. The former OleanView::module_data returned ModuleDataView.imports as Vec<String>: it decoded only a display rendering of the Name pointer, checked only pointer arity, silently discarded the three scalar Bool fields, and could collapse structurally distinct Names. Any environment import graph built from that view would permanently erase Reference semantics and could not satisfy fln-amv.9.

Scope: provide a typed decoded import record carrying the exact native fln_core::Name plus import_all, is_exported, and is_meta. Decode the physical pointer/scalar split from generated IMPORT_FIELDS and ABI layout facts rather than Rust layout or inferred defaults. Reject unsupported field inventories, validate compacted constructor pointer count and padded scalar storage, require canonical Bool encodings, charge cumulative semantic decode work to the existing resource budget, and preserve ModuleData.imports order and duplicates exactly. Update public examples/consumers directly; do not add a name-only compatibility shim. Document pin provenance and schema/epoch behavior.

Verification requirements: exhaustive fixtures cover all eight flag combinations; structural Name tests distinguish string and numeric components; multi-import fixtures prove exact order and duplicates; malformed constructor arity, short logical and physical scalar storage, noncanonical Bool values, and resource exhaustion return typed errors without partial output. Generated contract tests prove the field inventory matches IMPORT_FIELDS. Every row from selected real Reference-produced .olean fixtures is compared byte-for-byte with an independently recorded pinned-source oracle manifest covering import-all, exported/private, meta, and duplicate rows. Mutation-sensitive assertions must kill dropped, inverted, defaulted, or reordered fields. Extend the shared no-mock codec E2E lane with bounded schema-versioned row logs containing fixture identity/hash, import index/name/flags, expected/actual, contract epoch/commit, budget, claim/gate ids, outcome, divergence artifact, and final state. Run scoped format, complete fln-olean unit/integration/contract tests, the codec import-audit E2E, clippy with warnings denied, UBS excluding only documented guard-triggering false-positive categories, and applicable contract gates.

Dependency boundary: consuming this decoded type in the persistent environment graph is owned by fln-amv.9.1, and the complete decoder/graph/effective/provenance no-mock matrix is owned by fln-amv.9.4. Those obligations remain mandatory for the fln-amv.9 epic, but they are not prerequisites for closing this decoder bead because both beads depend transitively on this one. This avoids a semantic completion cycle without removing or weakening any downstream feature or test.

No hand-transcribed offsets, inferred flag, defaulting absent reads, display-only Name identity, name-only fallback, hosted Reference execution in a release path, or loss/reordering of duplicate imports.

## Steps to Reproduce

Decode a Reference-produced ModuleData containing import all, a private import, a meta import, or duplicate module rows with distinct flags. Under the former Vec<String> representation only dot-rendered module text survived, so all flag distinctions and structural Name identity were unavailable to downstream import-level computation.

## Acceptance Criteria

The typed decoder preserves exact Name structure, all three pinned flags, and exact array order with the complete positive, malformed, resource, contract, mutation-sensitive, real-fixture-manifest, and codec E2E coverage above. All stated scoped quality gates pass. Downstream graph obligations remain explicitly assigned to fln-amv.9.1/.9.4. No partial decoder, inferred flag, mock-only proof, unclassified skip, or deferred decoder requirement satisfies this bead.
close_reason: Lossless four-field Import decoding with exact Name identity, exhaustive malformed/resource tests, 50-row pinned oracle comparison, and safe shared codec E2E is complete; downstream environment integration remains in fln-amv.9.1/.9.4.

title: W2: Effects on asupersync — IO/Task semantics, lab-virtualizable, capability-observed
description:
Implement effects on asupersync (§6.7): IO, timers, filesystem, process, and task pools behind the Reference IO/Task semantics — Task.spawn/bind/get observable ordering, IO.Ref atomicity, IO.Process behavior, the interruption/checkSystem polling contract — displacing the vendored-libuv event loop wholesale.

Under the lab runtime the ENTIRE effect plane virtualizes (virtual time, injected faults, seeded schedules): this is how the Tribunal replays server sessions and torture-tests cancellation, and it is why no detached thread or raw OS handle may bypass the Cx discipline anywhere in the workspace. In faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process (drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance); sound mode can require declared capabilities and fail closed; the --pure-elab audit mode (W5) builds on this seam.

Acceptance: the G0-3 IO/Task parity micro-corpus passes on the production effect plane, then again under the lab runtime with virtual time (same observable results — the virtualization is invisible to compliant code); cancellation drains regions deterministically (obligation-leak oracle clean). Tests: Task ordering fixtures incl. nested spawn/bind chains; IO.Ref atomicity stress under lab schedules; checkSystem interruption-latency fixtures; capability-observation events verified in the provenance journal.

title: W2: Term store — total interning, alpha-canonical digest, expanded-weight budgets
description:
Implement the term store with total contractual interning (§7.1b): expressions, levels, and names hash-consed contractually, not opportunistically — one node per full structural identity INCLUDING binder names and mdata (metaprograms observe them; faithful Expr behavior demands exact round-trip), with pointer/ID equality BEING structural equality: Expr.equal collapses to a compare; kernel caches key on identity pairs.

Plus: an auxiliary alpha-canonical digest (binder names stripped) indexing kernel-side defeq/whnf caches so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work without confusing the metaprogram-visible plane. Sharing is maximal and MEASURED: every store keeps sharing statistics and enforces expanded-weight budgets — a term small on the wire can be astronomical unshared; an adversarial DAG-bomb is a first-class typed inspectable condition (§8.2c counts expanded weight, not bytes). Serialization preserves sharing exactly in both codecs.

Acceptance: interning invariant (structural equal implies pointer equal) holds under property tests across construction paths; alpha-digest cache sharing demonstrated on the G0-7 defeq fixtures; DAG-bomb fixtures produce typed Inconclusive under budget, never OOM or hang. Tests: sharing-statistics assertions on Corpus-derived fixtures; round-trip sharing preservation through both codecs; concurrent interning determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

title: W8: Lake surface — lakefile.lean on Golem, dual rebuild decisions, transactional resolution
description:
Implement the Lake surface (fln-lake, §13.3): lake CLI-compatible — lakefile.lean EXECUTES on Golem (the configuration DSL is just Lean; user code, so Golem executing it IS the compatibility mechanism), lakefile.toml takes the fast path, targets/facets map onto Ledger queries, require fetches via the D2 git tool through the shared subprocess capsule, lean-toolchain and elan layout honored so elan multiplexes FrankenLean toolchains transparently. Exit codes, --json, and the query surface match the pin.

The behavior upgrade is sound-mode and Behavior-Noted (BN-03): decl-granular no-op detection, with --faithful-invalidation restoring file-cone behavior for byte-for-byte CI reproduction studies. Internally TWO rebuild decisions are computed every time: the Reference decision (what the pin Lake would rebuild — faithful mode never omits an output upstream would produce, though it may satisfy it from a valid Ledger record and still project expected files/traces) and the native decision (which demand nodes actually changed); fln build explain <target> shows both, the delta, changed input identities, opaque barriers, and cache outcomes. Dependency resolution is transactional (a failed fetch never leaves a half-rewritten manifest presented as complete) and emits a resolution receipt; hermeticity is reported on the H0-H4 ladder, never overclaimed.

This bead also OWNS open question OQ-9: whether a native git-protocol client eventually replaces the D2 git tool, or git stays a permanent optional external — resolved here with the decision recorded in the plan decision log (the default posture is permanent D2 status, matching upstream Lake own contract; a native client is only justified if the closed-universe benefit outweighs the protocol-maintenance cost).

Acceptance: real Lake projects (incl. mathlib layout) configure and build unmodified; the Lake transcript corpus (C6) diffs clean on CLI surfaces; fln build explain output validated on fixture edits; OQ-9 decision recorded. Tests: lakefile.lean execution differentials; interrupted-fetch drills (C6 interrupted updates); manifest-atomicity fault drills; exit-code/JSON transcript fixtures.

This permits lakefile.lean and mathlib build validation as soon as the production runtime exists without dropping native_decide, VM, or wider W5 obligations from their own owners.

title: W5: Golem — FIR pipeline, FLBC interpreter, intrinsic table
description:
Workstream W5 "Golem" (§11): the metaprogram compiler and VM — the vehicle for ALL user-class Lean code (mathlib tactics, project macros, lakefiles, #eval, plugins) under the Oracle-Only Law (upstream implementation never rides it; the only Lean it executes is user code and source-elaborated library code per §4.3).

Pipeline: elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established disciplines: specialization, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, RC insertion with borrow inference + constructor-reuse/FBIP — performance-observable, the ecosystem tuned against it) -> FLBC register bytecode whose values ARE Marrow ABI objects (the quiet masterstroke: zero conversion across interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror facade — one calling convention for the whole membrane). Every pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with validators; the RC-insertion ownership validator is simpler than the optimizer it audits. Interpreter: threaded dispatch, inline caches, allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically, Reference semantics for panics/partial/stack/checkSystem (PG-7 floor: >= 3x Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus). Intrinsic table generated from the @[extern] census (Appendix C). Initialization ordering + capability-scoped effects incl. --pure-elab audit mode (§11.5). native_decide staged repair Tier 1: replay receipts (§11.6).

Exit: gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" — user-class code runs with #eval/IO/Task parity, Reference-built plugins load and run, intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — the workstream exit includes the explicit no-mock integration bead franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe. Individual compiler, interpreter, intrinsic, effect, and plugin-loader beads may close on their stable component contracts; the W5 epic and G2 evidence cannot close until the real user-code stack is exercised end to end with no production test stubs.

Closure topology: the concrete integration bead feeds Gate G2, and this W5 epic depends on G2 as its declared convergence exit while retaining every component and wider W5 requirement. G2 does not wait on the aggregation epic. This prevents an empty epic-close hop from delaying the first production execution proof without narrowing FIR, VM, intrinsic, effect, plugin, receipt, or performance scope.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: Baseline performance report — measure the Reference before ratifying gates
description:
Produce the W1 baseline performance report (§19.1): measure the Reference at the pin on the two pinned bare-metal profiles (32-core x86-64 Linux; Apple-silicon macOS) BEFORE any gate number is finalized. Every §19.2 gate ratio is ratified or amended against this report — a gate number without a measured baseline is marketing.

Measure: full-Corpus build wall (cold and warm-cache); per-file elaboration time distribution (p50/p95/p99); kernel-recheck time of the olean set; server worker import/attach time and RSS per worker on representative mathlib files; first-goal latency distribution; the interpreter micro-corpus; the bv_decide corpus with CaDiCaL. Record host facts, isolation checks, cache states, sample counts, and RAW samples (runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in — §19.2 discipline).

Acceptance: the report is a checked-in, regenerable artifact (script + data + rendered summary) with every number carrying corpus, machine, cache state, and dispersion; the PG-1..PG-M table is re-ratified against it and any amendment is recorded in the plan decision log. Tests: the measurement scripts are deterministic in methodology (seeded ordering, pinned thread counts) and re-runnable by one command; a second run on the same host must reproduce medians within the declared variance budget.

title: W2: D2 subprocess capsule — the one sanctioned tool-spawn path, fully provenanced
description:
Implement the D2 subprocess protocol as one shared capsule (Rule D2): the single implementation through which the ONLY two external tools FrankenLean may ever spawn — the system C compiler (only from the --backend c code generator, mirroring upstream leanc) and system git (only from Lake-compatible dependency fetching, mirroring upstream Lake) — are invoked. Nothing else in the program spawns processes, ever (no cc at check time, no CaDiCaL, no curl, no tar, no LaTeX), and CI enforces that this capsule is the only process-spawn site outside the Tribunal sandbox.

The protocol, in full: argv-only invocation (no shell interpretation, ever); per-job private temp directories; timeouts with process-TREE cancellation (no orphaned grandchildren); output-size limits; environment allowlist (nothing ambient leaks in); atomic result publication (a killed job leaves staging, never a half-written product); and the executable path AND CONTENT HASH recorded into provenance so a build record names exactly which cc/git produced what. Absence of a tool yields a typed CapabilityDenied error NAMING the alternative (Iron for cc; atp/HTTPS CAS sync guidance for git) — never silent substitution. Products of both tools are excluded from --reproducible artifact certification by construction (D17), and the Reference toolchain is NOT a third tool: a release check proves no fln binary can locate, spawn, or link it (D8).

Acceptance: leanc --backend c and Lake require both route through the capsule with full provenance rows; the spawn-site CI scan finds exactly one call site; kill/timeout drills leave no orphan processes and no partial products; absent-tool fixtures produce the typed error with the named alternative. Tests: process-tree cancellation drills (grandchild spawned by cc must die with the job); env-allowlist tests (a canary env var must not reach the child); output-bomb tests (limit enforced, typed failure, no OOM); provenance-hash tests (swapping the cc binary changes the recorded content hash and invalidates dependent D4-class records).

title: W7: norm_num + omega — native certificate-producing arithmetic cores
description:
Implement norm_num/omega native cores (§12.4): norm_num-class evaluation and the omega decision procedure with native cores (fln-bignum-backed), certificate-producing, kernel-replayed — every result enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact (FL-INV-06; the engines are untrusted by constitution, which is what makes native reimplementation safe).

Scope: numeric normalization over the Reference-visible semiring/ring/field structure conventions; omega over linear integer arithmetic with the upstream-observable goal coverage; certificates in the §8.6 discipline (simpler-than-recomputation checkers, recompute fallback); facade rows for the tactic entry points with golden-trace fixtures from the instrumented oracle.

Acceptance: agreement with the Reference on the norm_num/omega corpus slices (same goals closed, same goals refused); certificates replay through Crucible; performance within the envelope the PG-2 model assigns to these lanes. Tests: differential goal-closure fixtures; certificate round-trip + tamper tests; property tests over generated arithmetic goals (agreement with a big-integer reference evaluator); budget-exhaustion yields typed Inconclusive per FL-INV-07.

title: W4: Quill engine — Pratt parser, lossless green trees, exact SourceInfo
description:
Implement the Quill engine (fln-parse, fln-syntax; §9.1): token tables + Pratt-precedence parsing with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, and SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic positions, leading/trailing whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions exactly as upstream computes them — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract: parsing INTERLEAVES with elaboration (a command can install parsers used three lines later), commands install syntax/macro/elab rules visible immediately, precedence interactions match.

Internals (ours to design): lossless rope + persistent green tree (exact bytes, trivia, comments, UTF-8/UTF-16 positions) with red views adding context pointers per document snapshot; every node carries an origin set (literal span, macro-definition span, macro-call span, quotation spans, recovered markers) projected to the Reference single-primary-span convention at the wire while the full provenance graph feeds Palimpsest.

Acceptance: G0-4 curated corpus trees/positions byte-agree; C1/C2 parse products diff clean against the Reference frontend under instrumentation; the interleaving contract demonstrated (fixture where a command installs syntax used later in the same file). Tests: token-table and precedence property tests; UTF-8/UTF-16 position conversion fixtures (LSP boundary); green-tree losslessness (parse then print = identical bytes) across the corpus.

title: W12: Distribution — static binaries, elan layout, reproducible dual-builds, D8 release audit
description:
Implement distribution (§17.1, §22.2 W12): static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line names a FrankenLean toolchain and elan multiplexes it transparently; the install script; side-by-side epoch installs; the version-line law — parse-compatible with the Reference (version/commit fields preserved) plus a separate machine-readable implementation field; fln identity --json (implementation commit, epoch, profile, lockfile, TCB hash, evidence freshness); an exact-reference transcript mode for conformance harnesses only. Compatibility never becomes deception (§17.1).

Reproducible releases: release binaries built TWICE in isolated builders and byte-compared (divergence blocks); --reproducible certified across the matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64; windows bit-certification a separate declared decision, D17); the stdlib fixpoint (elaborating Init/Std from clean state twice — and once more through a freshly rebuilt toolchain — produces bit-identical oleans and logical roots: the native-world descendant of the classic triple bootstrap). The D8 release check: CI proves release binaries contain no symbol, path, or capability by which the Reference could be located, spawned, or linked; the ORACLE_FALLBACK harness is compiled out and proven absent.

Acceptance: cold-machine install (C9: clean-machine, air-gapped, path/locale variation) yields a working toolchain that builds a real project; dual-build byte-identity green; elan integration verified against real elan. Tests: install-script matrix; C9 bootstrap/reproducibility corpus; release-content audit (symbol scan, capability scan); toolchain-switching fixtures via lean-toolchain files.

title: W1: Workspace scaffolding — §21 crate map, pinned nightly, unsafe-ledger CI, layering checks
description:
Scaffold the cargo workspace per the cycle-free crate map (§21): every fln-* crate stubbed with its one-line charter as crate-level docs — fln-core, fln-hash, fln-bignum, fln-libm, fln-rt, fln-unsafe-abi, fln-unsafe-region, fln-env, fln-olean, fln-kernel, fln-checker, fln-parse, fln-syntax, fln-elab, fln-comp, fln-vm, fln-unsafe-jit (feature iron), fln-anvil, fln-verdict, fln-ledger, fln-lake, fln-server, fln-trace, fln-hound, fln-doc, fln-mcp, fln-tui, fln-cli, fln (library facade), fln-conformance, fln-wasm — plus vendor/lean4-src at the pin (three D8 roles only, Apache-2.0 notices carried, never linked).

Structural enforcement from day one: rust-toolchain.toml pinning the exact nightly (no or-later); #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at every ordinary crate root; the three fln-unsafe-* boundary crates with #![deny(unsafe_code)] roots and the unsafe-boundary ledger file (path, invariant, evidence, safe fallback, no-claim boundary per row); CI checks that (a) walk the dependency graph and fail on any edge violating the strictly-downward §5 layering or the D3 structural laws (no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe crate exports a type launderable into a checked declaration), (b) reject any unledgered unsafe site, (c) enforce the fln-kernel line-count covenant once the crate has content.

Acceptance: cargo check --all-targets green on the stubbed workspace from a cold checkout; the layering CI check demonstrably fails on a seeded illegal edge (add a test-only violation in CI to prove detection, then remove); the unsafe-ledger check fails on a seeded unledgered allow site. Tests: the CI checks themselves plus a workspace-graph snapshot test that any new crate or edge must be explicitly acknowledged in a reviewed file.

## Acceptance Criteria

No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — close repository-configuration and bootstrap gaps in the structural contract. Until an explicit reviewed Cargo-config contract exists, both `.cargo/config.toml` and legacy `.cargo/config` are forbidden because they can inject lint caps, compiler wrappers, linkers/runners, and source replacement outside Cargo.toml/WORKSPACE_GRAPH. The exact nightly must be validated structurally as one `[toolchain]` table with one dated channel and recognized typed keys only; path, decoy-section, duplicate, and unknown-key forms fail closed. CI performs the same SUITE.lock↔rust-toolchain validation with system Python before any repository-selected rustc executes, installs the explicit lock channel, and checks rustc commit via `rustup run <channel> rustc -Vv`. Seed both Cargo-config filenames and malformed/ambiguous toolchain shapes; retain the original layering, unsafe-ledger, closure, line-covenant, and no-mock evidence requirements unchanged.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — seal ambient compiler configuration. The structural lint posture is not sufficient by itself: the pinned rustc accepts unsafe code under both crate-level deny(unsafe_code) and forbid(unsafe_code) when `--cap-lints allow` is injected. Every authoritative gate must therefore invoke the exact locked cargo/rustc explicitly and fail closed or sanitize all ambient compiler/configuration channels, including RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS, rustc wrapper overrides, RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, Cargo home/ancestor config, target-specific rustflags, and any equivalent channel discovered by the implementation. The seal must not destroy user state or shared caches: use isolated per-run homes/targets or validated read-only inputs. Log the effective compiler identity and admitted environment in the evidence envelope. Keep source-level unsafe-ledger scanning as an independent defense; environment sealing does not replace it.
acceptance_criteria: All original description requirements and the mandatory verification definition of done remain binding. In addition, the hostile-environment mutation lane must prove the gate cannot be made to accept an unledgered unsafe operation by injecting `--cap-lints allow`, a fake rustc/workspace wrapper, an alternate RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, target-specific rustflags, or Cargo config from the repository, an ancestor directory, or Cargo home. Each case must produce a typed pre-build rejection or demonstrate that the channel was excluded, followed by a clean recovery pass. Unit tests also reject duplicate/repeated root workspace and package/dependency keys or sections so the constrained manifest parser fails closed on ambiguous TOML instead of silently choosing a last value.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: CI bootstrap hardening landed in 23d1a32. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Adversarial review of tools/structure-guard (Claude Fable session, via a dedicated review subagent, 2026-07-22). Reporting rather than editing since CopperHare is actively in this crate. The tool is very well-hardened — the subagent tried every smuggling class from the brief and confirmed SOLID: target-cfg/sub-table/dotted-key/renamed(package=)/workspace-inherited deps all fail closed to FLN-STRUCT-016; dev- and build-dependencies ARE collected and subject to layering+D3 (the 'hide an upward edge in dev-deps' attack is closed); equal-rank edges rejected (fr<=tr); D3 transitive BFS uses the full edge set; root-lint spoofing defeated (cfg_attr/nested-mod/string/macro decoys); unsafe-ledger scan walks the filesystem (not a hardcoded list) incl. tests/examples/benches/build.rs and ignores UNSAFE-LEDGER: inside string literals; symlink escapes flagged; all governance parsers fail closed on missing-schema/decoy-section/dup-key/short-hash; exit-code/report integrity correct (every finding->exit 1, no dedup drop, no severity downgrade). FINDINGS:
1) MEDIUM (real gap): audit_repository_cargo_config (checks.rs:166-186) checks ONLY <root>/.cargo/config.toml and <root>/.cargo/config. Nothing scans nested .cargo/ dirs. A regular file at crates/fln-kernel/.cargo/config.toml (or crates/.cargo/, tools/*/.cargo/) is invisible — yet cargo merges config from the invocation dir upward, so any per-crate 'cd crates/X && cargo ...' step picks up an injected rustc-wrapper/runner/linker/[source]-replacement OUTSIDE the reviewed graph. Contradicts the check's own doc comment. Fix: reuse the existing crates/+tools/ walk (already done in scan_symlinks) to emit FLN-STRUCT-016 for any .cargo/config(.toml) at any depth, not just root.
2) LOW-MED: a single non-UTF-8/unreadable governed .rs or manifest makes fs::read_to_string(...)? propagate to a whole-run exit 2, masking all other findings (griefing/diagnosability). Manifest PARSE errors are already localized to FLN-STRUCT-016; do the same for unreadable/non-UTF-8 source files instead of ?-to-exit-2.
3) LOW: legacy plain 'rust-toolchain' (no .toml) is never audited though the tool forbids the legacy .cargo/config filename by the same reasoning (defense-in-depth; rustup prefers .toml when both exist).
4) LOW: suite-dep only required to be is_some() path dep; not checked against the SUITE.lock path= (code comments 'SUITE.lock pending' — documented limitation).
5) LOW: LOC covenant (FLN-STRUCT-015) counts written lines; a macro_rules! in fln-kernel can expand under the 12k cap (inherent line-counter limit).
6) INFO: AllowSite doc says same-line marker allowed but scan_allow_sites only inspects lines above (fails closed/over-strict — doc/behavior mismatch).
Bottom line: no 'unsafe/upward edge ships green' hole; #1 is the one worth fixing promptly.

title: W2: Owned allocator — size-classed, heartbeat hook, membrane discipline
description:
Implement the owned allocator (§6.3): size-classed, mimalloc-tradition (thread-local pages, size-class bins, deferred frees) — owned rather than off-the-shelf because allocation behavior is SEMANTICALLY visible through heartbeat accounting (fuel parity, §4.2/§8.5) and performance-visible everywhere.

The heartbeat hook lives here: one counting law, calibrated by spike G0-6, shared by both kernel engines and Golem (allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically across interpreted and native paths). The allocator must also serve the CompatHeap discipline (§6.1b): membrane-only allocation and free, so foreign code observes exactly the Reference allocation contract at the ABI.

Acceptance: fuel-parity fixtures from G0-6 stay green when running over the real allocator; allocation-heavy corpus benchmarks show no pathological regression vs the baseline report envelope; zero leaks over the PG-6 four-hour soak profile (measured under the lab runtime with the leak oracle). Tests: size-class boundary unit tests; concurrent alloc/free stress under the asupersync lab with seed replay; heartbeat-count determinism test at thread counts {1, 8, 32} (same input, same tick totals — FL-INV-01 applies to fuel too).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W1: Performance-gate harness — bench binaries, baselines, tail gates, flame artifacts
description:
Build the performance-gate harness (§19.2, §19.1 discipline): the infrastructure every PG gate (PG-1 the Judge, PG-2 cold corpus, PG-3 the cone, PG-4 attach, PG-5 determinism, PG-6 memory, PG-7 Golem, PG-8 codec, PG-9 Verdict, PG-K bignum, PG-10 cache, PG-L latency, PG-1b consensus tax, PG-M the Mirror) plugs into — one harness, not fourteen ad-hoc scripts.

Per gate: a bench binary; a committed, versioned baseline; a variance budget; alert-first thresholds graduating to blocking after two releases of baseline (the manim discipline); regression gating on TAILS as well as medians (interactive p95 > 5% blocks; kernel-throughput > 5% blocks; build wall/RSS > 7% blocks); a flamegraph artifact automatically attached on regression identifying the bottleneck; a rollback path recorded for risky changes. Every benchmark bundle records host facts, isolation checks, cache state, sample counts, and RAW samples — runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in. Scaling profiles (parallel efficiency at {1,8,32,96} threads for elaboration-bound and check-bound workloads) publish per release. Every published number carries its §20.4 claim state: OBSERVED on a named corpus and machine, or TARGETED — documentation CI (W12 Witness) enforces that the README can never say "2x faster than Lean" off one warm-cache loop.

Detailed logging is constitutive: each bench run emits structured NDJSON (per-phase timings, host fingerprint, cache state, sample-by-sample results) archived as CI artifacts so any regression is diagnosable from artifacts alone. Acceptance: the harness runs the first real gate end-to-end (PG-8 or PG-K are the earliest candidates) against the W1 baseline with the full bundle emitted; a seeded regression trips the tail gate and attaches the flame artifact. Tests: harness self-test with planted regressions per threshold class; baseline-drift detection (a silently edited baseline fails CI); host-quality-check rejection fixtures; bundle-schema contract tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W2: Grimoire environment — persistent maps, O(1) snapshots, extension registry, logical roots
description:
Implement the full Grimoire environment (fln-env, plan §7.1): semantically the Reference environment over axioms, definitions, theorems, opaques, inductives, constructors, recursors, and quotients; environment extensions with import-time replay preserving Reference-observable ordering; pinned attribute state; and the exact module import graph. Mechanically use persistent HAMT-style maps, persistent journals, and immutable combined commit state with structural sharing so snapshots are O(1), the primitive under Athanor speculation, Lantern request views, and Envoy search trees.

Key contracts are cumulative and non-negotiable. Mathlib custom extensions round-trip as region data without executing upstream implementation code. Opaque extension payloads are preserved byte-exact, flagged in provenance, and honestly block fine invalidation/migration through them. Every extension declares merge and checkpoint semantics in a registry, and SetUnion maintains a separate evidence-anchored semantic view if raw replay bytes are observable. Declaration identity uses explicit frozen kind tags and includes every semantic field. Full-hash collision buckets are canonical and resource bounded.

Each immutable commit publishes one coherent state across declarations, extension registry/journals, attributes, module DAG, actual Arc-backed module payload contributions, canonical identity-only provenance manifest, and both provenance index directions. The canonical identity schema never substitutes names/digests for actual ConstantInfo or payload equality; atomic application preflights a one-to-one binding and collision cases. Paired provenance checkpoints preserve contributor/range identity. Bounded invalidation returns canonical witnesses and honest Definitely/Conservatively/Inconclusive/InternalFault grades. Three-way merge consumes a revalidated provenance merge plan and publishes all semantic state together or nothing.

Roots stay typed and separate. The logical root covers declarations, extension deltas/contracts, attributes/options exactly where their contracts say they are logical, and excludes wall clock, paths, scheduling, and import/provenance metadata. The module provenance root covers topology, ownership, artifact evidence, ranges, and completeness. Operational metadata has its own root. Metadata-only topology/provenance changes cannot alter logical_root; actual declaration/extension publication changes it exactly through canonical Environment rules. Ledger/receipt consumers carry the needed roots explicitly rather than silently folding one into another.

Acceptance requires real pinned olean modules to materialize through the identity plus payload transaction adapter; extension replay, import flags/effective closure, attributes, actual contribution indexes, separate roots, invalidation, paired checkpoint/restore, and combined merge match Reference/oracle manifests at the earned evidence level. Snapshot fork/read cost is O(1) with sharing/allocation evidence. Authoritative products, diagnostics, witnesses, roots, and normalized logs are stable across insertion orders, thread matrices, and certified hosts. Malformed, stale, collision, opaque/partial/missing, resource, cancellation, nonpublication, and recovery cases are typed and logged; no Inconclusive result is cached as accept/reject.

## Acceptance Criteria

All component beads through fln-amv.11 are closed without narrowed scope, and every declaration/extension/attribute/module/provenance/payload/index/root behavior above is integrated as one atomic persistent commit. Named unit/property/model/mutation suites, real no-mock positive plus negative/inconclusive/nonpublication/recovery E2E scenarios, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, sharing/allocation and determinism evidence, generated contract drift checks, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, scripts/check.sh, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and all parent gates are green. No mock-only release claim, Environment-only merge, name/digest-only application, hidden root change, partial publication, unclassified skip, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.
design: Closure topology: this parent directly joins fln-amv.9 module-graph epic closure and fln-amv.11 global atomic merge, in addition to the foundational core/hash/region contracts. fln-amv.11 consumes the concrete fln-amv.9.4 integration output rather than waiting on the aggregation epic. Thus module acceptance and global merge are both mandatory but neither is artificially serialized behind the other; the parent closes only when both are closed.

MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.

Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review sweep (Claude Fable session, 2026-07-22): fixed a content-addressing soundness bug in environment.rs decl_content_digest. Defn and Thm folded their 'all' (mutual-block) field into the digest, but Induct and Rec OMITTED theirs — so two inductives (or recursors) identical except for their mutual-block grouping produced the SAME Domain::DeclContent digest. Since this digest is the per-declaration CAS/Ledger content key, that collision could return a declaration carrying a stale 'all' field on a cache hit across a regrouping. Added 'all' to the Induct and Rec digest arms (mirroring Defn/Thm) and a regression 'mutual_block_membership_changes_the_content_digest' covering all four kinds. Mutation-kill verified: with the fix reverted the test fails on Induct/Rec (assert_ne on solo vs grouped 'all'); with it applied all four pass. No digest values were pinned anywhere, and no durable artifacts exist yet, so changing the inductive/recursor digest is safe. Gates: rustfmt, clippy -p fln-env -D warnings, 21/21 fln-env tests green, UBS 0 critical (isolated target).

title: fln-env: include opaque mutual-block membership in declaration content digests
description:
Problem: Environment::decl_content_digest encodes the mutual-block member list for definitions, theorems, inductives, and recursors, but omits OpaqueVal.all. Two opaque declarations that differ only in their mutual-block membership can therefore receive the same declaration content digest and contaminate Ledger/CAS identity and logical environment roots. Scope: encode OpaqueVal.all with the same canonical length-plus-ordered-name representation as every other all-bearing ConstantInfo variant. Preserve all existing digest fields and ordering. Verification requirements: (1) unit regression proves solo versus grouped opaque membership produces distinct digests; (2) one table-driven regression covers every current all-bearing variant: Defn, Thm, Opaque, Induct, and Rec; (3) environment logical-root coverage proves the corrected declaration digest propagates to snapshot identity; (4) the existing environment snapshot E2E lane is extended or explicitly demonstrated to emit detailed, versioned, line-oriented scenario logs containing case, expected digest relation, actual digest relation, and final result; (5) cargo fmt, fln-env unit tests, fln-env clippy with warnings denied, and UBS pass. No compatibility shim and no unrelated codec changes.

## Steps to Reproduce

Create otherwise-identical Opaque declarations whose `all` lists differ in length, order, or member name. Before the fix, `Environment::decl_content_digest` and the resulting logical roots are equal because `OpaqueVal.all` is never encoded.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut in the description is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. The added membership encoding must preserve the existing digest domain and canonical order, run in O(member count plus canonical member-name bytes), allocate no structure proportional to the whole Environment, and introduce no quadratic member-list pass. Boundary tests cover empty, singleton, repeated, reordered, and a declared large-member fixture; the E2E record includes member_count, canonical_member_bytes, digest/root ids, elapsed measurement, and the applicable construction/import budget. Repeating the same declaration and budget must produce the same digest and logical root. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, compatibility shim, deferred listed feature, or performance assertion without the recorded fixture satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: canonicalize full-hash collision buckets for schedule-independent iteration
description:
Problem: PMap iteration is insertion-order independent only for non-colliding hashes. Distinct keys with the same complete 64-bit key_hash are appended to a leaf bucket, so bucket iteration, Debug output, and any future environment enumeration depend on insertion/scheduling order. This violates FL-INV-01 under adversarial Name collisions even though LogicalRootBuilder currently masks the defect by sorting again. Scope: give PKey a deterministic total tie-break consistent with Eq and maintain every full-hash collision bucket in canonical key order during insert/overwrite/remove; do not sort the entire map at iteration time or rely on randomized hashing. For Name, use the pinned Name.cmp order after its stack-safety prerequisite is complete. Document the CGSE policy and complexity for large collision buckets. Verification requirements: permutation/property tests use many distinct constant-hash keys and prove identical iteration/Debug/equality after insert, overwrite, remove, fork, and reinsert; mixed near-collision tries exercise every hash chunk and the final partial chunk; a schedule matrix constructs the same environment in several thread partitions and compares enumerations and roots; deep valid names remain bounded once the Name-order prerequisite lands. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with versioned detailed logs containing collision cardinality, insertion schedule, canonical key order, expected/actual enumeration and roots, operation counts, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No iteration-time global sort, address-based tie-break, randomized seed, or collision rejection.

## Steps to Reproduce

Insert the same set of distinct constant-hash keys in forward and reverse order, then compare `PMap::iter` and Debug output. The current collision leaf retains insertion order, so the two enumerations differ.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: integrate atomic three-way environment merge across all semantic state
description:
Final child integration for fln-env: component APIs can merge individual structures, but speculative branches need one atomic three-way merge across the combined committed state. After every dependency lands, implement a typed merge of base, ours, and theirs containing Environment declarations/extensions, attribute state, module graph, canonical provenance manifest, actual payload-bearing contribution state, and both provenance index directions. A plain Environment-only merge is insufficient.

The lifecycle child of fln-amv.9.3 produces the exact ProvenanceMergePlan. Global merge must consume and immediately revalidate that plan against base/ours/theirs logical roots, provenance roots, schema/epoch, actual declaration and extension values, ranges, contributor identities, and resource envelope before any publication. It must not independently reconstruct a second provenance truth. A stale, mismatched, incomplete, or value-collision plan is a typed atomic refusal. The final commit publishes Environment plus graph/provenance/index state together, or publishes nothing.

Constants added on disjoint names combine; identical same-name additions are idempotent only after actual ConstantInfo equality, not digest equality; different same-name declarations and equal-digest unequal-value cases conflict with both origins and canonical identities. Extension registration/journals follow declared merge and checkpoint laws, including new-on-one-side, identical-new-on-both, AppendOrdered precedence, SetUnion semantic-view/raw-journal law, descriptor/ancestry/range mismatch, opaque provenance, and review-required conflicts. Attribute state follows its pinned contract. Import topology, actual module contributions, completeness grades, contributor indexes, and invalidation roots follow the revalidated provenance plan. Missing or partial input remains explicitly graded and never becomes verified completeness or rejection.

Aggregate every independent conflict in frozen category then canonical Name/module/range order rather than stopping at map traversal order. Define deterministic ours/theirs precedence only where AppendOrdered makes it observable. Before allocating conflict/output collections, enforce independent limits for declarations, extension rows/bytes, attribute rows, modules/import rows, provenance/index rows, names/depth, conflicts/witness bytes, total output bytes, and merge work. Cancellation is observed at fixed deterministic boundaries. Exhaustion or cancellation returns Inconclusive with usage facts and no authoritative partial environment; InternalFault covers inconsistent validated-state/index/plan invariants. Neither outcome is cached as accept/reject.

Root behavior is explicit and checked twice, before and after publication construction. Metadata-only module/provenance changes do not affect logical_root. Merged declarations, extension deltas, attributes when contractually logical, and options affect logical_root exactly through their canonical builders. Provenance root covers topology/ownership/evidence/completeness and remains separate. Operational metadata affects neither. The result reports base/ours/theirs/final logical and provenance roots plus transition reasons, and preserves structural sharing for every unchanged trie, journal chunk, module record, contribution array, index branch, and payload Arc.

Verification uses independent Vec/BTreeMap models for every component and the combined commit. Random fork/edit/merge tests and exhaustive tables cover no-op, one-sided, disjoint, identical, conflicting, opaque/partial/missing, all extension policies, attributes, topology, actual contributions, equal-digest collisions, stale plans/roots/bases, multi-conflict aggregation, every exact resource boundary, cancellation point, Inconclusive recovery, and InternalFault nonpublication. Branch permutations, insertion orders, and thread partitions yield byte-identical authoritative products, diagnostics, roots, and provenance plans. Failure-atomicity and allocation/pointer instrumentation prove unchanged state remains shared. Named mutants drop a declaration, extension, attribute, module, forward/reverse provenance row, plan revalidation, conflict category, completeness grade, root transition, or sharing edge.

Extend the shared no-mock environment E2E lane with a real multi-module branch/merge/failure/recovery scenario and detailed schema-versioned line logs containing snapshot/plan ids, base/ours/theirs roots, actual branch edits and origins, merge policy, ordered conflicts, contribution/index/range facts, completeness and authority grade, logical/provenance transition reasons, sharing/allocation/resource facts, publication/cacheability flags, expected/actual result, recovery action, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env and fln-hash/domain tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, every component scenario, and parent gates.

No last-writer-wins declaration overwrite, digest-only equality, dropped extension/attribute/module/provenance state, stale merge plan, independently rebuilt provenance truth, partial success, authoritative inconclusive result, hidden logical-root change, or traversal-order diagnostic drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

The global three-way merge must consume and revalidate the provenance lifecycle plan and atomically publish the entire combined committed state across declarations, extensions, attributes, module graph, actual payload contributions, completeness, and both index directions. Every success/conflict/stale/collision/resource/cancellation/internal-fault/root-transition/sharing case, independent combined model, permutation/thread matrix, named mutant, detailed real E2E positive-negative-recovery log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, component gate, and parent gate above is mandatory. Environment-only merge, partial publication, or deferred semantic state fails closure.
design: Dependency topology: global merge consumes the concrete module integration output of fln-amv.9.4 plus attribute state and every other component contract. It does not consume an implementation artifact from the fln-amv.9 aggregation epic. The fln-amv parent separately joins both fln-amv.9 epic closure and this global merge, so the complete module acceptance matrix remains mandatory while module-epic bookkeeping and merge implementation can close in parallel. No component proof is weakened or bypassed by this topology.
acceptance_criteria: The global merge consumes and revalidates the exact provenance plan, then atomically publishes the complete Environment plus attribute/module/provenance/payload/index state with correct separate roots and sharing. All actual-value equality/collision, extension-policy, completeness, conflict aggregation, stale precondition, FL-INV-07, resource, model, metamorphic, mutant, detailed no-mock E2E, test/fmt/clippy/UBS/domain/component/parent requirements in the description must be green. No Environment-only or partial merge satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: replace implicit Rust discriminants in declaration identity
description:
Problem: Environment::decl_content_digest encodes DefinitionSafety and QuotKind with `as u8`, binding durable declaration/CAS identity to implicit Rust source-order discriminants. Reordering an enum, inserting a variant, or changing representation can silently rewrite logical roots without an epoch/schema decision, and new variants do not force the identity code to choose a stable tag deliberately. Scope: replace both casts with exhaustive explicit canonical tag matches, document the stable mapping as part of Domain::DeclContent, and keep every other field and byte order unchanged. Any future variant must fail compilation here until assigned an intentional schema value. Verification requirements: table-driven unit tests cover all DefinitionSafety and QuotKind variants, prove pairwise distinct content digests and stable repeated roots, and include fixed canonical tag expectations independent of enum declaration order; a deliberate cast-based encoder must be detected by the test or policy check. Existing declaration-field, logical-root, insertion-order, and thread determinism tests remain green. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed versioned records containing variant, schema tag, expected/actual digest and root, epoch, and final result. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No repr-based workaround, debug-string encoding, or silent renumbering.

## Steps to Reproduce

Observe that declaration identity writes `DefinitionSafety` and `QuotKind` with `as u8`. Reordering either Rust enum changes durable digests despite no explicit identity-schema edit; the fixed-tag regression demonstrates the intended stable mapping.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: make adversarial full-hash collision handling resource-bounded
description:
Problem: a PMap full-hash collision leaf is a Vec. Looking up/removing a key is linear in collision cardinality, and persistent insertion clones the entire bucket, so constructing N distinct same-hash names is quadratic in comparisons and copied entries. Canonicalizing order in fln-amv.10 fixes determinism but not this denial-of-service/resource-accounting surface. Scope: after the canonical tie-break contract lands, replace large collision buckets with an immutable deterministic secondary structure or tiered representation that gives bounded/sublinear lookup and avoids whole-bucket copying per update while preserving O(1) snapshots, exact Eq semantics, canonical iteration, and small-bucket efficiency. Define promotion/demotion thresholds, secondary key identity, collision-of-secondary-key behavior, allocation/expanded-weight budgets, typed resource exhaustion, and structural-sharing rules; never reject a valid declaration merely because hashes collide. Verification requirements: model/property tests cover promotion, overwrite, remove, collapse, fork, equality, and iteration against BTreeMap for random and all-colliding keys; operation/allocation counters demonstrate non-quadratic 1k/10k collision-family construction and bounded snapshot cost; budget boundaries yield typed inconclusive/resource outcomes where the owning API provides budgets; deterministic permutations and thread partitions stay identical. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned logs containing collision cardinality, representation tier, comparisons, allocations/shared nodes, budget, expected/actual order and roots, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No randomized secondary hash, collision rejection, global mutable table, or timing-only performance claim.

## Steps to Reproduce

Insert increasing families of distinct keys whose `key_hash` is identical while counting comparisons and copied bucket entries. The Vec collision leaf performs linear lookup and whole-bucket cloning per persistent update, yielding quadratic construction.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: bind extension contracts into logical environment roots
description:
Problem: Environment::logical_root hashes each extension name and ordered payload journal, but omits ExtensionDescriptor.merge, checkpoint, and provenance. Environments with identical declarations and entries but different merge behavior, snapshot contract, or invalidation transparency therefore share a logical root even though their semantics differ. That can alias Ledger cache keys, receipts, branch behavior, and invalidation policy. Scope: define a stable explicit canonical encoding for every current MergeSemantics, CheckpointSemantics, and PayloadProvenance variant and include it in the extension delta digest before the ordered entry journal. Do not rely on Rust enum layout or debug text. Verification requirements: (1) table-driven unit coverage proves every descriptor dimension changes the logical root while an identical descriptor/journal remains stable; (2) all variants are exhaustively matched so additions fail compilation until assigned a schema value; (3) existing entry-order and insertion-order laws remain green; (4) the shared environment E2E lane must eventually emit versioned line-oriented records with descriptor case, expected root relation, actual relation, root values, and final result; (5) cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, fln-env clippy with warnings denied, and UBS pass. Preserve all existing extension payloads and registry behavior; this changes identity only where behavior already differs.

## Steps to Reproduce

Register the same extension name and payload journal in separate environments while changing only merge, checkpoint, or provenance semantics. Before the fix, every pair receives the same logical root because descriptor contracts are omitted.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: refuse extension merges with mismatched contracts
description:
Problem: ExtensionState::merge checks base/ours/theirs descriptor equality only with debug_assert_eq!. Contract mismatches therefore panic in debug builds and are unchecked in optimized builds, where data can be merged under base semantics and returned with another branch descriptor. This violates the typed-conflict promise, failure atomicity, and deterministic snapshot identity. Scope: replace assertion-only preconditions with an explicit typed conflict that reports the base, ours, and theirs descriptor identities sufficiently to diagnose which name/merge/checkpoint/provenance field diverged. Validate contracts before inspecting journals or selecting merge behavior. Preserve successful behavior for AppendOrdered, SetUnion, and ConflictsRequireReview. Verification requirements: deterministic table-driven unit tests cover mismatches in extension name, merge semantics, checkpoint semantics, and payload provenance on each branch; prove no panic and no input mutation; retain success and bilateral-change conflict coverage for all three merge modes; add or extend a shared environment E2E scenario with detailed versioned line logs for typed mismatch and clean recovery; cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, fln-env clippy with warnings denied, and UBS must pass. No release-only behavior split and no compatibility shim.

## Steps to Reproduce

Call `ExtensionState::merge` with matching journals but a differing descriptor on either branch. Before the fix, debug builds panic at `debug_assert_eq!`; optimized builds continue under the base policy and may return another descriptor.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut in the description is mandatory. Closure requires deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus typed negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. Descriptor validation must finish before journal traversal, payload cloning, merge-policy dispatch, or result publication; a mismatch therefore has work independent of journal entry and payload volume except for comparing the three bounded descriptors. Diagnostics use stable compact descriptor identities and field-level differences rather than embedding journals or payloads. Tests exercise mismatches with empty and declared large journals, prove the journals remain byte-identical, prove no partial product/root is observable, and show a subsequent compatible merge succeeds. The E2E record includes descriptor ids, differing fields, journal entry/byte counts, selected_policy set to none on refusal, expected/actual outcome, cleanup, and recovery state. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, debug/release behavior split, partial implementation, compatibility shim, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: validate extension branch ancestry before merge
description:
Problem: ExtensionState::merge documents that ours and theirs derive from base, then unconditionally skips base.entries.len() without verifying that either branch contains the exact base journal prefix. A shorter, unrelated, or same-length-different branch can silently lose entries, produce a false no-change decision, or merge histories that never shared the claimed base. Scope: after descriptor validation and before merge-policy selection, verify the full ordered base journal is a prefix of each branch. Refuse invalid ancestry with a typed deterministic conflict that identifies the extension, base/branch lengths, and common-prefix lengths without copying unbounded payloads. Preserve successful AppendOrdered, SetUnion, and review-required behavior for valid descendants. Verification requirements: deterministic unit tables cover shorter branch, first-entry divergence, later-entry divergence, same-length divergence, each branch independently, and both branches invalid; valid empty/base-only/one-sided/bilateral descendants remain green; errors do not panic or mutate inputs; existing order and root laws remain green; add or extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed versioned logs for invalid ancestry and clean recovery; cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, and UBS pass. No silent rebasing or best-effort merge.

## Steps to Reproduce

Pass a base journal and an ours or theirs journal that is shorter or differs inside the claimed base prefix. Before the fix, merge skips `base.entries.len()` without validating ancestry and can discard or misclassify entries.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: make extension journals persistently shared and linear-time to replay
description:
Problem: ExtensionState documents a structurally shared append-only journal, but stores Vec<ExtensionEntry>; push_entry clones the whole Vec on every append. Replaying N imported entries performs quadratic pointer copying and every branch duplicates its full journal, defeating the O(1)-snapshot/structural-sharing design at the extension layer. Scope: replace the contiguous Vec contract with an immutable persistent append log whose snapshot is one bounded pointer increment, append performs bounded work independent of total journal length (for example fixed-size shared chunks), and deterministic oldest-to-newest replay is linear without permanently flattening the journal. Because there are no users, revise the internal/public entries slice API directly to explicit len/is_empty/iter accessors; do not add a compatibility shim that reintroduces flattening. Preserve exact payload bytes, replay order, descriptor semantics, logical roots, branch-ancestry validation, and all three merge modes. Design must specify chunk size, iterator lifetime/order, equality, prefix comparison, clone/drop costs, and resource accounting. Verification requirements: model/property tests compare random append/fork/merge histories against Vec; pointer-identity/allocation instrumentation proves snapshots share all journal storage and each append creates only a bounded number of nodes/entry slots; boundary tests straddle empty/one/chunk-full/multi-chunk states; 10k and 100k replay tests demonstrate linear growth with recorded operations/allocations rather than a timing-only claim; all existing root/order/conflict tests remain exact. Extend the shared real-path environment E2E lane with schema-versioned logs recording entry count, chunk count, shared-node count, operations/allocations, expected versus actual order/root, timing/resource facts, and clean final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and the parent verification gates. No global mutable cache, leaked flattening buffer, or semantic reordering.

## Steps to Reproduce

Append successively larger entry sequences through `ExtensionState::push_entry` and count cloned entry slots. The current Vec-backed state copies the full prior journal on each append, producing quadratic cumulative work and no shared journal storage across branches.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: define and enforce SetUnion duplicate semantics without losing replay fidelity
description:
Load-bearing ambiguity: extension replay promises exact ordered preservation of imported payload entries, while MergeSemantics::SetUnion says entries form a byte-keyed set whose duplicates collapse. The current merge only filters entries from the theirs suffix against ours; duplicates already present in base or ours survive, and push_entry always appends. Normalizing blindly could destroy required Reference-observable replay data, while leaving behavior as-is makes the SetUnion contract false and branch-order dependent. Scope: first anchor the intended law to the pinned Reference extension behavior and the generated extension census/fixtures, then define whether set semantics apply to the stored raw journal, branch deltas, a derived semantic view, or merge output. Preserve raw import bytes/order whenever that is observable; if a semantic set view is required, represent it separately rather than laundering data loss. Specify equality/keying, first-occurrence ordering, base duplicates, duplicates within each suffix, duplicates across branches, empty payloads, hash collisions, and deterministic ours/theirs ordering. Verification requirements: table/property tests cover every duplicate location and branch permutation against an explicit model; a regression proves no required replay byte disappears; identical logical inputs yield identical roots and merge products; a deliberate one-sided-only dedup defect is detected. Extend the shared no-mock environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned records for raw journal, semantic view, expected/actual merge order, duplicate decisions, roots, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and applicable parent gates. This bead must resolve the contract from evidence before implementation; no guessed normalization and no silent data loss.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut in the description is mandatory. Closure requires the Reference-anchored raw-journal versus semantic-view law; deterministic table/property/model coverage of every duplicate location and branch permutation; the named one-sided-dedup mutant; and a shared real-path E2E success, negative, exhaustion, and recovery campaign with detailed schema-versioned logs. The implementation declares independent maximum-entry, cumulative-payload-byte, and per-entry-byte budgets at the environment boundary. All raw entries admitted within those budgets retain exact bytes and order; reaching a budget while projecting or merging yields typed Inconclusive, publishes no journal, semantic view, root, or partial merge, and a later within-budget merge must succeed. Boundary tests cover zero, exact-limit, one-over-limit, empty payload, duplicate-heavy, and unique-heavy inputs, including both branch orders. Evidence records configured and consumed entries/bytes, duplicate decisions, raw and semantic counts, expected/actual order and roots, terminal outcome, cleanup, and recovery state. The documented cost model must match the chosen exact-byte data structure and be checked on a declared large fixture; no silent truncation, guessed normalization, lossy deduplication, unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead. All stated fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates remain mandatory.
