title: W3: Crucible & Bignum — dual-engine kernel, independent checker, receipts
description:
Workstream W3 "Crucible & Bignum" (§8): the trusted kernel — the one subsystem where FrankenLean volunteers to be outnumbered — plus the owned arithmetic beneath it.

Deliverable order is constitutional: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md (the Judgment Specification, §8.1b) comes FIRST — a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to Reference source lines, against which both engines and the Tribunal generators are built. Then: K1 certified small-step evaluator (auditability-first, the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon); K2 NbE accelerator (memoized whnf keyed on term-hash x transparency, lazy delta by height) cross-checked against K1 continuously; fln-checker, a deliberately DIFFERENT independent implementation (eager normalization, own decoder over canonical wire objects, no unsafe-membrane dependency, WASM-clean, non-sharing proven structurally); fln-bignum (limb-based, ABI-layout-matching, thresholds tuned on the kernel-reduction corpus, PG-K gate vs GMP); typestate admission (UnvalidatedExpr -> ScopedExpr -> CheckedExpr, DeclarationEnvelope, four-way verdict incl. Inconclusive per FL-INV-07); typed resource budgets (§8.2c); fuel parity (§8.5); certificates + receipts + transparency log (§8.6); consensus policies where disagreement halts and never outvotes (§8.3c).

Covenant: fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC, forbid(unsafe), deps = fln-bignum + read-only term views + core/alloc only, one exported authority (check), CI-counted. Divergence law: kernel disagreement with the Reference at the pin is release-blocking; soundness beats bug-parity (D23). Exit: gate G1 — every mathlib olean re-checked with zero acceptance divergence, PG-1 met.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
EPIC CLOSEOUT FOR FL-INV-02 — the W3 evidence map must show the single authority API, opaque checked-state construction boundary, every environment publication consumer, prohibited dependency edges, compile-fail proofs, bypass mutation kills, and real no-mock admission E2E. K1/K2/checker consensus strengthens evidence but never changes the constitutional rule that fln-kernel rejection or inconclusive makes publication impossible.

Closure topology: component beads build the kernel/checker/receipt/CLI surfaces; fln-ob6 is the concrete G1 integration and performance gate; this W3 epic depends on that gate as its declared exit. It does not depend on the entire W2 epic, because the exact olean reader and environment/term-view prerequisites are already carried by fln-fur and the component graph. This preserves every W3 and G1 requirement while allowing the Independent Judge to ship before unrelated W2 frontier/runtime/plugin work.

title: Gate G3: The Mirror Holds — Init/Std from source, T2 identity, fixpoint bit-stable
description:
Convergence gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" (§22.3): the native toolchain elaborates Init and Std end-to-end FROM SOURCE — every builtin either native behind its facade row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing anywhere — with T2 identity against the Reference, the stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, and the metaprogram-corpus rig (curated mathlib-tactic slice through the facade) green at its declared L-levels. The ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes on the gate build.

This is the gate that proves the central bet (B2): the parser, elaborator, instance engine, tactic framework, and scheduler reproduce the Reference at stdlib scale, and real ecosystem metaprogram code cannot distinguish the native facade from the machinery it replaced. It is the last gate before the full Corpus becomes the target.

Evidence bundle: T2 differential results over Init/Std (acceptance, diagnostics, statement-level environment identity); the fixpoint runs (bit-identical oleans + logical roots across double elaboration and a freshly rebuilt toolchain); metaprogram-rig results with the facade-row L-level table; census-totality check output; the D8 compile-out proof on the gate binaries; PG-5 thread-matrix results at stdlib scale.

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: fln-kernel K1: soundness-boundary + inference test coverage (zht follow-up)
description:
Verification child for franken_lean-zht. Maintain the finite coverage audit for every K1 judgment, reduction, definitional-equality, resource, and admission rule that is actually implemented in fln-kernel. This bead owns evidence, not production semantics: it must never add a test-only acceptance path, weaken a rule, or stand in for an unimplemented kernel slice.

Maintain a rule matrix keyed by KERNEL_CONTRACT identifier with implementation location, public-surface fixture, positive case, discriminating rejection or near-miss, boundary/resource case where applicable, determinism status, mutation or defense-in-depth evidence, and no-mock replay coverage. Every implemented branch must have a named test through the public kernel authority. Rules not yet implemented remain explicitly assigned to their production slice, including franken_lean-5p2 for KR-205/KR-316/KR-317/KR-955, and become mandatory rows here as soon as that slice lands. The matrix must distinguish implemented-and-covered, implemented-but-uncovered, not-yet-implemented, structurally enforced, and externally replayed; an unimplemented row can never be reported as passing.

For each rule family, cover the successful judgment and the closest unsound or false-accepting alternative, not just malformed syntax. Required families include typing and inference, beta/zeta/projection/delta and recursor reductions, universe equivalence, proof irrelevance boundaries, application and binder congruence, eta laws, declaration safety and authority transitions, fuel/cancellation/FL-INV-07 outcomes, and schedule-independent verdict plus consumption. Use single-defect mutation kills where one site owns the property. Where redundant checks deliberately provide defense in depth, document and test the minimal multi-site mutant needed to remove the property rather than overclaiming a single-site kill.

The shared real-path no-mock E2E is the pinned real-olean kernel replay seeded by franken_lean-z6c and extended as K1 grows. It must record before/after accepted, rejected by stable family, inconclusive, and internal-fault counts; cross-check selected accepted modules with the pinned foreign witness; exercise a deliberately perturbed or invalid declaration that must not be accepted; and finish with a clean recovery replay proving later valid work is unaffected. Future full differential rigs in franken_lean-3j8 are additive and do not replace this closure evidence.

Emit concise human output and retained schema-versioned NDJSON. Each record includes run/scenario/step and rule ids, bead and invariant, epoch/reference/fixture identity, canonical module/declaration/input hashes, expected and actual verdict/error/consumption, budget/cancellation facts, engine/build and thread/seed facts, mutant or defense-in-depth id when applicable, witness result, exit status, first divergence, artifact references, authority/cacheability, cleanup, and asserted final state. Bound diagnostic volume and preserve stderr artifacts.

Do not close until a mechanical comparison of the implemented K1 rule inventory against this matrix reports zero implemented-but-uncovered rows, every production follow-up has contributed its boundary tests, all named mutations are killed or honestly classified as structural/defense-in-depth checks, the no-mock negative/recovery replay is green, and fln-kernel plus applicable Tribunal quality gates pass.
acceptance_criteria: The implemented K1 inventory has zero uncovered rows and every row is backed by named public-authority unit/boundary evidence, discriminating negative coverage, and mutation or structural enforcement evidence. Production slices such as franken_lean-5p2 contribute their rule rows when they land. Deterministic verdict/consumption and FL-INV-07 resource/cancellation behavior are covered. The pinned real-olean no-mock replay includes foreign-witness sampling, an invalid or perturbed negative case, clean recovery, retained human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs, and exact before/after counts. No test-only authority path, unimplemented-as-passing row, silent coverage deferral, or undocumented surviving critical mutant satisfies closure.

title: fln-hash canon: deep Expr/Level encoding and destruction must be stack-safe
description:
The iterative decoder fix in franken_lean-fnj removes recursive descent, but a successfully decoded or programmatically built very deep valid Expr or Level still reaches two recursive lifecycle paths in crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs: Canonical::write_body re-encoding/hash materialization and automatic destruction of nested Arc-backed nodes. Either can overflow a normal worker stack and abort the process. Make both paths depth-independent in safe Rust while preserving exact canonical bytes/hashes, structural sharing and reference counts, writer/error behavior, and ordinary shallow-path performance. Do not impose a semantic nesting cap, increase thread stacks, leak trees, or hide fatal probes.

## Steps to Reproduce

After franken_lean-fnj, construct or decode valid right/left-heavy Expr App/binder and Level Succ/Max/IMax chains at increasing depths in sacrificial child processes or threads with explicit 1 MiB and 2 MiB stacks. First canonical-encode/hash the value, then retain cloned/shared sub-DAG roots and release them in varied orders. The current write_body or automatic Drop should overflow at a platform/build-dependent threshold; record the smallest observed encoder and destructor thresholds and exact artifact/build/stack facts. Fatal probes never execute in the main test-runner process.

## Acceptance Criteria

Canonical encoding and destruction perform no input-depth recursion. Encoding is iterative, forward deterministic, byte-for-byte and hash-identical to the prior implementation for all valid shallow/corpus fixtures, and correctly handles writer failure/partial output according to the existing API. Destruction is iterative or otherwise proven stack-bounded with exact Arc ownership for unique nodes, multiply shared DAG nodes, cloned roots, partial construction/decode state, cancellation, error unwinding, and encoder failure cleanup. No unsafe code, intentional leak, global deferred garbage, refcount corruption, double drop, semantic/wire/hash change, or unbounded runtime-worker monopolization is allowed.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover every Expr and Level constructor; million-node left/right/alternating chains under a 1 MiB stack; balanced trees; maximally shared DAGs; random clone/drop orders; encoder writer failures at every boundary; partial construction; cancellation/error cleanup; allocation pressure; exact live-object/refcount accounting; exhaustive shallow old-versus-new byte/hash equivalence; and corpus canonical vectors. A real no-mock child-process E2E decodes, canonical-encodes, hashes, clones, and drops a valid at-least-100k-deep artifact with zero signal/hang/leak/survivor, then performs a clean shallow recovery. Negative/recovery scenarios cover truncated input, forced writer failure, and cancellation mid-lifecycle. Emit bounded human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs with seed/depth/shape/share factor, stack/build/toolchain/host facts, input/output/hash, object/refcount counts, timings/resources, expected versus actual, exit/signal, stdout/stderr artifacts, first divergence, cleanup and final state. Archive reproducible bundles and available leak/sanitizer evidence; a fatal-probe-only, mock-only, or leaked-tree result cannot close the bead.
design: Use explicit work stacks for both canonical emission and destruction. The encoder stack carries visit/emit actions that reproduce the existing preorder tag/scalar/child byte grammar exactly, including binder metadata ordering and writer-error precedence, without recursively calling child write_body. Stack-safe Drop takes ownership of the root Arc, repeatedly unwraps uniquely owned nodes, pushes owned children, and otherwise releases only the shared Arc reference; prove that shared descendants are neither traversed prematurely nor leaked and that a last-reference cascade remains iterative. Apply the same law to Expr and Level, keep all logic safe Rust, and bound cleanup batches or yield through the owning higher-level region where necessary without making object liveness nondeterministic. Mutation tests reintroduce recursive encode/drop and must be killed by bounded-stack subprocess scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: All description and design requirements are mandatory. In 100 consecutive bounded-stack child runs per Expr and Level shape, valid deep decode, encode/hash, clone/share, and destruction complete with exact bytes/hashes and zero SIGABRT, panic, hang, leak, refcount error, or survivor; writer/cancellation failures produce the specified outcome and clean state; a shallow recovery follows every failure. Unit/property/mutation/corpus differentials and real E2E bundles validate independently with detailed logs.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bug): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise canonical-byte equivalence, writer failures, unique/shared/cloned/partial/cancelled/resource-constrained values, refcount accounting, and shallow/deep lifecycle behavior. The real no-mock bounded-stack child-process scenario plus negative/recovery coverage is required. Human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts record exact redacted command/cwd, epoch/profile/toolchain/host/stack, seed/input/output hashes/depth/shape/share factor, expected versus actual, monotonic timing, resources/budgets, exit/signal, stdout/stderr references, live-object/refcount facts, first divergence, cleanup and final state. Archive fatal-reproduction metadata without running fatal probes in the main harness; close evidence names all tests, artifacts, leak checks, and reproducible commands. A skipped, leaked, or mock-only case cannot satisfy the lifecycle-safety claim.

Please fold write_body into this bead's iterative-destruction/traversal scope (or track separately) so deep-tree stack-safety is complete across decode (fnj, done), encode (write_body), and drop.

Therefore 6gy is now independently actionable, and fnj depends on it for its mandatory partial-error-cleanup proof. This removes a latent plan-space deadlock without narrowing either bead.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Adjacent lifecycle dependency filed as franken_lean-p8a.1 after reviewing the live implementation. Name is itself an unbounded Arc-backed parent chain; automatic Drop, derived structural Eq/Hash/Debug, Ord, quick_cmp_aux, and display recurse through parent depth. Expr and Level leaf payloads include Name, so a deeply nested Name can still make an otherwise shallow value violate this bead lifecycle claim. Canonical Name writing is already being converted to an iterative walk in the live canon.rs diff, which is good, but core Name lifecycle remains. Added p8a.1 as a blocker so 6gy cannot close over an unhandled embedded payload. This is review-only; no active owner file was edited and no fatal probe was run.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Critical review finding in the live uncommitted Drop implementation: both Expr and Level currently use Arc::try_unwrap inside their iterative drains. That does not preserve stack safety when final cloned roots are released concurrently. Two owners may both fail try_unwrap while the strong count exceeds one, then both drop the returned Arc; the last ordinary Arc drop can recursively destroy the deep chain. The Rust standard-library Arc::into_inner contract explicitly guarantees that if every clone calls it, exactly one call receives the inner value, and its linked-list example says the same manual Drop can still overflow if try_unwrap(...).ok() is used instead. The implementation should use Arc::into_inner for the ownership-election step and include a deterministic simultaneous cloned-root drop test, not only sequential retained_root drops. Static review only; I did not edit the active files or run a fatal probe.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Safe independent checkpoint verification on the live uncommitted Rust diff, without opening or executing the process-oriented E2E: rch exec -- cargo test -p fln-core exited 0 with 43 passed, including the new sequential Arc-sharing Drop tests. The targeted constructor/ordering test rch exec -- cargo test -p fln-hash canon::tests::iterative_encoders_cover_every_level_and_expr_constructor -- --exact exited 0 with 1 passed. Static review confirms iterative encoder task order matches the prior wire grammar. These checks do not close the known concurrent try_unwrap defect, and I deliberately did not run the deep lifecycle subprocess test per the user safety constraint.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Integrated RubyForest's concurrent-final-owner review into the live 6gy implementation: Expr/Level drains now use Arc::into_inner instead of try_unwrap, and the sacrificial-process path simultaneously releases two final cloned roots from separate 1 MiB threads. This targets the standard-library ownership-election guarantee that exactly one concurrent caller receives the inner node. Sequential refcount unit tests remain additive; 6gy is still blocked on p8a.1 and the full acceptance matrix.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Follow-up review after f18b2f4: the current working diff replaces try_unwrap with Arc::into_inner in Name, Expr, and Level and adds concurrent cloned-root drops, directly addressing the standard-library ownership-election flaw reported earlier. Safe local cargo test -p fln-core on this newer diff exited 0 with 43 passed. UBS over the four Rust files exited 1 only because it classifies the four deliberate test-only recursive mutation panic sites as critical; formatting, clippy, cargo check, and test build were clean. I did not execute the lifecycle subprocess. This remains a live uncommitted follow-up and still depends on p8a.1 for deep Name structural operations.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Independent fresh-eyes review PASSED (Claude Fable session) — the whole term-plane stack-safety story is correct end to end:
- ENCODERS (canon.rs, 265f260): Expr::write_body and Level::write_body are iterative LIFO work-stacks; I traced the push order for every constructor (App/Lam/ForallE/LetE/MData/Proj; Succ/Max/IMax) and the emission is byte-identical to the recursive form (children pushed in reverse so a subtree fully emits before its next sibling; post-order scalar fields BinderInfo/NonDep emitted at the correct point). Name/KVMap encoding is iterative too, so no path recurses on depth.
- DROP (expr.rs/level.rs/name.rs, f18b2f4): all three use node: Option<Arc<...>> + take_node_for_drop()=take() as a sentinel, Arc::into_inner to drain ONLY unique nodes (shared nodes merely decremented — exact Arc sharing preserved), and moved-out children drop as no-ops. Memory-safe (no double-free, no leak), stack-safe (heap worklist), and each type drains its own chain (a Param's Name drops via Name::drop). node_arc()'s expect is unreachable on a live Expr (None is transient to Drop only).
- DECODERS (mine, fnj): iterative read_expr_iter/read_level_iter, matching grammar.
Coverage is thorough: iterative_drop_preserves_shared_{expr,level}_arcs, recursive_{expr,level}_encoder_mutant on a 1 MiB stack, iterative_encoders_cover_every_constructor, deep-input decode-as-typed-error. No defect or gap found — this bead's implementation is sound.

title: Gate G4: The Corpus Whole — full mathlib, T2=100%, PG-2, mixed builds
description:
Convergence gate G4 "The Corpus Whole" (§22.3): full mathlib elaboration — T2 = 100%, T3 measured with a published ratchet, PG-2(G4) met (wall <= 0.5x Reference, same machine/threads), olean outputs consumed by the Reference toolchain in the mixed-build matrix. This is the gate that makes "an implementation of Lean" a fact rather than a claim: the Corpus is the de-facto behavioral specification (§1.9), and elaborating all of it with statement-identical environments is the definition of having implemented the language.

Also folded in per §22.2/§12.6: grind rows at L3 on the grind corpus; simp fidelity at scale; the Ledger incremental law live under the full Corpus (the PG-3 measurement baseline); native_decide Tier 1 as the sound-mode default; PG-M (metaprogram corpus <= 1.25x Reference).

Evidence bundle: the full T2/T3 differential ledger with every T3 residual classified (fidelity defect closed, or documented free choice with pinned CGSE policy); PG-2/PG-M numbers with claim states; the mixed-producer matrix transcripts (our oleans under the Reference, theirs under ours, interleaved builds); the R2 divergence ledger empty or published as errata with quantified blast radius (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G4 is the full-Corpus promotion over already-proven G3 infrastructure. Its exact production inputs are the engine-backed tactic facade integration; Ledger records/CAS and FL-INV-05 incremental law; real Lake build surface; faithful olean writer/mixed-producer path; and native_decide Tier 1, plus the inherited G3 and performance harness. It deliberately does not wait for W7 e-graph/portfolio frontier work or W8 distributed/cache-federation/epoch-bridge GA work, which are additive full-workstream obligations attached to G6. Full mathlib T2 = 100%, the T3 ratchet, simp/grind rows, PG-2/PG-M, mixed builds, negative/recovery, and detailed evidence remain mandatory here.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: C3 Tribunal fixtures: track manifest-bound .olean bytes for clean clones

title: W1: Convergence governance — R15 WIP caps and earliest-failing-gate routing
description:
Implement the program-control mitigation required by risk R15 so a large multi-agent effort converges on R4 instead of maximizing simultaneous subsystem starts. Define a versioned, reviewable policy over the live br dependency graph and evidence registry: distinguish workstreams, gate/contract prerequisites, active incidents/security fixes, verification lanes, and additive leapfrog work; cap active implementation workstreams; route capacity first to the earliest failing convergence gate and its transitive blockers; reserve bounded capacity for independent critical bugs and verification; and freeze additive work when convergence evidence regresses. Freeze means retain every bead and feature in the backlog with reasons and resume conditions, never delete or silently descope it.

Expose a robot-safe command/report suitable for CI and agents. It reads stable br JSON plus evidence/gate state, never launches a TUI, never mutates bead status implicitly, and emits both a concise recommendation and schema-versioned NDJSON containing graph/evidence hashes, active-workstream classification, WIP budget, critical path, earliest failing gate, selected/held work, reason codes, exception owner/expiry, starvation age, and exact next unblocking actions. The policy must handle stale/missing/cyclic/inconsistent graph or evidence inputs as typed inconclusive/internal outcomes, not as permission to start more work.

## Acceptance Criteria

The decision log freezes the initial numeric WIP budget, reserved verification/incident capacity, workstream classification law, priority order, exception authority, expiry/review cadence, fairness/starvation rule, and resume criteria. Configuration changes are reviewed, versioned, and evidenced; agents cannot bypass the cap by splitting or relabeling equivalent work. CI rejects unauthorized over-cap in-progress states and expired exceptions while providing actionable reasons; emergency/security work can proceed under a logged bounded exception without erasing the main critical path. The policy treats bv metrics as advisory evidence and validates semantic dependencies, so keyword similarity or a transient PageRank score cannot rewrite the plan automatically.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover empty/single/many-track graphs, ties, multiple failing gates, incidents, verification reservation, starvation, blocked and deferred beads, closed/reopened work, stale evidence, missing owners, cycles, duplicate labels, malicious metadata, expired exceptions, and stable output under reordered JSONL. A real no-mock E2E uses a temporary br project plus real br and bv robot output to exercise claim, over-cap rejection, gate regression freeze, incident exception, expiry, close/unblock, and clean recovery without mutating the production project. Detailed bounded human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs record tool/schema versions, exact redacted commands/cwd, graph/evidence/config hashes, selected/held ids and reasons, monotonic timings/resources, exit status, stderr/stdout artifacts, expected versus actual, and final bead states. A mock-only planner, hidden auto-mutation, feature deletion, or unlogged exception cannot close this bead.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this governance bead): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise policy classification, ordering, WIP arithmetic, failure states, exceptions, fairness, determinism, and non-mutation. Real no-mock temporary-project E2E coverage must use br changes and bv robot diagnostics for normal, over-cap, regression, incident, expiry, and recovery scenarios without touching production statuses. Emit concise human and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step, policy/schema/tool versions, exact redacted argv/cwd, graph/evidence/config hashes, active/selected/held beads and reason codes, budgets/exceptions/starvation, expected versus actual, monotonic timing/resources, exit status, stderr/stdout artifacts, and final states. Archive configs, fixtures, logs, and reproduction commands; a skip, mock-only result, hidden mutation, or lost feature cannot satisfy the claim.

title: W12: Front doors — lean/leanc/lake personalities + the fln multiplexer verbs
description:
Implement the toolchain front doors (§17.1, fln-cli): the binaries lean, leanc (the C-driver personality: flag-compatible, driving the D2 cc when present, explaining its absence via capability errors when not), lake, and the fln multiplexer exposing the new verbs — check-olean, audit (--tcb), replay, doctor (--sql), cache, olean, goals, serve-mcp, why-trusts, diff, build explain, server replay, verify-capsule, identity.

Contract: flags, exit codes, env vars (LEAN_PATH, LEAN_SYSROOT), and --json shapes pinned to the Reference at the pin (CLI transcript corpus, C-family fixtures); --trust levels ACCEPTED with upstream semantics but journaled into receipts and surfaced by fln audit rather than silently honored (BN-09); new flags live under --fln-* only; the version line stays parse-compatible (Reference version/commit fields preserved) with a separate machine-readable implementation field — compatibility never becomes deception, and an exact-reference transcript mode exists for conformance harnesses only. Robot-mode discipline per AGENTS.md: stable versioned schemas, deterministic where possible, explicit exit codes, line-oriented output, no human decoration mixed into machine streams.

Acceptance: the CLI transcript corpus diffs clean against the Reference for the shared surface; each fln verb has a --json schema contract test against a frozen fixture; elan invokes the binaries transparently via the release layout (verified in W12). Tests: golden transcripts per verb (human and JSON modes) with detailed logging of any drift; exit-code matrix fixtures; --trust journaling fixtures; capability-error fixtures for missing cc/git naming the alternative.

title: W12: Epoch migration autopilot integration — audited repair, explicit apply, rollback
description:
Integrate the §13.6 migration autopilot at the stage where all of its real prerequisites exist: the W12 epoch ratchet and machine-diffed censuses, W8 epoch bridges/cache identities, W9 semantic diff and blame slices, and W11 Bloodhound's verified proof-repair engine. Audit an unchanged downstream project against the new epoch; locate the earliest failed constraints and affected surfaces; propose minimal source/config/import/termination/instance/simp repairs; run lockstep old/new builds; validate every candidate through Athanor, Crucible, and the configured consensus policy; and emit an explicit apply transaction with a sealed rollback bundle. It never edits a project without an explicit apply.

Issue-tracker integration must respect D2: shipped FrankenLean tooling never spawns br or any third external tool. The ratchet emits a deterministic, schema-versioned migration-issue manifest with one row per moved symbol/divergence and exact br import/apply instructions for the repository-maintenance harness or human operator; applying that manifest is outside release-binary execution and is separately receipted.

Acceptance: a synthetic epoch delta and at least one real historical Lean migration run end to end; unchanged projects that need no repair remain byte/semantic clean; repaired projects build under the new epoch with every patch kernel-checked; rejected or partially applied migrations restore exactly from the rollback root; unclassified divergences block publication; migration issue manifests are deterministic and complete.

Verification: unit tests for census-to-impact mapping and transaction preconditions; property tests for rollback identity/idempotent re-apply; repair-ranking and explicit-consent negatives; lockstep divergence fixtures; and shared no-mock E2E scripts covering audit -> propose -> verify -> preview -> explicit apply -> rollback, with detailed old/new roots, patch hashes, receipts, issue-manifest rows, commands, final states, and first-divergence logs.

title: W6: Builtin census + Mirror facade — census-total, evidence-leveled, no hidden oracle
description:
Implement the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (§4.3, Appendix C): mechanical extraction over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources emitting the census (symbol, Lean signature, universe/binder telescope, module, kind, attributes, extern symbol, effect class, PARTITION CLASS: toolchain-api / library-code / user-facing-data) and, from the toolchain-api subset, generated facade stubs (upstream signature, telescope, effect class) whose bodies call the native Quill/Athanor/Crucible/Golem/Anvil/Marrow services.

The three laws: (1) census TOTALITY — no unclassified symbol; a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the surface L-level, never gets guessed. (2) Fidelity by evidence (FL-INV-03) — every toolchain-api row proven against the oracle: fixture differentials, golden decision traces, the metaprogram-corpus rig; a row whose API intrinsically exposes internal object identity or allocation order is classified at its honest L-level with a Behavior Note, never hand-waved. (3) No hidden oracle (D8) — the fallback for a facade defect is fixing the native implementation against fixtures; there is no run-the-upstream-definition switch in any release binary. Every row states: symbol, upstream semantics anchor (source location at the pin), equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), evidence, determinism class, L-level.

Acceptance: G3 requirement — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with every builtin either native-behind-its-row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing; census diff tooling ready for epoch advances; the G0-8 resistance list resolved row by row. title: W2: Marrow & Grimoire — ABI twin, object model, environment, olean codec
description:
Workstream W2 "Marrow & Grimoire" (§6, §7): the runtime/ABI twin and the environment/module codec — the load-bearing act of drop-in compatibility and the spine of gate G1.

Marrow: the Reference object model implemented exactly in Rust under the lean_* exported names (header packing, tag bytes, scalar-field packing, odd-tagged-pointer small Nats, tri-state RC discipline), with layout constants compiled from the GENERATED contract tables of §6.5 — never transcribed. Two heaps, one membrane (§6.1b): CompatHeap (exact ABI layouts, membrane-only allocation, unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles for toolchain-internal structures); values cross only through generated converters; no raw Compat pointer ever enters the kernel (D22). Owned size-classed allocator with the heartbeat hook (fuel parity lives here). Compacted regions: construction, mmap-load, relocation — deliberately the SAME code path the olean codec uses.

Grimoire: the environment as persistent HAMT-style maps with O(1) snapshots and logical roots (§7.1); total contractual interning with alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b); olean codec read (three validation levels) and faithful byte-identical write (FL-INV-04); .ilean codec; olean-next frontier lane; import-at-daemon-scale shared heap (§7.5). Effects ride asupersync behind Reference IO/Task semantics (§6.7). fln-libm owned deterministic transcendentals (§6.8). Exit: G1 spine ready — real mathlib oleans resurrect, re-emit byte-identically, and load into the Reference.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W6: Deterministic dataflow scheduler — speculative parallelism, canonical merge, perturbation-validated effects
description:
Implement the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4, §10.6): declarations as dataflow nodes — parse yields a conservative dependency pre-scan; elaboration proceeds speculatively in parallel asupersync regions against O(1) environment snapshots; a declaration that reads a not-yet-committed name suspends on its true dependency or aborts-and-retries; commits merge in CANONICAL ORDER (source order per module; module order per Ledger topology) so the resulting environment, diagnostics, and InfoTrees are those of sequential elaboration — bit-for-bit, at any thread count (FL-INV-01 by construction, the invariant upstream Elab.async demonstrably lacks).

Mechanics: optimistic concurrency — transactions get source-order IDs; commit-time read-set validation against the latest committed predecessor either commits in canonical order or discards/rebases; no last-writer-wins anywhere; conflicting extension mutations serialize. Side-effecting commands are ordering barriers detected conservatively: unknown effects serialize; known-commutative registered effects parallelize. Command effect summaries (ReadsDecl(name, Type|Value|Attributes), ReadsInstances(head), ReadsSimpSet, ReadsGrammar, ReadsOption, WritesDecl, WritesInstance, WritesGrammar, WritesEnvExtension, UsesCapability, Opaque(reason)) are captured dynamically and validated BY PERTURBATION: mutate an allegedly-unread input, require the product unchanged; a failing perturbation demotes the producer to Opaque and opens a Parity-Ledger bead. Cross-module speculation rides Ledger hashes-in-flight; mis-speculation costs a retry, never a wrong answer.

Acceptance: PG-5 thread-matrix {1,8,32} bit-identical environments/diagnostics/artifacts per commit on the corpus; speculation win measured against the sequential baseline; perturbation validator running in CI. Tests: DPOR-bounded schedule exploration of the merge protocol under the asupersync lab; abort/retry storm fixtures; seeded effect-summary lies must be demoted.

title: fln-hash canon: recursive Expr/Level decoders stack-overflow (SIGABRT) on deep untrusted input
description:
Canonical Expr and Level decoding currently uses recursive descent once per attacker-controlled nesting tag. A compact malicious artifact or wire payload can exhaust a normal Rust worker stack and trigger an uncatchable SIGABRT, violating the decoder totality contract, D8, and FL-INV-07. Replace both recursive readers with explicit heap-backed state machines while preserving the exact canonical wire format, result values, typed errors, and compatibility for legitimate deep mathlib terms. A hard nesting cap is not an acceptable substitute because it can reject valid artifacts; caller resource budgets may stop work only through the typed inconclusive/resource path.

## Steps to Reproduce

In a sacrificial child process or spawned thread with an explicit 8 MiB and 2 MiB stack, feed a valid schema header followed by a long Expr App-tag chain, and separately Level Succ/Max/IMax chains, without enough leaves. Increase depth until the current implementation prints stack overflow and exits by SIGABRT. The established reproduction overflows around 20k to 50k Expr frames in release on 8 MiB and around 1k to 2k in debug; exact thresholds are evidence, not acceptance constants. Fatal probes must never execute in the main test-runner process.

## Acceptance Criteria

Expr and Level decoding use no input-depth recursion in tag parsing, child scheduling, assembly, or error cleanup. The implementation accepts every previously valid byte sequence subject only to existing format and explicit resource rules; emits byte-for-byte identical canonical re-encodings and hashes; reports malformed, truncated, over-budget, cancelled, and allocation-failure cases through stable typed outcomes; performs linear work in consumed input plus produced nodes; and cannot panic or abort on arbitrary bytes. State-machine invariants, frame variants, maximum pending work, and partial-value cleanup are documented.

Named unit/property/mutation tests cover every Expr and Level constructor; truncation at every field boundary; invalid tags/indices/lengths; highly unbalanced and alternating shapes; at least 100k attacker-controlled nesting tags under a 2 MiB stack; random malformed bytes; budget and cancellation boundaries; and old-versus-new semantic/wire/hash equivalence on exhaustive shallow plus corpus fixtures. A real no-mock CLI/artifact E2E runs bounded-stack child probes for deep malformed inputs, asserts typed exit with no signal/hang/survivor, then performs a clean recovery decode. Emit concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with seed/depth/shape/input hash, stack/build/toolchain/host, budgets/timings/resources, expected versus actual, exit/signal, stderr/stdout artifacts, first divergence, cleanup and final state. The separate franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy bead owns destruction of successfully built very deep DAGs and blocks the parent fln-hash closeout.
design: Use iterative explicit work frames for Expr and Level. Each frame records the constructor tag, already decoded scalar fields, the next child slot, and child results; a separate value stack assembles nodes only after all children succeed. Reads remain canonical and forward-only, lengths/indices remain checked before allocation, and partial failures unwind heap containers rather than the call stack. Preserve error precedence and offsets exactly where they are compatibility-visible. Prove depth-independent call-stack usage with a bounded-stack child test and mutation-kill a deliberately recursive variant. Do not use unsafe code, increase thread stacks, catch aborts in-process, leak partial trees, or add a semantic depth cap.
acceptance_criteria: All requirements in the description and design are mandatory. The old fatal reproducer exits through a typed error in 100 consecutive bounded-stack child runs for both Expr and Level families with zero SIGABRT, panic, hang, or survivor; clean inputs immediately afterward decode and hash identically. The complete unit/property/mutation/corpus differential suite and the real child-process E2E pass with independently valid detailed logs. franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy remains an explicit blocker for full fln-hash lifecycle closure.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bug): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise all constructors and every malformed, resource, cancellation, and failure-atomicity boundary. The real no-mock bounded-stack process scenario plus failure/recovery is required. Human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts record exact redacted command/cwd, epoch/profile/toolchain/host/stack, seed/input hash/depth/shape, expected versus actual, monotonic timing, resource budgets, exit/signal, stdout/stderr references, first divergence, cleanup and final state. Archive fatal-reproduction metadata without running fatal probes in the main harness; close evidence names all tests, artifacts, and reproducible commands. A skipped or mock-only case cannot satisfy the safety claim.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (Claude Fable review session, 2026-07-22): crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs Expr::read_body and Level::read_body are recursive-descent decoders that recurse once per nesting level of ATTACKER-CONTROLLED bytes (Expr via App/Lam/ForallE/LetE/MData/Proj/Sort/Const; Level via Succ/Max/IMax). There is no depth bound. A crafted artifact that is just a schema header followed by a chain of EXPR_APP tag bytes (0x05) drives unbounded call-stack recursion.

REPRODUCTION (verified): decode a chain of ~2,000,000 EXPR_APP tags on a std thread with an 8 MiB stack (Linux default; what std/asupersync worker threads get) -> 'thread has overflowed its stack; fatal runtime error: stack overflow, aborting' (SIGABRT). Threshold on 8 MiB stack: release build overflows between depth 20k-50k (~200 B/frame); debug build overflows between 1k-2k (~4 KB/frame); a 2 MiB spawned test thread overflows around a few hundred. So a <=2 MB malicious .olean/receipt/olean-next artifact or wire message aborts the whole process/daemon.

This is masked on the dev box ONLY because ulimit -s is unlimited there (recursion just gets slow, no crash). It is fully reachable on any normal deployment.

SEVERITY: violates the codec's own stated contract (canon.rs:11 'Decoding is total over arbitrary bytes: every failure is a typed CanonError, never a panic') and doctrine D8 / FL-INV-07 (malformed artifacts/protocol messages must not panic; a stack-overflow abort is strictly WORSE than a panic — uncatchable, kills the process, no typed Inconclusive). The canon codec is exactly the untrusted-input surface (decl content digests, receipts, olean-next, Diagnostic-on-the-wire).

WHY A DEPTH CAP IS NOT THE FIX: any hard recursion cap safe on the smallest realistic decode stack (~hundreds on a 2 MB test thread) is BELOW what legitimate mathlib oleans can reach (long app spines, nested binders in the low thousands), so a cap would false-reject valid artifacts — breaking the .olean read surface, which §4.1 forbids breaking casually. The correct fix is an ITERATIVE (explicit heap work-stack) decoder: decode depth bounded by heap (input size), not call stack — no overflow, no false rejects.

COVERAGE GAP that let this exist: the existing 'malformed_inputs_are_typed_errors_never_panics' test only exercises small malformed inputs; it never tests deep nesting.

RELATED latent issue (separate, note only): dropping a very deep valid Expr/Level tree (nested Arc<ExprNode>) also recurses in Drop and could overflow — but that requires already holding a deep tree in memory. Out of scope for this decode-path fix; worth a follow-up if deep valid oleans exist.

FIXING NOW in this session: converting both decoders to iterative explicit-stack machines that preserve the exact wire format, plus a bounded-stack regression test. canon.rs is committed-clean and idle (mtime 4h).

title: G0-9: Instrumented-oracle spike — golden decision traces schema, volume, replay rig
description:
G0-9 Instrumented-oracle spike (§22.1-9, feeds §18.3): patch the Reference IN THE TRIBUNAL SANDBOX (build-time-only, test-only, never shipped, never linked — D8) to dump decision traces at the points our native machinery must match — unifier approximation choices, postponements, unfoldings and their order, instance-search candidate orders and outcomes, macro expansions, simp rewrite firings, heartbeat consumption — on a pilot corpus, and prove the trace schema, volume, and replay-rig design.

Why this exists: under the Oracle-Only Law there is NO hosted upstream engine to fall back to, so the unifier/instance/simp fidelity burden (Critical risk R2) is carried entirely by evidence. Golden decision traces at Corpus scale are the strongest available mitigation: Athanor, Synod, and Anvil are implemented AGAINST the traces, not against vibes, and trace-replay rigs run continuously thereafter.

Acceptance: (a) the patched-oracle build recipe is scripted, hashed, and reproducible in the sandbox; (b) trace schema versioned and validated (typed events, source anchors, stable ids); (c) volume measured on the pilot corpus with a stated projection to Corpus scale (storage in the Tribunal frankensqlite store); (d) a replay rig prototype diffs a hand-written toy unifier against traces to prove the comparison machinery. Traces are fixtures, never components; the generator lives behind the D8 wall. Tests: the pilot traces + replay rig land in fln-conformance as the seed of the continuous golden-trace lanes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.

title: W3: Crucible K2 — NbE accelerator, memoized whnf, cross-checked against K1
description:
Implement Crucible engine K2, the NbE accelerator (§8.3): normalization-by-evaluation with environment-aware closures, memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with definitional-height comparison — the engine that makes decide, big Fin computations, and mathlib defeq-heavy corners fast (the PG-1/PG-2 arithmetic depends on it; the design was priced by spike G0-7).

Laws: K2 implements the SAME judgments as K1 (one KERNEL_CONTRACT); K2 verdicts are spot-audited against K1 continuously (sampled per run, exhaustively in Tribunal soaks) and any disagreement fails loudly and ships as a fixture; every fast-lane shortcut carries exactly one registered justification — a local proof argument in the kernel design note, a certificate checked by a simpler routine, or differential evidence (which licenses an optimization and never impersonates a proof). The size covenant counts K2 lines too. Cache keying uses the alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b) so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work invisibly to metaprograms.

Acceptance: the G0-7 twenty-problem tail suite meets the envelope the PG-2 model requires; K1/K2 continuous cross-check wired and green on the full fixture corpus; PG-1 trajectory measured (full mathlib olean recheck time on the 32-core profile). Tests: differential K1-vs-K2 on generated term storms; memoization-correctness property tests (cached verdict == fresh verdict under transparency changes); fuel-parity fixtures shared with G0-6 stay green on both engines.

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. design: FL-INV-02 ACCELERATOR BOUNDARY — K2 may accelerate the same judgment but cannot expose a second admission API or mint an accepted state through unchecked memoized data. Cache entries and NbE shortcuts remain internal evidence; the common fln-kernel authority gate validates status, epoch, environment root, declaration hash, resource outcome, and required K1 cross-check policy before any opaque checked result exists. Unknown/stale cache versions recompute and disagreement halts.

title: W3: fln-checker — structurally independent checker, WASM-clean, consensus seat
description:
Implement fln-checker, the independent kernel checker (§8.3b): a deliberately DIFFERENT second implementation, not a feature flag over fln-kernel — separate normalization algorithm (eager where K1/K2 are lazy), simpler persistent maps and substitutions, its OWN decoder over Grimoire canonical wire objects (never the primary heap nodes), no dependency on the unsafe membrane, an independently derived error-class mapping, a separate build artifact, and a WASM build for a foreign execution substrate (the WASM Judge second engine).

Non-sharing is proven STRUCTURALLY: CI verifies fln-checker shares data schemas but no semantic implementation modules with fln-kernel (dependency-graph rules). The checker joins the consensus policies of §8.3c: standard (deterministic content-seeded sample + all high-risk forms — never runtime randomness), release (full closure), paranoid (everywhere, optionally distinct hosts). The honest economics: roughly 2x kernel CPU where it runs, single-digit percent end-to-end (PG-1b measures it). Common-mode risk R6 (shared Judgment Specification misreading) is why FOREIGN witnesses (lean4checker, lean4lean via export) exist in the council; this bead includes wiring the external-witness sampling seam.

Acceptance: full agreement with K1/K2 on the corpus; a seeded spec-divergence mutant (deliberately misimplement one rule in a test build) is caught by the differential lane; WASM build passes the same vectors bit-for-bit. Tests: non-sharing CI check; consensus-policy integration tests where a planted disagreement HALTS publication with both traces attached (never outvoted).

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 CONSENSUS BOUNDARY — fln-checker is an independent veto/evidence seat, never an alternative admission authority. Its verdict and WASM/foreign witness results are serializable evidence but cannot construct the fln-kernel opaque checked state. Tests attempt receipt replay, forged checker success, stale-root success, unknown policy/version, and all-negative/one-positive vote combinations; none may publish when fln-kernel rejects or is inconclusive.

title: W5: Golem runtime integration — generated intrinsics, effects, plugins, G2 parity
description:
Integrate the production Golem execution stack after its independently testable components exist: validated FIR-to-FLBC output, the FLBC interpreter host-call ABI, the census-generated intrinsic table, Marrow allocation/RC, asupersync IO/Task effects, initializer ordering and capability receipts, and the inbound Reference-plugin loader. This bead owns the no-mock end-to-end proof of the §11 claim; component beads may prove their own stable interfaces without pretending that future components already exist.

Required behavior: compile and execute real user-class Lean code (mathlib-style tactics, project macros, lakefile-shaped configuration, #eval and IO/Task bodies) on ABI-valued registers; cross interpreted code <-> native intrinsics <-> Reference-built plugin calls with zero representation conversion; preserve initialization/extension ordering; count heartbeats through the same allocator hook; enforce typed budgets and cancellation; retain RC balance and ownership-shadow evidence; record capability and plugin provenance. No test stub, prototype intrinsic, or oracle artifact may satisfy production acceptance.

Acceptance: the G0-3 corpus is promoted to the complete production stack; #eval/IO/Task and plugin results match the Reference at each registered comparison class; PG-7 interpreter measurements are emitted against the W1 baseline; the C4 inbound matrix is green at honest per-platform L-levels; initialization-DAG and capability receipts are complete; two runs at each certified thread count produce identical logical results and evidence identities where required.

Verification: unit contract tests at every component seam plus shared no-mock E2E scripts for pure computation, IO/Task ordering, capability denial, cancellation, panic/partial guards, plugin load/run/crash, and RC/heartbeat accounting. Every scenario emits the mandatory human and NDJSON evidence with the first cross-boundary divergence and final ownership/obligation state.

title: W5: Initialization + capability-scoped effects — ordering, --pure-elab, receipts
description:
Implement initialization + capability-scoped effects (§11.5): initialize / builtin_initialize ordering, import-time extension replay interleaving, and real-world IO effects exactly as upstream — under asupersync capabilities, so a --pure-elab audit mode can DENY ambient filesystem/network/clock to metaprograms and report exactly which declarations demanded them.

The posture: capability-scoped metaprogramming is a sound-mode observability win and a frontier-mode sandbox, NEVER a semantic change to compliant code (faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process — drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance). Initialization DAG receipts (from the plugin door, §6.6) unify with this: cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable. This bead also covers the interaction with Golem-hosted lakefile execution (Ledger, §13.3) — lakefiles are user code with declared effect surfaces.

title: G0-4: Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike — macro-heavy corpus tree/position diff
description:
G0-4 Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike (§22.1-4, feeds §9): parse a curated set of macro-heavy Corpus files and compare Syntax trees, byte positions, and hygiene observables against the Reference (driven through its own frontend under instrumentation in the Tribunal sandbox).

Hygiene is the classic reimplementation graveyard: macro scopes appended to names, quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching, and the parse/elaborate interleaving (a command can install a parser used three lines later) are all API surface that metaprograms and diagnostics observe. This spike visits the graveyard early. Curate files whose MEANING depends on hygiene corner cases: scope-shadowing across macro expansion, antiquotation splices, syntax categories with custom precedence, notation-priority interactions, auto-bound implicit capture.

Acceptance: (a) node kinds, atom/ident structure, SourceInfo (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions) byte-agree on the curated set; (b) hygiene-decorated generated names agree under the stable-expansion-path derivation; (c) every divergence triaged: fidelity defect (fix) or undocumented-upstream-behavior (fixture + upstream report + Behavior Note candidate); (d) findings feed the fln-syntax / fln-parse interface freeze. Tests: the curated set becomes the standing hygiene fidelity rig in fln-conformance, run per-commit.

title: W10: Backends & ABI doors — Iron native backend, JIT, C parity, outbound plugins
description:
Workstream W10 "Backends & ABI doors" (§17.3, §17.4, §11.3): the sovereign compilation path and the bidirectional plugin promise.

Scope: Iron static backend — FLBC to native objects/executables (x86-64, aarch64 first) with NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc: the owned baseline emitter grown a static mode; lake build of executables with zero external toolchain (the G6 headline). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): baseline template JIT with inline caches burned in; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig; certified builds never enable it. --backend c parity generator: emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 optional tool #1) — parity apparatus and exotic-target escape hatch, never the anchor. Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics on the differential corpus. ABI door outbound (§17.4, the harder direction, staged to G5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the Reference host, per-platform Tribunal matrix; inbound (Reference plugins into FrankenLean) lands earlier under W2/G2. Risk R5 honesty: outbound tiers per platform in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5.

title: W2: olean-next + fln olean tool — content-addressed modules, sealed capsules, diffs
description:
Implement olean-next + the fln olean tool (§7.4, frontier): the second module lane — a content-addressed, position-independent format: per-declaration records keyed by Ledger hashes, structure-shared term DAGs with dictionary compression, mergeable and diffable, mmap-friendly WITHOUT relocation, carrying certificate attachments (§8.6) inline. olean-next never replaces olean in faithful/sound artifacts (D-08); it is the Ledger native interchange and the format proof-cache federation speaks.

Capsule shapes: THIN (references into the shared CAS) and SEALED (a complete transitive object pack, verifiable on an air-gapped machine — the archival/audit form, and the .flnpack the WASM Judge consumes). Attested evidence objects may carry RaptorQ repair sidecars via the asupersync coder; repair reconstructs to staging, re-verifies the canonical hash and a full schema decode, publishes without changing identity, and journals the corruption/repair event — repair is a durability feature, NEVER a trust shortcut. The fln olean CLI tool converts both directions (olean <-> olean-next) and prints human-readable diffs of module contents — the un-opaquing of weakness census #8 (pointer-dump oleans are unmergeable, un-diffable, un-addressable).

Acceptance: round-trip olean -> olean-next -> olean preserves semantic hash and (faithful path) bytes; sealed capsules verify air-gapped (no CAS access); repair-sidecar loss and corruption drills pass with journaled events; diff output validated on known module pairs. Tests: format fuzzing under budgets; thin/sealed equivalence property tests; a tamper test (any flipped byte fails verification with a typed error naming the object); dictionary-compression determinism (same input, same bytes).

title: W12: Epoch ratchet — mechanical pin advances, census diffs, migration beads
description:
Implement the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (§22.5, §13.6): the mechanical 8 steps — (1) fetch + hash the official release into the epoch lab; (2) regenerate every census on every platform (ABI, olean, extern, builtin, options, CLI, LSP schemas); (3) run the existing corpus differentially and mine new behavior into fixtures + fresh instrumented-oracle traces; (4) machine-diff the public/Meta API, artifact, ABI, CLI, Lake, LSP surfaces; (5) regenerate facade stubs and OPEN MIGRATION BEADS for every moved symbol; (6) close or classify every new divergence (unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim); (7) run the unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; (8) publish the capsule, evidence bundle, and migration report — then move the pin.

Cadence target: a new stable upstream release reaches R3 within four weeks and R4 by the following cycle, lag publicly visible on the dashboard (risk R14: the newest closed epoch stays R4 while later epochs ship R2/R3 previews — latest-parity never implied). Supported-epoch count: initial posture current + previous stable (OQ-10). Epoch bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) are the pressure-release valve keeping the treadmill from owning the roadmap.

Acceptance: the ratchet runs end-to-end against a SIMULATED epoch advance (a synthetic delta over the current pin) producing the capsule, the census diffs, the auto-opened migration beads, and the block-on-unclassified behavior; the lag dashboard renders from ledger data. Tests: census-diff correctness fixtures (planted deltas per surface must be caught and classified); bead-opening automation tests; block-on-unclassified enforcement test.

This bead owns epoch acquisition, regeneration, differentials, surface diffs, facade regeneration, classification gates, downstream dual-epoch runs, capsule/report production, and the manifest. franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w consumes those outputs for project repair.

title: W1: Substrate & Contracts — fln-core, extraction law, Tribunal bootstrap, baselines
description:
Workstream W1 "Substrate & Contracts" (§22.2): the foundation everything else compiles against, plus the conformance apparatus bootstrapped FIRST so every later workstream is born differential.

Scope: fln-core (names, universe levels, Expr constructor inventory with cached hashes / loose-bvar ranges / has-fvar+has-mvar flags exactly as the Reference exposes them — §1.1; literals, options, byte positions); fln-hash (owned BLAKE3-class hashing, domain-separation registry, canonical serialization schemas); the mechanical contract-extraction toolchain (ABI_CONTRACT.md, OLEAN_CONTRACT.md, extern + builtin censuses per Appendices B/C — extracted from the pin by checked-in scripts, never hand-transcribed, CI re-extracts and fails on drift, Rule D5/D9); the Tribunal rig + differential harness against the Reference binaries (epoch laboratory: hashed official archives, CLI/LSP transcripts, quirk ledgers, per-platform reproduction commands — §18); the W1 baseline performance report of the Reference at the pin on both pinned bare-metal profiles (§19.1 — gate numbers are ratified against it); the dependency-closure allowlist + SUITE.lock ceremony (D1); windows functional CI; G0 coordination.

Depends on nothing; everything depends on it. Exit: contracts generated and CI-locked, Tribunal executes a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential, baseline report checked in, closure audit green.

title: W7: simp faithful core + deterministic compiled rewrite-index format
description:
Implement the simp faithful core + compiled rewrite indexes (§12.1, §12.2): the Reference simp semantics — simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree candidate retrieval, congruence closure interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors, attribute-driven extension — implemented natively end to end (driver, congruence interplay, extension dispatch are Anvil code behind obs-class Mirror facade rows), with the Corpus simp-heavy files plus instrumented-oracle rewrite traces as the fidelity corpus. Mathlib simp EXTENSIONS and simprocs are user code on Golem calling the native driver through the facade.

Compiled rewrite indexes (§12.2): per-library discrimination automata compiled at Ledger commit time and shipped as content-addressed CAS artifacts (an olean-next attachment) — mathlib tens of thousands of simp lemmas become a preloaded matcher, not a per-session index build. Staleness is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (content-addressed against the simp-set own hash).

Acceptance: simp outcomes match the oracle on the simp-heavy fidelity corpus (rewrite firings, order where observable, result terms); index-backed retrieval produces identical candidate sets to freshly-built trees (representation-equivalence tests); index build determinism (same simp-set hash, same artifact bytes). Tests: golden rewrite-trace replay; dsimp/simp-only behavioral fixtures; congruence-interplay differentials; a stale-index mutation test (perturbed simp-set with unperturbed index must be structurally unloadable).

title: W9: Replay bundles + protocol rigs — deterministic server bugs, editor smoke matrix
description:
Implement session replay bundles + protocol rigs (§14.3, §18.2): every session can emit a replay bundle (init transcript, edit and request sequence, content ids or sealed sources, scheduling profile, normalized outbound transcript); fln server replay bundle reproduces the session and reports the FIRST divergence — server bugs become deterministic objects, not race anecdotes.

Protocol rigs: recorded editor sessions replayed against BOTH servers (FrankenLean and the Reference) with wire-level diffing under registered nondeterminism classes (request interleaving) normalized by versioned normalizer code; the vscode-lean4 smoke matrix in CI (open, edit, hover, goals, completion, go-to-def, widgets, waitForDiagnostics across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); the C5 corpus grown continuously from real usage.

Acceptance: replay determinism — a bundle replays bit-identically under the lab runtime; the smoke matrix green across the editor scenarios; first-divergence reporting demonstrated on a seeded server mutant. Tests: bundle round-trip fixtures; normalizer-honesty tests (a normalizer may never discard an error body to pass — planted-divergence detection); torture under the asupersync lab: cancellation storms, fault injection, crash-recovery of session state with seed replay (§18.2 torture lane).

title: W8: Cache federation + epoch bridges — validated atp CAS, conservative cross-epoch reuse
description:
Implement cache federation + epoch-stable caching (§13.5, §13.6): the CAS syncs over atp (multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable) or HTTPS fallback, entries signed and verified; a fln cache shim preserves the lake exe cache muscle memory and hydrates from the community existing archives during transition. Because records are keyed semantically, a cache populated by ANY machine on the epoch serves every project shape — first-mathlib-build becomes: sync frontier, elaborate your delta (PG-10: hydration >= 2x lake exe cache get on the lossy-path fixture, >= 1x clean).

Security law (R18): a remote entry is UNTRUSTED DATA until validated locally — node key and schema, content hashes and closure, epoch/platform compatibility, capability-input binding, kernel/checker receipts where relevant, signature policy, decompression bounds; a malformed entry becomes a miss plus a security event, never trusted-state poison; concurrent fill is lease-suppressed; corruption quarantines are first-class records.

Epoch-stable caching: toolchain bumps re-key the epoch, but the Ledger records WHY — a pin advance ships a machine-diffed census, and records provably untouched by the delta may carry epoch bridges (frontier, off by default, conservative allowlist, gated behind its own soundness rig — OQ-6 owns allowlist v1). The migration autopilot (audit project against the new epoch census, propose rewrites from the repair classes, lockstep builds, apply-transaction with rollback; never edits without explicit apply) is specified here and consumes the Bloodhound repair engine (W11).

Acceptance: PG-10 on the WAN fixtures; validation rejects every corpus of malformed entries; bridge soundness rig green on allowlist v1. Tests: multi-donor pull under injected loss; poisoned-entry fixtures; lease-suppression races under the lab; quarantine-and-revalidate drills.

design: FL-INV-05 FEDERATION AND BRIDGE BOUNDARY — remote signatures and atp reconstruction prove transport/integrity only, never semantic reuse. Every hydrated object re-enters the local untrusted-candidate validation path. An epoch bridge is a versioned proof object over an allowlisted census-delta class and exact input roots; missing, unknown, ambiguous, revoked, or over-budget bridge evidence is a miss. Poison, replay, cross-tenant/capability, decompression, equivocation, lease, and bridge-unsoundness mutations must fail without contaminating trusted state.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Companion to franken_lean-fnj (which fixed the DECODE path). Discovered during the fnj review (Claude Fable session, 2026-07-22). crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs Canonical::write_body for Expr and Level is recursive over tree depth, and Expr/Level themselves (nested Arc<ExprNode>/Arc<Node>) recurse on Drop. A sufficiently deep tree therefore overflows the stack when re-encoded (e.g. computing a decl-content digest) or when dropped. LOWER REACHABILITY than fnj: this requires a deep tree already in memory, which now (post-fnj) can arise from decoding a valid deep olean — real mathlib terms are nowhere near deep enough to trigger it, but a crafted valid deep artifact could. Fix: make write_body iterative (explicit work-stack, mirroring the fnj decoder) and add manual iterative Drop impls for Expr and Level that disassemble the Arc chain without recursion. Regression: encode + drop a ~1e5-deep valid term on a 1 MiB-stack thread without abort. Not urgent; filed so the fnj decode fix isn't mistaken for closing the whole depth-safety story.

title: W7: Verdict — owned CDCL SAT with owned proof logging and checker
description:
Implement Verdict (fln-verdict, §12.5): an owned CDCL SAT solver — watched literals, VSIDS/CHB-class heuristics, clause management, inprocessing kept modest and honest — with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker: the bv_decide pipeline bit-blasts through Verdict, Verdict emits a checkable unsatisfiability certificate, and a small independent checker (kernel-adjacent in audit rigor, separate in code) validates it before the reflected proof is admitted. The external-binary TCB (CaDiCaL + LRAT toolchain, weakness census #7) dies here; Behavior Note BN-06 records the change (same theorems, owned certificate chain, no external solver).

Parity posture (risk R17 honesty): correctness is absolute from day one; competitiveness is a ratchet — PG-9 tracks Verdict against the Reference solver on the bv_decide corpus with a published gap budget (100% agreement; wall <= 2x Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, ratcheting thereafter). If the gap persists, it PUBLISHES honestly; semantics never depended on solver speed.

Acceptance: 100% verdict agreement on the bv_decide corpus; every unsat certificate validated by the independent checker before admission; PG-9 wall within budget at G5. Tests: SAT/UNSAT differential vs known-good results on standard benchmarks + the bv_decide corpus; certificate-checker independence verified structurally (no shared solver modules); fuzzed CNF + fuzzed bit-blasting inputs under budgets; a planted bogus-certificate must be rejected.

title: Gate G6: Sovereign & Beyond — no-cc executables, Ledger GA, R4 declared
description:
Convergence gate G6 "Sovereign & Beyond" (§22.3): Iron builds real executables with no C compiler; Ledger GA with cache federation and PG-3 (the cone) + PG-10 (cache hydration); the leapfrog surfaces shipped (Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge); W12 distribution complete; --reproducible certified across the matrix. title: W3: fln-bignum — owned kernel-grade bignum, ABI-layout limbs, PG-K gate
description:
Implement fln-bignum (§8.4): kernel-grade arbitrary precision replacing GMP — limb-based magnitude representation matching the ABI bignum-object layout (Marrow mpz-shim is a VIEW, not a conversion), schoolbook/Karatsuba/Toom multiplication with thresholds tuned on the KERNEL-REDUCTION corpus (not synthetic benchmarks), Knuth-D division, binary/Lehmer gcd, exact decimal parsing/printing for literals. Constant-time-ness explicitly NOT claimed (this is a prover, not a KMS).

Why owned: GMP sits under every literal in the Reference and inside kernel reduction itself (§1.10) — an unauditable C dependency in the most trusted position. The kernel accelerated Nat operation set (add/sub/mul/div/mod/pow/gcd/comparisons/bitwise per the pin) routes through fln-bignum so decide-class proofs keep the same asymptotics. The crate stays in-repo rather than the suite (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant; revisit trigger recorded if a second suite member needs bigints — §2.11).

Acceptance: PG-K gate — kernel-reduction operation mix <= 1.15x the GMP-backed Reference kernel on the arithmetic-heavy fixture set (parity on the small operands that dominate; bounded, PUBLISHED gap on giants); ABI-layout view verified against C4 probes. Tests: differential vectors vs known-good results across the full operation set incl. boundary limb counts; parsing/printing round-trips vs Reference outputs; threshold-tuning harness checked in with the corpus profile it tuned against.
