comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Arithmetic-core bootstrap started by session agent (claim blocked; ABI-limb layout half pends 53v contracts). Surface: crates/fln-bignum, scripts/extract/gen_bignum_vectors.py.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reviewer note on the live uncommitted snapshot, no edits: scripts/e2e/bignum_vectors.sh cannot currently seed its advertised division-by-zero mutant. The seeder replaces the first BigNat::from_u64(0) occurrence, but nat.rs contains no such occurrence; div_rem returns BigNat::zero instead. Because the Python seeder runs while shell errexit is active, its no-op exit also terminates the script before mutation_rc is captured or the seeded_mutant failure event is emitted. The header additionally says truncated-subtraction mutation while the implemented scenario targets division by zero. Use an exact semantically anchored replacement with an asserted single match and capture the seeder status under the same explicit error-handling pattern as other steps. This is an E2E correctness finding, not a claim against the arithmetic implementation.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Second reviewer finding on the live snapshot: the module-level statement that no code path panics on any input and the bead requirement for typed resource exhaustion are not yet true. BigNat::shl accepts an arbitrary u64 count, converts the limb count to usize, then allocates that many zero limbs directly; a huge count can trigger capacity or allocation failure rather than a typed resource outcome, and the usize conversion is not portable to wasm32. Decimal parsing, pow, and other growth paths likewise expose infallible allocation APIs with no budget or cancellation channel. This is expected to remain open during bootstrap, but the public contract must be weakened now or, preferably, the kernel-facing growth operations must use checked sizing plus budget-aware fallible allocation and typed outcomes before the bead can close.

title: W12: Distribution & Epochs — releases, elan layout, epoch ratchet, Witness matrix
description:
Workstream W12 "Distribution & Epochs" (§22.5, §13.6, §17.1): shipping the sovereign toolchain and keeping it current against a monthly upstream without ever lying about parity.

Scope: static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line can name a FrankenLean toolchain and everything downstream just works; the install script; version-line parse compatibility with a machine-readable implementation field + fln identity --json (compatibility never becomes deception, §17.1); reproducible releases (built twice in isolated builders and byte-compared; --reproducible certified matrix; windows bit-certification as a separate declared decision, D17); the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (the mechanical 8 steps of §22.5: fetch+hash, regenerate censuses on every platform, run corpus differentials, machine-diff all public surfaces, regenerate facade stubs + open migration beads per moved symbol, close-or-classify every divergence — unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim, run unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs, publish capsule + evidence bundle + migration report); side-by-side epoch installs; the public lag dashboard (target: new stable reaches R3 in four weeks, R4 next cycle); the Witness claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4: docs wording may never exceed evidence state; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.

This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: fln-core — names, levels, Expr inventory, options, positions
description:
Implement fln-core (§21, §1.1): the shared vocabulary every crate compiles against. Names (hierarchical, with macro-scope decoration hooks for Quill), universe levels (zero/succ/max/imax/param/mvar with the normalization laws incl. imax u 0 = 0 — Prop impredicativity depends on it), the kernel expression inventory (bvar de Bruijn, fvar, mvar, sort, const+levels, app, lam, forallE, letE, lit Nat/String, mdata, proj) with the Reference-observable accessories: cached hashes, loose-bvar ranges, has-fvar/has-mvar flags (metaprograms and fast traversal pruning read these — they are API, not internals). Options model with the canonical resource limits (maxHeartbeats 200000 in thousand-units, maxRecDepth 512) and the newer resource-limit surface. Byte positions in UTF-8 exactly as upstream computes them (wire-visible through diagnostics and LSP).

Distinct newtypes per semantic kind from day one (DeclNameId, ExprNodeId, BinderDepth, ReductionFuel, EnvCommitId — §8.2b): no integer is accepted as two semantic kinds anywhere in the workspace.

Acceptance: constructor inventory + flag semantics verified against C0 micro-fixtures derived from the pin; hashing observables match the Reference on the fixture corpus. Tests: property tests for level normalization laws; flag-computation unit tests; fixture diff harness wired into fln-conformance.

title: fln-core Name lifecycle and structural operations must be stack-safe
description: Name is an unbounded persistent parent chain backed by Arc nodes. The current automatic destructor and the derived structural PartialEq, Eq, Hash, and Debug implementations recurse through every parent; Ord, quick_cmp_aux, and to_display_string also recurse. A deeply nested Name can therefore exhaust the call stack during ordinary cleanup, comparison, hashing, diagnostics, or rendering. It can also be embedded as a leaf payload in Expr and Level, so those types are not fully depth-independent merely because their own node drains are iterative. Replace every input-depth recursive Name lifecycle and structural traversal with safe iterative logic while preserving the pinned Lean observable hash, prefix-first Ord, quickCmp ordering, rendering, structural sharing, Arc reference counts, and shallow behavior. No semantic depth cap, larger-stack workaround, leak, unsafe code, or hidden fallback is acceptable. Add deterministic constructor-complete tests for deep string, numeric, mixed, shared-prefix, cloned-root, unequal-prefix, overflow-component, diagnostic, and cleanup cases; exact shallow old-versus-new behavior vectors; randomized clone/drop orders; and bounded-stack real-path verification with clean recovery. title: W1: fln-hash — owned hashing, domain separation, canonical serialization
description:
Implement fln-hash (§21): owned BLAKE3-class hashing with a domain-separation registry and canonical serialization schemas — the identity layer beneath the Ledger (decl hashes, logical roots), Grimoire (semantic hash vs byte hash, §7.3), receipts, the transparency log, and every cache key in the program.

Requirements: (a) domain separation is a REGISTRY, not a convention — every hash use site declares its domain tag and CI greps for unregistered hashing; (b) canonical serialization schemas are versioned, with the schema registry shared with the codecs (Appendix B: every durable format specified once, generated into both the codecs and the conformance corpus); (c) the semantic-hash / byte-hash distinction is structural: re-encoding and compression can change bytes without pretending to change meaning; (d) logical roots (§7.1) digest declarations + extension deltas + options while EXCLUDING wall-clock, paths, and scheduler traces — two hosts producing the same trusted environment share a logical root.

Acceptance: hash vectors checked in and stable across platforms; nothing in the program hashes outside this crate (CI-enforced). Tests: unit tests per hash domain with frozen vectors (cross-platform identity on the certified matrix); canonical-serialization round-trip property tests over generated values; a domain-confusion mutation test (same bytes hashed under two domains MUST differ, and a planted unregistered hash call must fail CI); logical-root determinism e2e test — two elaboration runs on different thread counts and (in CI) different hosts produce identical roots, with the full input closure logged in the test output for diagnosis on mismatch.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W1: Quality gates — scripts/check.sh, CI pipeline, e2e smoke harness with detailed logging
description:
Build the quality-gate infrastructure AGENTS.md promises: scripts/check.sh running cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test in order, stopping on first failure — wired as the CI test step (never duplicated inline in workflow files), plus ubs integration on changed files and the e2e smoke harness.

The e2e smoke harness is the always-runnable end-to-end script family with GREAT, DETAILED LOGGING: each script logs every step (command, inputs, expected vs actual, timing, environment fingerprint) to both human-readable and NDJSON streams so failures are diagnosable from CI artifacts alone without reproduction. Initial smokes: workspace-builds-cold (clean checkout -> cargo build), contracts-regenerate (extraction scripts run, drift check), tribunal-smoke (Reference-vs-Reference differential returns clean), and — growing with each gate — judge-smoke (fln check-olean on a small fixture set), parity-smoke (a tiny corpus through T2). Every future subsystem bead extends this family rather than inventing parallel harnesses; test logging conventions (structured events, stable ids, no swallowed stderr) are established here once.

Acceptance: scripts/check.sh green locally and in CI; a seeded failure in each stage stops the pipeline with the failing stage named; e2e logs validate against the NDJSON schema and are archived as CI artifacts. Tests: self-test mode for the harness (planted failures produce correct exit codes and log records); log-schema contract test.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead is the mandatory verification contract and scenario registry, not only a convenience script.

In addition to the existing scope, define a machine-checked verification manifest schema keyed by bead id, requirement/claim id, FL-INV id, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, workstream, and convergence/performance gate. Every implementation bead must register: deterministic unit tests for success/boundary/error/resource/cancel/failure-atomicity behavior; applicable property, metamorphic, fuzz, mutation-kill, and fault-drill coverage; at least one real user-path E2E scenario and one negative/recovery scenario; and the evidence artifacts that close the requirement. CI fails on an implemented row with no registered coverage, an orphan scenario, a release claim backed only by mocks, an unclassified skip, or a scenario that does not assert its final state.

The shared E2E runner must produce paired human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs containing run/scenario/step correlation ids, bead/claim/invariant/row/gate ids, redacted command argv, cwd, toolchain epoch, mode/profile, platform, thread count, seed, cache state, host facts, canonical input hashes, expected/actual values, monotonic timing, resource/budget facts, exit/signal, stdout/stderr artifact references, receipt/logical roots, first-divergence records, and cleanup/final-state assertions. Logs are bounded and secret-redacted, stderr is never swallowed, and CI archives everything needed to diagnose a failure without rerunning it.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — process supervision and evidence publication are part of the trusted test-result boundary. Every launched stage must be causally contained before target exec: establish the guardian/subreaper, session/process-group identity, start ticks, and pidfd handles behind a bounded child gate, publish readiness only after those bindings exist, then release exec. Never signal a raw numeric PID after an asynchronous gap. Setup/readiness has its own bounded timeout; execution timeout starts only after child-authored readiness. Fast leader exit, detached descendants, PID churn/reuse, pidfd exhaustion, cancellation storms, and every supported signal are mandatory typed cases; successful, semantic-nonzero, unexpected-nonzero, and signalled immediate-exit commands run as a repeated stress matrix with zero false internal faults and zero survivors.

Finalization installs one common HUP/INT/TERM handler atomically before doing work and uses a durable write-once decision protocol. `validate-bundle` remains read-only; a distinct `adopt-bundle` operation may recover a valid decision by O_EXCL marker publication, fsync of marker plus every parent through the artifact root, and full post-adoption revalidation. Relative/absolute artifact arguments are normalized and containment-checked once; parent walks detect fixed points and every finalizer has a bounded termination budget. Concurrent adopters are idempotent, cancellation cannot be adopted as pass, mismatched decision/marker fails closed, and crash points from decision through directory fsync have named recovery tests. Quality runs use a per-attempt isolated Cargo target (or prove cache identity cryptographically), bind UBS inventory to the final governed source, and never promote inconclusive/internal-fault evidence.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the evidence envelope begins before fallible setup. Once a fresh artifact directory exists, UBS inventory, vendor binding, governed-root hashing, host/argv fact capture, initial event publication, snapshot construction, and every other setup step execute under the same finalization state machine as ordinary stages. Setup failure, concurrent governed-source drift, cancellation, or internal fault must still yield exactly one typed terminal record and a durable independently validatable bundle; the pre-envelope may do only argument validation and atomic creation/refusal of the artifact directory. No run may strand an ambiguous partial directory containing setup artifacts but no verdict.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — authoritative builds run inside a sealed compiler environment. Resolve the exact SUITE.lock toolchain before repository-controlled compilation, invoke cargo and rustc through that explicit rustup channel, and exclude or fail closed on ambient RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS, RUSTC and Cargo rustc-wrapper overrides, RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, target-specific rustflags, Cargo-home configuration, and ancestor .cargo configuration. Use per-attempt isolated Cargo home and target directories without deleting or mutating user caches, and record the admitted environment plus compiler path/version/commit in the initial evidence envelope. This is required because pinned rustc accepts deny(unsafe_code) and forbid(unsafe_code) crates containing unsafe when cap-lints allow is injected; source posture and environment isolation are independent defenses.
acceptance_criteria:
The verification manifest and log schemas are frozen with contract tests; every existing task bead has a registered verification obligation; seeded omissions (missing unit coverage, mock-only gate evidence, missing negative E2E, unclassified skip, swallowed stderr, absent final-state assertion, secret leakage, or malformed NDJSON) fail CI with the exact bead and requirement named. scripts/check.sh and the shared runner work locally and in CI, and a planted E2E failure is diagnosable from archived artifacts alone.

Process-boundary acceptance is additive: the built-in self-test and an external 100-iteration immediate-exit matrix are green for exit 0, registered semantic nonzero, unexpected nonzero, and signal termination; delayed child readiness cannot consume execution budget; detached-child and PID-reuse churn leave zero survivors and never signal an unrelated process. Deterministic HUP/INT/TERM injection at spawn, running, terminal-publication, finalizer-entry, decision-written, marker-linked, and post-fsync boundaries always yields one schema-valid terminal record and one durable bundle whose typed verdict matches the linearization point. Relative and absolute publication paths terminate identically; escaping paths fail boundedly; validate is side-effect-free; adoption is durable and concurrently idempotent. One stable-source full scripts/check.sh run and both real no-mock structural E2Es must independently validate their committed bundles before this bead closes.

Setup-boundary acceptance is additive: plant failure and cancellation at every step from artifact-directory creation through the first stage readiness, plus a concurrent governed-file mutation during initial hashing. Every case terminates within budget with one schema-valid terminal event, an explicit setup/inconclusive/internal classification, bounded retained stdout/stderr, and a committed bundle whose manifest inventories the partial setup artifacts. Reused or escaping artifact paths fail before mutation; every fresh accepted path is either durably finalized or carries a separately machine-readable pre-creation refusal outside that path.

Compiler-environment acceptance is additive: a hostile-environment matrix injects cap-lints allow through ordinary and encoded rustflags, fake rustc and workspace wrappers, an alternate toolchain, target-specific rustflags, repository/ancestor/Cargo-home config, and equivalent supported Cargo channels. Every attempt is either rejected before repository compilation with a typed bundle or demonstrably excluded from the effective rustc argv; no hostile marker executes. Each mutation is followed by a clean recovery run, and the bundle independently proves the exact compiler commit and isolated Cargo home/target identity.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Live gate regression after the current signal-handling edits (2026-07-22 10:46 EDT): ./scripts/check.sh exits 2 at evidence-self-test. In target/check/check-20260722T144655Z-1841227/evidence-self-test/semantic_failure/stage.meta.json, a planted child exit 7 is misclassified as cancelled/signal_SIGTERM (wrapper 4, child_exit 7), so self-test aborts with `evidence: semantic exit was not a failure`. The outer check evidence bundle itself commits and validates as typed internal_fault. Please preserve/restore supervisor signal state between self-test cases and add an order-sensitive regression proving a preceding cancellation/terminal-publication test cannot leak SIGTERM state into the semantic-failure case. fln-8mj E2E is green at target/e2e/structure-gate-20260722T143809Z-1629724, but fln-8mj cannot close until this mandatory full gate passes.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Additional independent failure in the same check run: outer evidence-self-test.meta.json reports supervisor failure EvidenceError: cannot inspect process 1844927: [Errno 3] No such process from /proc/1844927/stat. Current proc_stat_facts treats FileNotFoundError as an ordinary vanished process but routes ProcessLookupError/ESRCH through generic OSError into internal_fault. /proc process lifetime races can surface ESRCH too; classify that as vanished/None and add a rapid-exit race regression. This is distinct from the leaked SIGTERM classification in comment 22.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Causal correction after correlating metadata/timestamps: the ESRCH race and semantic_failure SIGTERM are probably not independent. The outer supervisor wraps evidence.py self-test; its metadata has the /proc ESRCH error plus resource.term_sent=true. That outer failure occurred while the inner semantic_failure case ran (inner 14:47:05.072-05.152 UTC; outer ended 05.244), so terminate_tree legitimately delivered SIGTERM to the self-test process and the inner case observed it. There is not yet evidence of a prior-case signal-state leak. First make proc_stat_facts treat ESRCH as an ordinary vanished process, then rerun the order-sensitive signal test to determine whether a separate restoration bug remains.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Follow-up live supervisor race after the 18-case self-test passed (2026-07-22 10:52 EDT): ./scripts/check.sh now passes evidence-self-test, shellcheck, fmt, check, and clippy, but exits 2 during cargo test. target/check/check-20260722T145218Z-1952497/test.meta.json records `supervisor failure: EvidenceError: cannot inspect process 1959206: [Errno 3] No such process`; child_exit is null and the supervisor sent TERM even though the direct fresh-target full cargo test is green. This is a normal short-lived cargo test subprocess race in descendant discovery. proc_children/live_tree_members must treat ENOENT after task enumeration as process exit, not internal fault, while still failing closed on permission/malformed-proc errors. Add a high-churn many-short-children stress case (repeat enough to hit PID/task disappearance between enumeration/read/open) and prove pass classification plus zero survivors.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Gate checkpoint: target/check/check-20260722T145649Z-2037194 passed evidence-self-test, shellcheck, fmt, check, clippy, cargo test, structure-guard, and vendor-tree. UBS then exited 1 on one scanner-critical timing-sensitive-comparison finding at the terminal signal-name assertion. The assertion is not secret material, but policy requires all UBS criticals fixed; current scripts/evidence.py already uses hmac.compare_digest there. Because that fix landed during the run, the failure bundle was intentionally not close evidence. A stable-source full rerun remains required; concurrent fln-euo work is now changing governed fln-conformance inputs, so wait for that active edit window to settle.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Fresh static blocker in the active check.sh finalizer edit: it now accepts any existing validate-bundle-green marker even when complete-bundle returned nonzero, claiming validation durably adopts it. validate-bundle is read-only; it does not prove the marker link and parent-directory entry were fsynced. A crash or SIGKILL after link but before directory fsync can leave a valid-looking yet non-durable marker, and an actual fsync error is also being rounded up to success. Preserve fail-closed semantics unless a typed adoption operation explicitly fsyncs the marker and parent directory, then revalidates all bindings; distinguish post-commit signal from publication/fsync failure. Mere existence plus content validation is not durability evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: New deterministic-test blocker from target/check/check-20260722T150547Z-2286209: evidence-self-test exited 2 after the timeout case was correctly classified, because timeout-pids.txt did not yet exist. The 250 ms timeout starts before spawn/scheduling and the test immediately reads the child-created file after supervision, so machine load can terminate the child before it publishes descendant identities. Do not just weaken the survivor assertion. Add an explicit child-ready handshake/test hook before starting the timeout clock (or run the wrapper asynchronously, wait for the PID file/readiness, then arm a deterministic timeout), and retain proof that every published PID is dead. This is a real order/load flake in a mandatory gate.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh umbrella-gate finding (2026-07-22 11:06 EDT): check run target/check/check-20260722T150547Z-2286209 failed evidence self-test before cargo gates because self_test reads timeout-pids.txt unconditionally after a 250 ms supervised timeout, but under host load the child had not executed the write before timeout/termination (Errno 2). The timeout case currently tests scheduling luck, not just descendant cleanup. Please make setup deterministic (for example, have the child publish a readiness sentinel/PID file and have the test wait with a bounded monotonic deadline before arming the timeout, or otherwise separate setup from the timed operation); preserve typed inconclusive semantics and add a delayed-start regression. Bundle correctly committed as internal_fault, so this is a harness self-test defect, not fln-8mj product failure.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Second umbrella attempt found an active-edit parser mismatch (run target/check/check-20260722T150920Z-2390203): cmd_run passes args.test_fail_after_link at evidence.py:3644, but --test-fail-after-link is currently registered only on complete-bundle (line 4692), not the run subparser. The evidence-self-test wrapper therefore crashes with AttributeError before executing its child. During the same run, the governed UBS inventory changed and finalization correctly refused close evidence. Please either keep this injection parameter internal to complete-bundle or define the run-parser field with a safe default, and add parser smoke coverage over every evidence.py subcommand so Namespace/handler drift is caught deterministically.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Static recheck after the latest 19-case self-test happened to pass: the timeout race from comments 31/32 is still present in current scripts/evidence.py. The timeout case starts a supervised 250 ms deadline and then unconditionally reads timeout-pids.txt; stage.ready.json only proves the supervisor spawned the child, not that the child executed its PID-file write. There is no child-ready handshake before the timeout clock. A loaded host can still kill the child before line 3870 and reproduce Errno 2. Please do not treat one green self-test as remediation; add a child-created readiness sentinel and arm the timed phase only after a bounded wait, while retaining zero-survivor proof. Also, target/check/check-20260722T150920Z-2390203 began during the 15:09:59 evidence.py edit and ended missing supervisor metadata/incomplete bundle, so it is not close evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Repetition result against the current file: six sequential direct self-test runs all passed 19/19 (artifacts target/check/rur-selftest-stress-20260722T1515Z-{1..6}). This lowers the observed frequency under the present load but does not close the statically present pre-child-readiness race described in comment 34; the earlier target/check/check-20260722T150547Z-2286209 failure is already a live counterexample. Keep the bead open until setup and timeout arming are causally separated.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: New deterministic CLI-supervisor blocker against current scripts/evidence.py: `python3 scripts/evidence.py run ... -- /usr/bin/true` returns 2/internal_fault instead of pass. Artifact target/check/rur-cli-run-parser-probe-20260722T1517Z/stage.meta.json reports `supervisor failure: EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime`; readiness is incorrectly labeled spawn_failed even though exec succeeded. Race: run_supervised reads non-zombie child facts, then remember_process/pidfd binding loses the very short-lived child and promotes a successful stage to internal fault. Add real CLI-parser/supervisor smoke cases for immediate exit 0 and immediate semantic/nonsemantic nonzero exits. The fix must bind/control the child lifetime before it can execute/exit (or otherwise prove terminal status and descendant scope safely); do not merely ignore failed pidfd binding, since a fast leader could leave detached descendants.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Current-snapshot reproduction after the nested-guardian changes: 20 sequential real CLI runs supervising `/usr/bin/true` produced 5 false internal_fault results (iterations 1,2,6,15,16) and 15 passes. Artifacts: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-stress-20260722T1529Z-{1..20}; each failure reports supervisor_or_capture_failure/cannot bind child process lifetime. This confirms comment 36 remains live and frequent, not stale from a pre-guardian snapshot. Stable structural E2E passed because its commands happened to live long enough; full runner correctness still requires a before-exec lifetime barrier and immediate exit 0/semantic-exit regression lane.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Design note for comments 37/41 (no code edit; PinkCat still owns the path): a race-free supervisor needs an exec barrier, not a retry around pidfd_open. One viable Linux design is an internal child-gate executable/subcommand that becomes the session leader, self-stops before exec, and later execs the requested argv in the same PID after the parent binds start-ticks + pidfd + pgid and publishes readiness; the parent then SIGCONT via pidfd. Give the gate its own bounded setup-failure protocol and keep argv logging bound to the actual target, not the wrapper. Add immediate exit {0, registered semantic nonzero, unregistered nonzero, signal} stress tests plus a gate-that-fails-before-exec case and a target-that-detaches-a-child-then-exits case. For the timeout test, separately wait on a child-authored readiness sentinel under a bounded setup deadline, then arm the 250 ms execution budget; supervisor-spawn readiness is insufficient.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Source-stability confirmation for comment 38: a second 20-run `/usr/bin/true` CLI-supervisor stress on an unchanged scripts/evidence.py inode mtime (1784734114 before and after) produced 6/20 false internal_fault outcomes (iterations 2,11,12,15,17,19). Artifacts target/check/rur-immediate-exit-stress2-20260722T1530Z-{1..20}. This removes the possible active-edit confound from the first stress result.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review of the new bundle.decision recovery protocol (current evidence.py, 2026-07-22 11:37 EDT): validate_bundle now creates/links bundle.complete.json and fsyncs the bundle when only bundle.decision exists. That makes a command named validate-bundle state-mutating and turns ordinary read-only inspection into the recovery/commit linearization path. Please split this into an explicit typed adopt-bundle operation (decision validation -> canonical marker O_EXCL link -> marker/parent/ancestor fsync -> full revalidation) and keep validate-bundle strictly read-only; have shell finalizers invoke adopt only when complete-bundle failed but a valid decision exists, then invoke validate. Add tests proving validate alone never creates files, adoption is idempotent under concurrent adopters, a cancellation sentinel cannot be adopted, mismatched decision/marker fails, and crash after decision/before marker recovers durably. Direct current self-test passes 21 cases at target/evidence-self-test/copperhare-decision-probe-20260722t1137, but it does not cover this API side-effect contract or resolve the fast-exit/timeout races.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Static signal-safety blocker in current shell revision: ACTIVE_RUNNER_START_TICKS is populated in check.sh/structure_gate.sh/closure_audit.sh, but never read. stop_active_runner still begins with raw `kill -s "$name" "$pid"` and all liveness checks are raw /proc PID reads; a wrapper can exit and its PID be reused between capture/signal, especially in the SPAWNING pending-signal path. The new variable therefore proves nothing. Bind the wrapper lifetime before clearing SPAWNING and route TERM/liveness through a pidfd/start-tick-checked helper; on inability to bind, do not signal a raw PID. Add a deterministic fast-wrapper-exit plus injected pending-signal test that churns PIDs and proves no unrelated process is signaled. This is separate from the inner supervisor child-exec barrier in comments 37/41/42.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: New loaded-host self-test flake in PinkCat run target/evidence-self-test/postfix-review-final-20260722T1141Z: the first large_output_pass case was typed inconclusive/timeout (5 s budget, 10.49 s total including termination), with only ~0.30 CPU seconds consumed and both bounded tails correctly retained. The child was scheduler-starved/pipe-blocked long enough that the nominal happy-path timeout, not the behavior under test, failed. Keep deliberately short timing only in timeout/cancellation cases; give ordinary correctness cases a generous setup/execution budget or a deterministic progress handshake so shared-host contention cannot turn them into false harness failures. Preserve inconclusive classification (do not retry/promote it inside evidence); the outer gate may rerun a fresh attempt, but only a clean pass may close.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Authoritative-gate isolation finding (check artifact target/check/check-20260722T154516Z-3101780): default-target clippy failed with unresolved fln_core::{expr,lean_hash,level,name,options} even though current crates/fln-core/src/lib.rs exports all five and the immediately preceding cargo check passed. A fresh isolated target (`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-fln-clippy-proof-20260722t1146 cargo clippy --locked --all-targets -- -D warnings`) compiled every crate and exited 0 in 3.3 s. This is stale/shared Cargo target contamination (also previously observed as 26 vs current 29 structure-guard unit tests), not a source failure. scripts/check.sh must allocate a per-run target directory or cryptographically bind/validate any reused RCH target to the exact governed root/toolchain/profile; record it in evidence. Add a regression that seeds a stale same-package rmeta/test binary and proves the gate executes the current source. Never cargo clean/delete shared caches; use isolated targets.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Deterministic CPU-spin blocker found from the live nested-binding probes (2026-07-22 11:55 EDT). `complete_bundle` passes caller-supplied `manifest_path`/`digest_path` into `durably_sync_manifested_bundle`; that helper absolutizes `art_dir` but appends those two paths to `files` without absolutizing/require_within normalization. For a valid CLI invocation using relative paths, `parent = path.parent` remains relative while `art_dir` is absolute, so `while parent != art_dir` can never terminate; once `parent` reaches `.`, `parent.parent == parent` and it spins forever at 100% CPU. Live counterexamples are the relative-path complete-bundle processes for target/evidence-self-test/nested-binding-audit-20260722T155227Z. Normalize and containment-check manifest/digest/commit paths once at the helper boundary, and make the parent walk explicitly fail if it reaches a fixed point before art_dir. Add relative and absolute CLI regression cases plus an escaping-path case; all must terminate under a bounded outer harness with identical semantics.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: New deterministic nested-finalization failure in PinkCat probe target/check/manual-20260722T-nested-signal-v2 (2026-07-22 11:53 EDT): the inner planted `false` stage was recorded classification=fail/reason=child_exit_semantic_failure, then validate-run rejected it as `planted failure contract is inconsistent`. The outer wrapper correctly reached internal_fault/process_tree_cleanup_unproven, but its `evidence.py complete-bundle` process 3362819 has remained CPU-bound (~92% CPU, >3 minutes) instead of finishing/failing within a bounded publication budget; parent check/self-test and driver remain waiting. This is both a self-test contract mismatch and unbounded finalization defect. Add a unit case for planted semantic failure metadata/validation agreement and a complete-bundle termination budget or demonstrably finite algorithm; retain the artifact and do not promote this probe to close evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Post-fix source-stable regression (2026-07-22 12:05 EDT): 20 real CLI `evidence.py run ... -- /usr/bin/true` invocations against unchanged evidence.py identity 1784736052:201055 produced 3/20 false internal_fault results (iterations 1, 13, 19). All three metadata files report `supervisor_or_capture_failure` / `EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime`, null child exit, and zero survivors. Artifact: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-postfix-20260722T160506Z. This confirms the relative-path durability fix and 21-case self-test did not address the before-exec lifetime race from comments 37/41/42/43. Keep RUR and fln-8mj open until an exec barrier plus immediate-exit regression matrix is implemented and repeatedly green.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Verified finalizer-entry signal hole in all three current shells. `on_exit` installs HUP, INT, and TERM handlers in three separate `trap` commands (check.sh:371-373; closure_audit.sh:255-257; structure_gate.sh:255-257). A different signal arriving between those commands still invokes the old `on_signal`, whose `exit` aborts the already-running EXIT trap before terminal/manifest/bundle publication. Minimal Bash reproduction exited 143 with exactly `exit-enter` then `old-signal` and never `exit-finished`. Install one common HUP/INT/TERM finalizer handler atomically in a single `trap ... HUP INT TERM` command before any finalization work, then decode the signal inside it (or use one mode-aware handler throughout). Add deterministic entry-window HUP/INT/TERM tests proving a typed, committed final bundle; a green ordinary full gate does not cover this race.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh current-source counterexample after the 21-case self-test green bar (RubyForest, 2026-07-22 12:13 EDT): the source-stable 20x immediate-exit matrix against /usr/bin/true failed 3/20 (iterations 1, 2, 8), each wrapper exit 2 with classification=internal_fault, reason=supervisor_or_capture_failure, child_exit=null. scripts/evidence.py SHA-256 was 1f2533be7b8bdbe757641bfc1fd845f3f2059b4c80649deda3f32dc78fe7eaea before and after. Artifacts: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-current-20260722T161301Z-3671216. The immediately preceding source-stable built-in self-test passed 21 cases at target/evidence-self-test/rur-current-20260722T161205Z-3653372, demonstrating that current self-test coverage still misses the ordinary fast-exit lifetime-binding race. RUR/fln-8mj must remain open until child exec/lifetime identity is causally bound and a repeated immediate-exit matrix is deterministically green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Latest-revision verification (RubyForest, 2026-07-22 12:21 EDT): after evidence.py changed again, a source-stable 20x /usr/bin/true matrix still failed 5/20 (iterations 6, 7, 14, 15, 17), all exact EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime / internal_fault / null child exit. SHA-256 was 633f998950307129a8ff186f15d9b314f1320b96c7a9935b31d2ef004939be4 before and after. Artifacts: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-latest-20260722T162102Z-3816809. This supersedes any inference that the 12:17 evidence revision fixed comments 52/55; it did not add the required exec barrier.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: External matrix attempt retained at target/check/evidence-immediate-matrix-20260722T163153Z-3964136 and stopped after the evidence/check/E2E sources changed mid-run, so it is diagnostic only, not close evidence. Against starting evidence.py SHA 633f9989..., pass iterations immediately reproduced multiple false internal_fault/cannot-bind-child-lifetime results; iteration 59 also became an unexpected timeout. The live evidence.py changed to SHA 110c1d42... during the run, and all three shell wrappers changed afterward. No artifact was deleted. Please rerun the full 100x4 source-stable matrix after the pre-exec child gate and atomic finalizer handler revisions settle.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Review of the current uncommitted launch-gate revision (evidence.py SHA 110c1d42..., wrappers mtimes 12:33 EDT): the new gate binds the outer guardian before it forks the worker, which improves shell-to-guardian cancellation safety, but run_supervised still calls Popen(target) and only afterward reads /proc and pidfd-binds the target. Therefore the exact target-exec race remains, and a target that forks/exits before admission can also evade descendant scope. RUR acceptance requires the gate in the target child before target exec (or an equivalent causally bound spawn primitive), followed by parent pidfd/start-tick admission and only then release. Please retain the guardian gate but do not treat it as satisfying the 100x4 target matrix by itself. The three sequential on_exit trap installs also remain visible in current wrappers.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Dynamic proof against the current uncommitted guardian-gate protocol (RubyForest, 2026-07-22 12:39 EDT): 20 source-stable immediate-success attempts executed the exact sequence new wrappers intend—start gated guardian, bind its session-leader PID/start_ticks as the direct child, publish identity-checked release, then run /usr/bin/true. Result: 12/20 failed. Eight reached the target and reproduced internal_fault / EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime (iterations 2,3,4,7,8,9,14,17); three failed guardian binding (6,15,16); one failed guardian release (5). Artifact: target/check/rur-guardian-gate-immediate-20260722T163907Z-4039217. Combined evidence.py+check.sh hash was stable before/after at 1b932546c03de614e7b64087ea9bf48325a0e6dcae92d072a7c704b6106922c3; individual hashes at report time: evidence.py c413494196f9e4ea62b4eb6a52010a1accc20989387fd3f4b06aefa39f71463a, check.sh fd7399def01e7f8c961ee7741fe1ff2e64f2ffadbd7f9c4087dc2b3436ba6314. This confirms comment 69: the outer guardian gate does not close the target pre-exec race and presently introduces setup failures of its own. Keep the revision unpromoted until the target child itself is gated/admitted and the 100x4 matrix is zero-failure.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Review correction for the latest wrapper bytes (check.sh SHA 81b25f63..., structure 330f40aa..., closure 1644ae7a...): although on_exit still contains three sequential trap commands, the EXIT trap now sets FINALIZER_TRANSITION=1 before entering on_exit, and the previously installed common on_signal handler delegates immediately to on_finalizer_signal while that mode is set. That closes the specific old-handler entry window from comment 53 in design terms. Keep the deterministic HUP/INT/TERM entry-window and publication-boundary tests mandatory before promotion; this correction does not affect the independently reproduced target pre-exec race in comment 70.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: The guardian-gate revision proven failing in comment 70 has now entered main in peer commit 7197d77 (alongside diagnostic work): evidence.py c4134941..., structure_gate.sh 330f40aa..., closure_audit.sh 1644ae7a..., with check.sh base from the same commit. This is now a committed main-branch gate regression, not merely an uncommitted experiment. Do not use a lucky pass from this protocol as RUR/fln-8mj close evidence; the target-child pre-exec gate and zero-failure matrix are still required. I will not rewrite the peer commit or modify the active owner paths without handoff.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Fresh clean-tree full-gate attempt against main e596d0b used isolated Cargo target and artifact target/check/fln-8mj-full-20260722T164709Z-4180581. A peer changed scripts/evidence.py at 12:47:10 during pre-envelope setup; the attempt terminated after writing only ubs-inventory.json and vendor-binding.json. There is no run.ndjson, human.log, terminal record, manifest, or bundle because check.sh installs its finalization envelope only after inventory/vendor/hash/host setup. This is honest non-evidence, retained and not deleted. The design/acceptance now explicitly require post-artifact-directory setup failures and source drift to finalize through a typed durable bundle.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Review of the in-progress stopped-exec approach (evidence.py diff at 12:48 EDT): stopping the child before target exec is the right containment layer, but two ordering requirements are still load-bearing. (1) Do not publish/release merely because pidfd binding succeeded: Popen can return and the parent can bind while the helper is still runnable, before it executes SIGSTOP. An early SIGCONT is then lost and the helper later stops forever. Require child-authored gate readiness or a repeated identity-bound /proc state proof of T/t before release. (2) run_supervised currently computes deadline_ns from started_ns before Popen; time spent awaiting/releasing the stopped child would consume execution budget. Record a separate bounded setup/readiness duration and start the execution deadline only after confirmed release. Seed delayed-stop, early-CONT/lost-wakeup, never-stops, and stop-then-dies cases in addition to the 100x4 immediate matrix.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Concrete wiring bug in the current stopped-finalizer diff (wrapper hashes/mtimes 12:50 EDT): process-start-ticks gained --stopped and resume-bound-process correctly rejects a non-T/t process, but all three run_finalizer_command call sites invoke process-start-ticks with --session-leader only. The binder can therefore return before cmd_stopped_exec reaches SIGSTOP; resume then rejects and the wrapper kills a valid finalizer as resume_failed. Pass --stopped at all three binding sites and keep the post-bind T/t recheck in resume. Add a tight immediate-finalizer stress case; do not weaken resume to accept runnable state.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Contract extension from the fln-8mj adversarial audit: pinned nightly-2026-07-13 compiled unannotated unsafe under both deny(unsafe_code) and forbid(unsafe_code) when cap-lints allow was injected (both exit 0; /tmp/fln-cap-lints-audit.QjXNT3). RUR now explicitly owns a sealed, logged compiler environment and hostile-environment matrix in addition to the existing isolated-target requirement. This does not authorize edits while the active evidence owner is stabilizing target pre-exec containment.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Dependency-model correction: fln-evidence-runner-bootstrap-btk extracts the causal-supervision/durable-bundle/setup-envelope/sealed-toolchain prerequisite and is now an explicit dependency of RUR. RUR still depends on fln-8mj and retains every broader verification-registry, CI, archive, Tribunal, and future E2E obligation. This is decomposition, not scope reduction.

title: Gate G5: The Daemon — vscode-lean4 unmodified, PG-4/5/6/L, Verdict PG-9
description:
Convergence gate G5 "The Daemon" (§22.3): Lantern serves vscode-lean4 unmodified across the smoke matrix; PG-4 (attach), PG-5 (determinism), PG-6 (memory), PG-L (interactive latency ladder) met; the machine session API live; Verdict at PG-9; FrankenLean-built plugins load in the Reference (ABI door, outbound — or honestly tiered per platform per the R5 fallback).

This is the gate at which FrankenLean becomes a daily-driver: the editor experience (the ecosystem most-felt surface) is proven at wire fidelity and at interactive latency, the single-daemon shared-heap architecture delivers its promised arithmetic (once-per-daemon import replacing the per-worker multiplication), and the decision-procedure stack stands on owned components end to end.

Evidence bundle: the editor smoke matrix transcripts (open/edit/hover/goals/completion/widgets across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); PG-4/5/6/L measurements with claim states and tail distributions; session-API load results (thousands of concurrent branches); PG-9 corpus agreement + wall numbers; outbound C4 matrix results or the per-platform tier table; 4-hour soak with zero leaks.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G5 is the concrete daily-driver daemon gate. It consumes G4, Lantern wire/topology, immutable snapshots and machine-session API, the replay/protocol/editor smoke rig, Verdict PG-9, and the outbound plugin matrix. Those beads collectively own PG-4/5/6/L, vscode-lean4 compatibility, cancellation/fault recovery, branch-load evidence, and retained transcripts. G5 does not wait for the entire W9 forensics and semantic-analysis catalog. Full W9 remains mandatory at G6, so no causal graph, semantic merge, time-travel, why-trusts, or SQL feature is lost.

title: W2: Inbound plugin door — dlopen Reference-built plugins, revocation-first, receipts
description:
Implement the inbound plugin door (§6.6): Reference-built shared libraries (precompileModules artifacts, user @[extern] libraries) dlopen into FrankenLean, lean_* imports resolved against Marrow exports, initializers run under Golem initialization ordering (§11.5).

Posture: a plugin is native code and is trusted as such; FrankenLean adds PROVENANCE (hash + origin in the build record), the --no-plugins audit mode (plugin-dependent declarations flagged), and revocation-first loading in sound mode (hash + trust-card check BEFORE dlopen, never after). Each load emits an initialization-DAG receipt so cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable rather than opaque loader failures. The panic law holds at every exported boundary: no Rust panic crosses the ABI; wrappers catch, convert only what the epoch contract maps, and never fabricate a successful Lean result after an internal panic (§6.5).

Acceptance: real Reference-built mathlib-adjacent plugins load and run (G2 requirement, inbound half); --no-plugins elaboration flags exactly the dependent declarations; a deliberately crashing plugin isolates with a typed PluginCrashed error and a receipt, not process death in the daemon. Tests: the C4 cross-load matrix inbound lanes per platform; initializer-ordering fixtures; revocation-first denial fixtures (tampered hash never reaches dlopen).

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — W2 owns the inbound loader membrane: generated-symbol resolution, revocation-before-load, trust-card/hash checks, provenance, panic isolation, allocator/RC correctness, --no-plugins dependency marking, and a typed initializer-handoff contract. Full Golem execution and Reference-observable initializer/extension ordering are not claimed until W5 initialization exists; franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe owns that no-mock integration. This bead now also depends on the owned allocator because a real plugin cannot honestly run on object-layout code alone.

title: G0-7: Defeq-tail spike — the twenty nastiest Corpus whnf problems on K2 design
description:
G0-7 Defeq-tail spike (§22.1-7, feeds §10.2/§8.3): collect the twenty nastiest definitional-equality / whnf problems in the Corpus (the pathological tail that dominates elaboration wall time) and time them on the K2 NbE-accelerator design — memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with height comparison, environment-aware closures.

Why: PG-2 (full-corpus elaboration <= 0.5x Reference) is impossible if the defeq tail does not fall; and the cache keying / lazy-delta strategy must be DECIDED before Athanor (W6) freezes against it. This spike turns the central performance bet from hope into measurement. Candidate problems: mathlib instances with deep unfolding chains, Fin/Nat computation-heavy defeq, structure-eta-heavy goals, whnf under binders with transparency flips.

Acceptance: (a) the twenty problems extracted as standalone fixtures with Reference timings captured on the pinned baseline machine; (b) K2 prototype timings within the envelope the PG-2 arithmetic requires (document the model: tail share of total wall x speedup needed); (c) the cache-keying and delta-ordering design ratified or amended in writing; (d) alpha-canonical digest sharing (§7.1b) validated as the cache-key normalization. Tests: the twenty fixtures become a named benchmark suite with per-commit regression tracking (tails gate, not just medians — §19).

title: W9: Snapshots + machine session API — publication generations, O(1) proof-state forks
description:
Implement snapshots + incrementality + the machine session API (§14.3, §14.4): every open document version maps to an immutable DocumentSnapshot (uri, version, content id, project logical root, GrammarEpochs at boundaries, command transactions, publication generation); edits derive new snapshots; superseded requests keep their snapshot but LOSE publication authority — the stale-diagnostics-into-a-new-buffer race is structurally impossible; every outward message rechecks its generation first.

Incrementality: command-granular snapshots extended DOWNWARD — Quill sub-command reparse + Athanor dataflow means an edit inside a proof re-elaborates from the finest enclosing checkpoint (tactic-block granularity where upstream incrementality already reaches; declaration floor everywhere else), against O(1) environment snapshots, cancel-correct under typing bursts (PG-L: edit-ack + stale-cancel <= 25 ms p95; verified first-goal after mid-proof edit <= 100 ms p50). Diagnostics deduplicate by stable identity (typed cause + primary origin), not string equality; code actions carry idempotency keys + preconditions (stale-goal actions rejected with a structured response, never applied optimistically); progressive proof-state negotiation ($/frankenlean/progressiveProofState: ParseReady -> ProvisionalGoals -> VerifiedGoals -> ImpactComplete, provisional never cached as trusted); multi-project sessions bind each root to its epoch capsule.

The machine session API (§14.4): fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, diff environments — O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon; the substrate Envoy sells and the number that embarrasses the 18-735 s/branch literature. Acceptance: PG-L ladder met on the warm corpus; race-freedom verified under lab typing storms; session API forks measured at O(1). Tests: DPOR exploration of edit/cancel/publish interleavings; stale-precondition fixtures; generation-recheck mutation test (a message skipping the recheck must be impossible or caught).

title: W9: Lantern wire + daemon — byte-compatible LSP dialect, one shared import heap
description:
Implement the Lantern wire dialect + daemon topology (§14.1, §14.2): LSP with the $/lean/* extensions — plainGoal, plainTermGoal, the rpc/* session protocol (connect/call/release, reference-holding semantics), waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens with the Reference legend, diagnostics shapes, widget delivery (client executes JS, wire-compatible pass-through) — BYTE-compatible so vscode-lean4 and every other client connects to lean --server and cannot tell.

Topology: the watchdog/worker PROCESS model is not replicated — only its observable behavior is. One daemon, all open files, all projects on a toolchain epoch, one shared immutable import heap (§7.5): the ~60 s x N-workers x GBs x N multiplication becomes once-per-daemon (PG-4: warm attach <= 2 s p50; PG-6: steady RSS serving >= 8 open mathlib files <= 1.3x ONE Reference worker). Per-file fault isolation becomes per-session region isolation: a crashing elaboration cancels an asupersync region, not a process; the supervisor restarts the session with the same client-visible semantics as a worker restart (BN-04). Transport is asupersync plumbing; zero protocol code written here.

Acceptance: vscode-lean4 connects unmodified and passes the smoke matrix basics; recorded-session replays (C5) diff clean at the wire with registered nondeterminism classes normalized; PG-4/PG-6 trajectory measured. Tests: wire-level golden transcripts; rpc session-lifetime fixtures (reference holding/release); region-crash isolation drills (one session dies, others unaffected, client sees worker-restart semantics).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this W9 wire bead explicitly owns OQ-7. Freeze widget fidelity tiers outside VS Code, the wire-compatible pass-through baseline, the capability and isolation boundary for any franken_node host, supported widget fallback behavior, version negotiation, and the evidence threshold for graduating the exploratory host. fln-in1 consumes this decision and may not silently reinterpret widget semantics.

Dependency topology: Lantern consumes the G4-complete language/toolchain, Ledger records/CAS, FL-INV-05 invalidation, real Lake project state, compacted shared regions, scheduler, and typed diagnostics. It does not require W8 cache federation, epoch bridges, or distributed elaboration to prove the single-daemon user experience. Those full-W8 features remain G6 blockers. This bead must use the same core records and roots that full W8 later federates; no daemon-only cache or project truth is allowed.

title: W5: native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts, trust-surface provenance
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts (§11.6): support native_decide with the Reference semantics and the Reference caveat stated LOUDER (it extends trust to Golem/Iron), plus the first stage of the honest repair: every reflection-class evaluation emits a replay receipt — function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts — recorded against term identities, replayable by Crucible at a stated cost multiple. Certified profiles verify always; standard samples by policy.

Trust bookkeeping shipped with it: provenance marks every native_decide-dependent theorem; fln audit --tcb lists a project trust surface (axioms, native_decide sites, plugins, facade classes); fln why-trusts <decl> walks the transitive trust cone (§15.6 seam — the Palimpsest query lands in W9, but the provenance emission lands here). Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay as certified-path default, G6) and Tier 3 (verified evaluator, research) are explicitly OUT of this bead — each tier stands alone and the trust ledger records which tier each build achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope.

Acceptance: receipts emitted for the reflection corpus; Crucible replay verifies them at the stated cost multiple; sampling policy configurable and journaled; the trust ledger row per build records the tier. Tests: receipt round-trip fixtures; a tampered-receipt fixture must fail verification; provenance-marking completeness on a corpus with known native_decide sites.

title: W10/W11: WASM Judge packaging contract — resolve OQ-12 before browser integration
description:
Resolve plan OQ-12 in its named W10 architecture phase before the W11 terminal/browser integration freezes packaging. Define the WASM Judge release boundary: kernel plus independent checker core, capsule decoder and receipt/transparency verification, and the browser capsule experience. The decision may split these into integrity-bound artifacts, but it may not delete the plan browser capability: users must still be able to verify a sealed capsule in a stock browser, inspect declaration/dependency results, and obtain a verifiable receipt without a network service.

Freeze artifact composition and TCB accounting; size, startup, verification-latency, and memory budgets; supported browser/WASM feature matrix; deterministic native-versus-WASM vectors; offline loading and content integrity; CSP and JS-host boundaries; capsule streaming and adversarial resource limits; version negotiation; accessibility and failure rendering; and whether the browser shell is embedded, separately signed, or both. Full browser elaboration remains tier-2-if-ever and must not inflate the Judge tier-1 claim.

## Acceptance Criteria

A versioned packaging contract and decision-log row resolve both OQ-12 alternatives without losing the capsule-browser deliverable. Every shipped artifact has an explicit hash/signature relationship, version/epoch compatibility rule, TCB inclusion statement, resource envelope, update/rollback story, and offline verification procedure. Measured prototypes on the declared browser matrix establish honest size/startup/verification/memory baselines using a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule and the same vectors as native K1 plus fln-checker.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover package manifests, version negotiation, content-integrity binding, capsule streaming, unknown versions, truncated/tampered inputs, resource exhaustion, and native/WASM vector identity. A real no-mock browser E2E script runs online-disabled in each supported engine, verifies a good capsule, rejects byte flips and mismatched UI/core assets, survives refresh/retry without false success, and emits detailed human plus bounded schema-versioned NDJSON logs with browser/platform/epoch/build hashes, exact scenario and step ids, input/output hashes, timings/resources, expected versus actual, first divergence, terminal status, cleanup, and artifact references. A skipped browser or mock-only run cannot satisfy support or performance claims.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead own acceptance): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise every package, integrity, version, streaming, resource, determinism, accessibility, and failure-atomicity rule. Real no-mock browser E2E coverage must include good, tampered, mismatched-asset, resource-exhaustion, offline, refresh, and recovery paths on the declared support matrix. Emit concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step, bead/claim/invariant/gate, exact redacted invocation and origin, epoch/mode/profile/browser/platform/build hashes, input and artifact hashes, expected versus actual, monotonic timings, resources/budgets, terminal status, console/stdout/stderr references, first divergence, cleanup and asserted final state. Archive the contract, rejected alternatives, fixtures, raw measurements, logs, and reproducible commands; skips are typed limitations and cannot satisfy the skipped support or performance claim.

title: W11: Bloodhound — verified search, premise retrieval, proof repair, counterfactuals
description:
Implement Bloodhound (fln-hound, §16.1): the candidate-production system under one law — every candidate is untrusted text/terms until Athanor elaborates and Crucible accepts it (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs; determinism class D3, never touching the trust story).

Scope: in-toolchain hybrid search (frankensearch two-tier: lexical + semantic, bundled embedder, no network/Python/service) over statements, names, docstrings, simp/instance membership, indexed INCREMENTALLY at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator verified by actual elaboration; Loogle-style structural patterns on a type-shape fingerprint lane; CandidateCards (source/term, provenance + retrieval channels, required imports/instances, rank components, elaboration outcome, kernel outcome, fragility signals, replay identity) in three grades — initial (low-latency lexical/type-head/local-context), refined (structural unification, graph ranking over Palimpsest cones, prior-use paths, optional learned rerank), verified (kernel-accepted under the exact snapshot); a failed refinement never withdraws a valid initial result.

Proof repair (the migration autopilot engine): diff old/new environment + extension graphs, find the earliest failed constraint cell, re-elaborate with holes around the blame slice, search replacements (renames, binder-order shifts, bridge lemmas, instance-priority shifts, simp-set drift, termination adjustments via TerminationCards, import moves), validate each minimal patch through the FULL pipeline, rank by semantic change size + fragility delta, emit a patch transaction with rollback. Counterfactual analysis (replay under altered policy on branch-local snapshots) and minimization (declared objective vectors, trade-offs shown). Learned components (frankentorch tier): local-first, identity on every card, candidates-only, removal leaves a complete deterministic baseline.

Acceptance: search quality measured on a query corpus; repair success rate measured on a broken-proof corpus derived from real mathlib migrations; every repair kernel-validated. Tests: index incrementality (commit -> queryable within the latency budget); candidate-grade monotonicity fixtures; repair rollback drills; learned-tier removal leaves baseline green.

title: W4: Quill — parser, Syntax/SourceInfo, hygiene, macro transactions
description:
Workstream W4 "Quill" (§9): the parser and macro engine — the reimplementation graveyard, visited early with flowers (spike G0-4).

Scope: token tables + Pratt-precedence engine with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract (parsing interleaves with elaboration; commands install syntax/macros visible immediately; precedence interactions match). Internals: lossless rope + persistent green tree with red views; origin sets per node projected to the single-primary-span convention at the wire; GrammarEpoch content-hashing of the active grammar state with memoization keyed on (position, category, precedence, epoch); typed parser-registration effect summaries (an OpaqueParserEffect forces the conservative suffix barrier). Hygiene: macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them; generated identifiers derive from stable expansion paths, never thread timing; macro expansion as a memoizable transaction with declared capability reads (§9.2). Sound-mode improvements (§9.3): specified error recovery (recovery may add diagnostics, never change acceptance; RecoveredSyntax law), sub-command incremental relex/reparse, provenance into Palimpsest.

Exit: hygiene fidelity rig green on the macro-heavy corpus; syntax trees, positions, and hygiene observables byte-agree with the Reference across C0-C2 fixtures.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.

This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: G0-10: Dependency-closure audit + authoritative SUITE.lock
description:
G0-10 Dependency-closure audit + SUITE.lock (§22.1-10, D1, §2.10): generate the FULL transitive dependency closure of the workspace (runtime, build, dev, fuzz tracked under separate policies), allowlist every package (name/version/source/checksum, features, license, build-script and proc-macro status, native-link status, unsafe-audit status, reason, owner, upgrade policy), commit SUITE.lock (exact commits/checksums for every FrankenSuite repo, the rustc nightly, target-feature sets, the Reference pin v4.32.0 with its commit hash, and the Corpus pin — one ceremony), and stand up the extraction scripts for the ABI/olean/extern contracts in CI.

The doctrine being enforced: authoritative fln-* crates introduce NO new unreviewed direct runtime dependencies; the audit is an owned fln-conformance check; CI fails on any unlisted package; no serde, no tokio, no rocksdb, no LLVM, no cranelift, no gmp-sys, no external SAT solver. Also prices risk R16 (suite-dependency drift — an unapproved transitive dep inside a reused suite crate is a finding, not a shrug).

Acceptance: (a) the closure inventory generated mechanically and checked in; (b) allowlist review complete with zero unlisted packages and zero policy violations; (c) SUITE.lock committed and CI builds ONLY from the lock; (d) contract-extraction jobs wired into CI failing on drift among pin, contract docs, and generated constants modules. Tests: the audit runs on every CI build forever; an allowlist diff requires an explicit review artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — eliminate duplicated ownership with franken_lean-53v. This bead owns the complete governed dependency closure, allowlists, authoritative SUITE.lock ceremony, lock-only build enforcement, and seeded closure-policy failures. The mechanical ABI/OLEAN/extern/builtin extraction scripts and their drift CI are owned by franken_lean-53v; G0 closes only with both evidence bundles. Do not implement a second extraction path here.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — every declared closure-policy field is semantic, not decorative. Preserve each SUITE.lock suite path and crate→repo mapping in the parsed model; every manifest suite dependency must canonicalize to that exact pinned repo path, and every pinned crate/repo/path must agree bidirectionally with WORKSPACE_GRAPH and the closure allowlist. Validate vocabulary and observable truth for source, checksum, features, license, build-script, proc-macro, native-link, unsafe-audit, policy, owner, upgrade, and reason; a present-but-ignored field does not satisfy D1. The Cargo.lock parser requires exactly top-level version=4, only the supported package keys/array grammar, terminated dependency arrays, and no duplicate package identity/source/checksum keys. Missing, stale, conflicting, wrong-path, malformed, symlinked, or unverifiable rows fail closed with stable codes and named recovery fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: Generated closure inventory and reviewed allowlist are bijective over every Cargo.lock package and every workspace/suite package; source/version/checksum/features plus build-script/proc-macro/native-link/unsafe policy are verified against manifests, lock data, and the real pinned suite trees. Every suite manifest path resolves canonically to the package→repo→SUITE.lock path, no path alias or symlink escape is accepted, and CI uses `--locked` plus the exact SUITE.lock toolchain/target configuration. Unit and shared no-mock closure E2E lanes seed and recover from: wrong suite path, unlisted/stale package, registry/git source, malformed or non-v4 lock, unterminated/unknown arrays, duplicate keys/rows, invalid policy vocabulary, build-script/proc-macro/native-link mismatch, checksum/provenance drift, absent suite checkout, commit mismatch, and allowlist diff without a review artifact.
