comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Fresh structural audit finding before this bead can close: the current SUITE.lock parser validates path= syntax but discards the path, and checks.rs accepts any path-valued dependency whose package name appears as suite-dep. Thus `asupersync = { path = "/tmp/fake" }` can satisfy FLN-STRUCT-010 instead of being bound to SUITE.lock path=/dp/asupersync. Also parse_allowlist presently requires checksum/license/proc-macro/native-link/unsafe-audit/owner/upgrade fields syntactically but discards most values; the audit compares only name/version/source class and build-script absence. Extend the bead acceptance/tests so the exact manifest canonical path must match package -> repo -> SUITE.lock path, suite/workspace source classes are cross-checked both ways, and every declared policy field has validated vocabulary plus mechanically verified semantics where observable. Seed wrong suite path, invalid booleans/audit policy, checksum/provenance drift, proc-macro/native-link drift, and recovery cases. Do not close on field presence alone.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Additional fail-closed parser cases for the xwf work: parse_cargo_lock currently accepts a missing/non-v4 top-level lock version, treats any `key = [` as an ignorable array instead of requiring `dependencies`, and does not error on an unterminated array at EOF. Seed each malformed shape and require FLN-STRUCT-016 rather than relying on a later Cargo invocation. Also reject duplicate package name/version/source/checksum keys rather than last-write-wins.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Fresh preflight review of tools/structure-guard/src/lockfile.rs: parse_cargo_lock is not yet fail-closed for its constrained v4 grammar. It accepts duplicate name/version/source/checksum keys (last value wins), an unquoted source/checksum as None, any top-level version value/duplicates, and EOF while a multiline array remains open; any key with value [ enters the array-skip path. Seed each malformed shape and track seen keys/lock version/array owner plus mandatory close. Also, audit verifies build-script=no against build.rs only when source=workspace; source=suite rows are trusted without inspecting the pinned suite package path. comments.text: Additional schema-contract gap: CLOSURE_ALLOWLIST.txt says CI fails checksum mismatch and types proc-macro/native-link/unsafe-audit, but AllowRow stores/enforces only name/version/source/build-script/policy. checksum, license, proc-macro, native-link, unsafe-audit, owner, and upgrade merely have to be present and may contain arbitrary or empty values; checksum is never compared to LockPackage. title: W8: Ledger records + CAS — demand nodes, five planes, retention tiers
description:
Implement Ledger records + the CAS store (§13.1): every declaration commit produces a record — decl_hash = H(name, elaborated statement, elaborated value/body class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, toolchain epoch, mode) — stored content-addressed (frankensqlite metadata + CAS blobs) with dependency edges as first-class rows. The environment at any point is a Merkle frontier keyed by logical roots; a module is a view; a build is a query.

The record generalizes to a demand node — kind, implementation version, epoch, semantic inputs, declared reads/effects (the §10.6 vocabulary), capability inputs, output schema, determinism class D0-D4 — spanning five interlocking planes with separate retention: source, language, trust (kernel/checker receipts), build (facets, codegen, links), service (LSP products, indexes). An LSP hover can be evicted without touching the checked declaration beneath it; retention tiers T0 (trusted roots, never sole-copy-evicted) -> T4 (observational, sampled) with eviction weighing recomputation cost, fan-out, and pressure.

Acceptance: record creation + retrieval on real elaboration output; plane separation demonstrated (service-plane eviction leaves trust plane intact); logical-root stability across hosts. Tests: hash-input completeness property tests (any semantic input change changes the hash; any excluded input does not); retention-tier eviction policy fixtures; frankensqlite crash-recovery drills on the metadata store (WAL semantics honest under kill -9).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. design: FL-INV-05 STORAGE BOUNDARY — CAS presence, signature, receipt presence, or matching key yields only an untrusted candidate, never a semantically admitted cache hit. Record schemas bind every semantic/capability/determinism input and separate byte identity from logical identity; publication is atomic and generation-bound. Only the validated reuse path owned by franken_lean-5h8 may promote a candidate, and its proof/recompute evidence is linked in the record. Seeded omissions of each hash-input class and stale-generation publication must be killed.

title: G0-1: ABI resurrection spike — parse a real mathlib .olean via extracted contracts
description:
G0-1 ABI resurrection spike (§22.1-1, feeds §6/§7.2): parse a REAL mathlib .olean at the pin with a prototype Marrow/Grimoire reader — header (magic, version, base address), region relocation, ModuleData traversal — walk every constant and every environment-extension entry, and validate object-graph integrity against the extracted contract tables (not against hand-written constants!).

Why this is first: the entire drop-in promise rests on the object model and the region format being EXACTLY right, and on the extraction methodology (checked-in scripts parsing the pinned lean.h and runtime layout sources -> generated Rust constants module) being viable before anything depends on it. A layout truth discovered late invalidates every downstream interface.

Acceptance: (a) a chosen set of real mathlib oleans (include at least one with heavy environment-extension payloads, e.g. simp-set extensions) fully traversed with zero integrity faults; (b) every field decoded is traceable to a generated contract row with provenance; (c) unknown/opaque extension payloads preserved losslessly and flagged, never guessed; (d) a written findings memo enumerating every layout subtlety discovered (tag values, scalar packing corner cases, relocation quirks) folded into OLEAN_CONTRACT.md / ABI_CONTRACT.md. Tests: fixture corpus checked into the Tribunal (C3 family seed) with a round-trip traversal harness and hostile-input fuzz smoke (bounds, tags, reachability) under resource budgets.

title: W2: fln-libm — owned deterministic transcendentals, bit-locked across platforms
description:
Implement fln-libm (§6.8): owned, fixed-implementation Float transcendentals — sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pow, atan2, hypot (and the rest of the family the extern census enumerates) — with documented accuracy (<= 1 ulp target, per-function error tables published) and IDENTICAL BITS on every certified target, locked by cross-platform vectors in CI. No platform libm is linked, period (D21).

Why: Float arithmetic is hardware IEEE-754 binary64 (portable), but upstream defers transcendentals to platform libm, so compiled evaluation, #eval, and native_decide-class computation drift across glibc/macOS/Windows in the last ulps — the Reference cannot promise ITSELF cross-platform bit-stability (§1.6b). Kernel judgments never consume floats (Float ops are opaque to the kernel), so the soundness surface is untouched; the evaluation surface gains bit-stability recorded as a Behavior Note (platforms disagree with each other; we pick one correct answer and keep it everywhere).

Acceptance: cross-platform vector suite green on the certified matrix (identical bits linux-x86-64 / linux-aarch64 / macos-aarch64); error tables generated and published per function; a CI symbol-scan proves no libm import in any release binary. Tests: ulp-error measurement harness against high-precision references; boundary cases (subnormals, infinities, NaN payloads, huge args for range reduction); Float printing/parsing round-trip fixtures matching the Reference conventions.

title: G0-2: Independent-check spike — prototype kernel vs lean4checker on real modules
description:
G0-2 Independent-check spike (§22.1-2, feeds §8): a prototype Crucible checks a nontrivial upstream module from its olean — statements AND proofs — with verdicts diffed against lean4checker on the same input.

This is where the kernel judgment inventory (§1.2, Appendix A) meets reality: typing rules for every constructor, universe normalization with the imax collapse, defeq (beta, eta for functions and structures, delta by height, zeta, iota, proof irrelevance, Quot computation, Nat/String literal rules), inductive validation (strict positivity, nested-to-mutual, K-like elimination), structure eta, environment rules. The spike does not need speed (K2 comes later) — it needs to prove the rules are correctly understood and that the differential harness against a foreign checker is practical.

Acceptance: (a) chosen module set (progressively: a small Init module, a Std module, one mathlib file with defeq-heavy content) checks with verdicts identical to lean4checker; (b) any disagreement triaged to root cause — each becomes a fixture and, if upstream-vs-theory, is pre-classified under the §8.2 soundness-beats-bug-parity law; (c) fuel/heartbeat hooks stubbed but counted, proving the accounting seam exists. Tests: the diff harness itself lands in fln-conformance as the seed of the standing kernel differential rig (§8.7) and runs in CI on the fixture set from this spike onward.

title: FrankenLean program root: drop-in native-Rust Lean 4 toolchain (plan §0)
description:
Program root for FrankenLean (franken_lean, crate prefix fln-): a ground-up, native-Rust reimplementation of the ENTIRE Lean 4 toolchain — parser, macro engine, elaborator, unifier, instance engine, tactic framework, simp + decision procedures, trusted kernel, compiler, VM, runtime/ABI twin, module codec, build fabric, and language server — that is a drop-in replacement at six binary surfaces (source language, .olean read AND write, lean_object C ABI, .ilean/artifact chain, the Lean.* Meta API via the Native Mirror, LSP + $/lean/* + CLI).

Constitutional laws (never violate): the Oracle-Only Law D8 (upstream implementation code NEVER executes as a component; the Reference lives only inside the Tribunal as differential oracle, fixture/census mine, and Init/Std source input); closed dependency universe (std + pinned nightly + FrankenSuite: asupersync, frankensqlite, franken_networkx, frankensearch, frankentui, franken_markdown, fastmcp_rust, atp); zero mandatory external tools (cc and git optional-only per D2); forbid(unsafe) outside fln-unsafe-abi/-region/-jit; kernel covenant <= 12 KLOC with FL-INV-02; determinism as invariant FL-INV-01; claims have types (D7, L0-L4 / R0-R5 evidence axes).

Success = R4 (drop-in epoch replacement) for the pinned epoch (Reference leanprover/lean4 v4.32.0; Corpus mathlib4 at compatible commit, both pinned in SUITE.lock) on the declared platform matrix, via gates G0..G6 (§22). The single source of truth is COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_FOR_THE_DESIGN_OF_FRANKEN_LEAN.md; this bead tree is its executable projection. Sequencing bends to dependency; ambition does not bend to sequencing.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W3: Crucible K1 — the certified small-step evaluator under the covenant
description:
Implement Crucible engine K1, the certified evaluator (§8.3): a direct, boring, small-step-flavored implementation of the full judgment inventory, optimized for AUDITABILITY — the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon. K1 is the definitional reference for K2, the engine forced by --kernel k1 for audit builds, and the engine compiled into the WASM Judge.

Constraints (the covenant, §8.1): fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC total across both engines, forbid(unsafe_code), dependencies only fln-bignum + Grimoire read-only NativeHeap term views + core/alloc; zero I/O, zero threads, zero global mutable state, zero plugin hooks; one exported authority: check(Environment, Declaration) -> Verdict. The covenant is CI-enforced: line count + dependency-graph walk + TCB inventory generated from the actual graph every run; growth requires amending the plan first. Typestate admission (§8.2b) and the typed resource algebra (§8.2c) are part of this bead: budgets from the caller Cx (node count, depth, expanded weight, reduction steps, allocation), cooperative cancellation checkpoints, no recursive host traversal over attacker-controlled depth, exhaustion = typed Inconclusive with a consumption profile.

Acceptance: G0-2 module set re-checked by production K1 with verdicts identical to lean4checker; every KERNEL_CONTRACT rule maps to named code + fixtures; DAG-bomb and deep-term fixtures produce Inconclusive under budget. Tests: rule-per-rule C0 micro-fixtures; generated well-typed term storms (agreement with the Reference kernel); mutation campaign — a skipped positivity check or inverted universe condition must be killed by a named test (§18.2).

design: FL-INV-02 IMPLEMENTATION OWNER — K1 implements the sole authority transition. Successful checking yields an opaque checked result whose constructors and authority-bearing fields are private to fln-kernel and are never deserializable; all environment publication paths require that live result or an equivalent kernel-owned typestate transition. Rejections and FL-INV-07 Inconclusive/InternalFault values cannot inhabit the accepted state. CI walks public APIs and dependency edges; compile-fail tests reject direct construction, cache/receipt replay as authority, raw environment insertion, and unsafe-crate laundering. A seeded bypass mutation must fail the standing admission E2E before this bead closes.

1) kr306_proof_irrelevance_does_not_leak_to_type: T:Sort 1 (a Type, not a Prop), a,b:T distinct -> must be NotDefEq. Kills any is_prop that admits Sort 1 (proof irrelevance leaking into Type = equating distinct data). Verified: mutating is_prop to also accept Sort 1 makes this test FAIL (Accepted), and NO pre-existing test caught it (kr306_in_prop uses a Prop; distinct_axioms operates at the Sort 1 level where is_prop(Sort 1) infers Sort 2).
2) kr306_proof_irrelevance_requires_defeq_propositions: hp:p, hq:q with p,q distinct Props -> must be NotDefEq. Kills dropping the type-defeq half of proof_irrel_eq (which would equate every proof of every prop). Verified: replacing the final is_def_eq(t_type,s_type) with Ok(true) makes this test FAIL; kr306_in_prop cannot catch it (shared prop).

Both mutation-kills demonstrated live and reverted. Also cleared a UBS critical (a pre-existing panic! in the fl_inv_07 test) by rewriting to assert!(matches!(...)) — behavior-preserving. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Follow-up (Claude Fable session): the projection path (KR-112 infer_proj) was entirely untested — including the soundness-critical Prop-leak guard (KR-901). Added test coverage to tests/k1_judgments.rs (test-only, additive):
- kr112_projection_infers_the_field_type: a Type-valued structure S:Sort 1 with mk:D→D→S; proj S 0/1 s both infer to D, and asserting the wrong field type yields DefinitionTypeMismatch. First test of the projection telescope walk.
- kr901_projection_cannot_leak_data_out_of_prop: a Prop-valued structure Pstruct:Prop with a DATA field (pmk:D→Pstruct, D:Sort 1) — proj Pstruct 0 hp must be rejected (InvalidProjection), else the kernel extracts a datum from a proof, defeating proof irrelevance = UNSOUND. Plus a discriminating control (an all-Prop QBox:Prop with a Prop field projects fine). title: W6: Monadic tower + ElabTxn — explicit transactions, audited rollback, mvar graph
description:
Implement the monadic tower + ElabTxn (§10.1): the Core/Meta/TermElab/Tactic state SEMANTICS (metavariable contexts with delayed assignments, local contexts, backtracking discipline, option threading, message logs, InfoTree recording) as NativeHeap structures, with Mirror-facade views projected into Marrow objects exactly where user metaprograms touch them — the toolchain pays the ABI shape only at the seam it actually crosses (the Oracle-Only Law dividend, §6.1b).

Elaboration state is EXPLICIT, not ambient: each command runs in an ElabTxn (base logical root, local context, metavariable store, universe store, constraint queue, extension overlay, message buffer, InfoTree builder, capability context, decision ledger, deterministic seed, budget); nested elaboration uses child transactions with exactly five outcomes (commit all; commit diagnostics/info only; roll back completely; expose candidate + unresolved obligations; fork into deterministic alternatives). Rollback is AUDITED: Tribunal leak tests prove no failed alternative leaves assigned metavariables, registered declarations, or messages behind — the bug class upstream ambient monads make chronic is made structural here. Metavariables form an explicit dependency graph (type, context, kind, origin, assignment state + justification, delayed constraints) with targeted wake-up: assigning one reschedules only constraints that actually read it.

Acceptance: tower observable semantics verified against C1/C2 elaboration fixtures; leak tests green across the five outcomes; wake-up precision measured (no spurious rescheduling on the fixture set). Tests: transaction-outcome property tests; a planted leak mutant (child txn leaves an assignment behind) must be caught; InfoTree shape differentials vs the oracle.

title: W8: Watch mode + distributed elaboration — local re-verification, no distributed trust
description:
Implement watch mode + distributed elaboration (§13.7): lake build --watch (filesystem events -> cone recompute -> live frankentui progress with dependency-cone visualization) and distributed elaboration — remote workers pulling Ledger tasks over asupersync RPC, results RE-VERIFIED by local Crucible on arrival: distribution without distributed trust (workers may lie; results validate locally; disagreement blocks and never votes — the same trust model Envoy remote check service reuses, §16.3).

Acceptance: watch mode recomputes exactly the invalidation-matrix cone on real edit streams with the frankentui surface live; distributed builds produce bit-identical Ledger states to local builds (FL-INV-01 extends across the wire); a lying-worker fixture (corrupted result) is caught by local re-verification and quarantined. title: W5: FLBC interpreter — ABI-valued registers, inline caches, PG-7 floor
description:
Implement the FLBC interpreter (fln-vm, §11.2): threaded dispatch, inline caches on constructor tags and closure arities, direct-call fast paths for the intrinsic table, allocation through Marrow (heartbeats count identically to native paths — fuel parity crosses the VM boundary), and the Reference semantics for panics, partial non-termination, stack depth, and checkSystem interruption polls.

The design masterstroke to preserve at all costs: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so calls cross between interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror native facade with ZERO conversion — one calling convention for the whole membrane (validated by spike G0-3). Performance floor PG-7: >= 3x the Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus — deliberately conservative because the interpreter is the default vehicle for the long tail of user tactics (the interpreter tax is weakness census #5).

Acceptance: #eval/IO/Task parity corpus green (G2 requirement); PG-7 met on the micro-corpus against the W1 baseline; interruption polling honors the checkSystem contract (latency fixtures). Tests: opcode-level unit tests; VM opcode fuzzing under budgets (§18.2); inline-cache correctness under polymorphic call sites (cache hit == cache miss result); cross-boundary call matrix (interpreted <-> intrinsic <-> plugin) with RC-balance assertions.

title: fln-env: define the canonical module-provenance schema and graph identity
description:
Child 1 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3. Define the immutable identity-only schema for exact module contributions: imported declaration names, extra generated declaration names, and ordered extension-entry contributions identified by stable ranges and entry identities without copying payloads. Represent understood, opaque, partial, and missing completeness explicitly. Register a dedicated versioned fln-hash domain and canonical encoding whose root covers module and epoch identity, artifact evidence, exact ordered direct-import rows including duplicates and flags, every contribution identity, and completeness grade. The root is an explicit ModuleGraph/Provenance identity for future Ledger demand keys and receipts; prove that it never changes Environment logical_root and is not operational metadata.

Identity-only is deliberate and must be explicit in the public contract. Declaration ConstantInfo values and raw extension payload bytes remain Arc-backed in the Environment/import transaction rather than being duplicated here. The artifact digest plus separate Environment logical root bind semantic content; the schema binds ownership/order/range/completeness. The atomic-apply child owns the one-to-one actual-value transaction preflight, equal-digest unequal-value collision handling, and semantic logical-root transition. This schema must neither imply that names/digests are sufficient to apply a module nor grow a second copy of semantic payload state.

Specify and freeze field and enum tags, positional layout, ordering, duplicate policy, range identity, schema evolution, resource accounting, malformed-input refusals, and canonical set versus ordered-sequence semantics before downstream APIs freeze. Tests include field-by-field golden encodings, domain-registry uniqueness and completeness, canonical insertion-order independence, order-sensitive rows/contribution sequences/ranges, exact logical-root noninterference, exact boundary/resource tables including cumulative decoder accounting before allocation, all enum-tag round trips and unknown refusals, and named mutants that omit an extra declaration, extension contributor, completeness grade, artifact binding, direct-row field, graph identity field, or entry identity. Generated schedule matrices, storage-sharing checks, noncanonical/future/truncated/trailing/unknown inputs, semantic malformed tables, opaque and partial grades, and exact duplicate rows are mandatory.

Run fln-hash and fln-env tests, domain-registry checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and applicable parent gates. No disconnected provenance truth, host data, payload flattening, name/digest-only application claim, implicit versioning, unbounded decode allocation, or hidden logical-root change.
acceptance_criteria: All identity schema, encoding/tag, domain, resource, noninterference, golden, ordering/metamorphic, sharing, malformed, and named mutation requirements above are implemented and green. The contract must explicitly preserve the identity-only versus Arc-backed application-payload boundary and defer neither a field, completeness class, resource boundary, cumulative decoder case, nor negative input. This child closes on exhaustive unit/property/mutation evidence; the shared real decoded-fixture no-mock E2E is explicitly owned by fln-amv.9.4 and must exercise this schema before the parent can close. Closure requires fln-hash and fln-env tests, domain-registry checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS with zero applicable critical findings, and predecessor gates. No name/digest-only application claim, payload duplication, or independent E2E-complete claim satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: apply module contributions atomically with bidirectional provenance indexes
description:
Child 2 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3, after franken_lean-module-provenance-schema-cxn. Implement one preflighted immutable transaction that registers a module record, adds every decoded declaration and extra generated declaration, replays each extension contribution in exact order, and updates module-to-contribution plus contribution-to-module indexes together or publishes nothing.

The canonical provenance manifest is intentionally identity-only, so the apply API must take a separate payload-bearing ModuleApplyTransaction envelope. The envelope carries the validated manifest root and module contribution record, actual Arc-backed ConstantInfo values for declarations and extra declarations, and Arc-backed extension payload entries keyed by descriptor and ordinal. Preflight must derive and compare every declaration name, canonical declaration identity, extension payload digest, ordinal, range, descriptor, module, artifact, epoch, and completeness field one-to-one against the manifest. Missing, extra, reordered, or mismatched payloads are typed refusals. Digest equality never substitutes for byte/value equality: an equal-digest unequal-value case is an explicit identity-collision conflict. Provenance indexes retain identities and Arcs, never flattened payload copies.

Publish one immutable committed state containing Environment, module graph/provenance manifest, and both indexes; do not maintain independently mutable truths. Required index keys include ModuleId to exact contribution record, declaration Name to owner ModuleId/class/ordinal, and extension Name plus journal ordinal/range to contributor ModuleId and entry identity. Stable extension ranges bind descriptor, starting journal identity and length, ordered entry identities, contributor module, artifact, epoch, and completeness. Unknown extension registration, descriptor mismatch, noncontiguous or stale range, and base/checkpoint drift are typed before publication. Opaque payloads remain byte-exact and graded, not decoded or normalized.

Return field-specific outcomes for missing direct targets, partial decoded modules, exact idempotence, conflicting module records, duplicate or conflicting declaration ownership, unknown or mismatched extension descriptors, replay range drift, stale graph/provenance/logical/base/checkpoint identity, identity collision, and every entry/byte/name/index resource limit. Missing targets and partial decode produce an explicit AppliedIncomplete grade that blocks complete-cache/fine-invalidation claims; they are never rendered or cached as rejection. Exact idempotent reapplication is a no-op only when every provenance field and every actual declaration/payload value is equal. Any collision or mismatch is a conflict, never last-writer-wins.

Logical-root behavior is explicit: graph, artifact, ownership, and provenance metadata alone cannot alter Environment logical_root; publishing actual declarations and extension deltas changes logical_root exactly through the existing Environment rules. The result returns and verifies before/after logical and provenance roots so neither topology leakage nor a dropped semantic contribution can hide. Preserve O(1) snapshots and bounded path copying; report allocation/path-copy work facts.

Tests must compare forward and reverse indexes and the combined committed state against independent maps over generated batches; prove all-or-nothing behavior for every early and late failure; cover complete and AppliedIncomplete success, exact idempotence, every conflict field, unknown descriptors, digest-collision fixtures, replay bytes/order/ranges, empty payloads, and opaque/understood payloads; record pointer/allocation sharing; run insertion and thread-order metamorphic matrices; and kill mutants that drop a declaration, extra declaration, extension row, reverse row, payload-binding check, root precondition, or preflight check. Unit evidence is schema-versioned and line-oriented with transaction/root ids, counts, ranges, limits/usage, grade, expected/actual outcome, publication flag, and final state. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock E2E remains solely in fln-amv.9.4.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain checks, targeted and parent fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates. No partial publication, name-or-digest-only payload binding, last-writer-wins ownership, flattened payload copy, disconnected provenance store, hidden logical-root change, or falsely complete partial state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The payload-bearing transaction envelope must bind actual Arc-backed declaration and extension values one-to-one to the validated canonical manifest before atomically publishing Environment, graph/provenance state, and both index directions. Every success grade, exact-idempotence, identity-collision, conflict, incomplete, stale-root, replay-range, descriptor, ownership, payload-binding, logical-root transition, and resource case above is mandatory. Generated independent models, early/late all-or-nothing tables, schedule metamorphics, sharing/allocation evidence, named mutants, detailed versioned unit logs, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Real no-mock fixtures remain in fln-amv.9.4. Partial publication, identity-only application, or deferred indexes fail closure.
acceptance_criteria: The payload-bearing transaction envelope must bind actual Arc-backed declaration and extension values one-to-one to the validated canonical manifest before atomically publishing Environment, graph/provenance state, and both index directions. Every success grade, exact-idempotence, identity-collision, conflict, incomplete, stale-root, replay-range, descriptor, ownership, payload-binding, logical-root transition, and resource case above is mandatory. Every negative, cancellation, or Inconclusive case must prove zero publication and be followed by a clean successful recovery transaction whose values, indexes, logical root, and provenance root are verified. Generated independent models, early/late all-or-nothing tables, schedule metamorphics, sharing/allocation evidence, named mutants, detailed versioned unit logs, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Real no-mock fixtures remain in fln-amv.9.4. Partial publication, identity-only application, deferred indexes, or an unrecoverable failed transaction fails closure.

title: fln-env: compute deterministic module invalidation cones with provenance witnesses
description:
Child 3 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3, after the atomic committed state and bidirectional indexes. Implement bounded deterministic invalidation queries for five distinct change classes: artifact identity, direct-import topology including every import flag and missing-target appearance, declaration contribution identity/content, extension contribution descriptor/range/payload identity, and opaque or partial completeness boundary.

Queries operate only on the single validated committed state from the atomic-apply bead. Each request carries the expected provenance root and, where semantic state matters, expected logical root; stale roots are refused before traversal. Change seeds are resolved through exact reverse indexes rather than name guesses. Artifact and topology changes seed the owning module; declaration changes seed the exact declaration owner; extension changes seed the exact descriptor/ordinal contributor; a missing target becoming available seeds every exact importing row. Effective-import reverse facts may be reused only where their flag/state phase is semantically exact. Until declaration-level causal edges exist, propagation across module imports is conservatively module-granular and must not claim finer precision.

Results distinguish DefinitelyImpacted, ConservativelyImpacted through opaque, partial, or missing state, Unaffected, Inconclusive resource exhaustion or cancellation, and InternalFault invariant failure. Every impacted module carries a canonical shortest witness path with typed edges and terminal reason. Edge kinds include change seed, owns declaration, owns extension range, direct import with all flags, effective visibility/state propagation, missing target, and opaque/partial boundary. Tie breaks use explicit ModuleId and edge-kind order, never hash, allocation, insertion, or worker order. Definitely impacted always outranks conservative classification for the same module, while all equal-length witnesses use the frozen tie policy.

Opaque or incomplete extension state stops fine-grained reasoning at that boundary but continues conservative module-level reverse propagation. Equal-digest unequal-value collision evidence is an InternalFault/conflict input, never silently unchanged. A corrupt cycle or inconsistent forward/reverse row in an allegedly validated state is InternalFault with no authoritative cone. No finding from Inconclusive or InternalFault may be cached as acceptance, rejection, or an authoritative unaffected result.

Define independent limits for modules discovered, import rows examined, index rows examined, witness edges/bytes, names/name depth, output records, and total encoded/logged bytes. Check limits before expansion or output allocation. Exhaustion and cancellation return a typed Inconclusive envelope that may include a deterministic diagnostic prefix plus exact usage facts, but the prefix is explicitly non-authoritative and cannot drive cache reuse. Cancellation observation and budget tie points are fixed so replay with the same seed/budget is deterministic.

Tests compare random edit cones, grades, and witnesses against an independent graph/index model; cover every change class, import flag, completeness grade, missing-target transition, diamond and equal-shortest tie, validated DAG and corrupted-cycle/inconsistent-index refusal, stale logical/provenance roots, cancellation at every fixed observation point, and every exact resource boundary. Insertion and thread-order metamorphics must produce byte-identical authoritative output. Named mutants omit an import edge, declaration/extension reverse edge, opaque stop, conservative propagation, witness reason, collision check, or root check. Emit detailed schema-versioned line-oriented unit evidence with request/root ids, change seed/class, examined module/import/index counts, grade, canonical witnesses, limits/usage, expected/actual outcome, authority flag, cacheability, and final state.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates. No fine invalidation through opaque data, name-only seed resolution, nondeterministic witness, unbounded traversal, authoritative partial result, or rejection cached from inconclusive work.

## Acceptance Criteria

All five change classes must produce bounded deterministic graded module cones from the single committed provenance/index state, with exact reverse-index seed resolution, stale logical/provenance-root validation, canonical typed shortest witnesses, fixed cancellation points, and FL-INV-07 authority/cache rules. Independent random models, all flag/completeness/missing/collision/corruption/boundary/cancellation tables, schedule metamorphics, named mutants, detailed versioned evidence, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Advisory-only, name-guessed, unbounded, or authoritatively partial queries fail closure.
acceptance_criteria: All five change classes must produce bounded deterministic graded module cones from the single committed provenance/index state, with exact reverse-index seed resolution, stale logical/provenance-root validation, canonical typed shortest witnesses, fixed cancellation points, and FL-INV-07 authority/cache rules. Independent random models, all flag/completeness/missing/collision/corruption/boundary/cancellation tables, schedule metamorphics, named mutants, detailed versioned evidence, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Every negative or Inconclusive result proves nonpublication and is followed by a successful recovery query against the unchanged committed roots. The shared real decoded-fixture no-mock E2E is owned by fln-amv.9.4 and must exercise invalidation, failure, and recovery before the parent closes. Advisory-only, name-guessed, unbounded, authoritatively partial, or independently E2E-complete claims fail closure.

title: fln-env: preserve module provenance across snapshots checkpoints and semantic merges
description:
Child 4 of 4 and one of two parallel completion blockers for fln-amv.9.3 after the atomic committed-state contract. It is independent of the invalidation-query child: both consume the atomic state, and the parent joins both before integration. Integrate the atomic committed Environment plus graph/provenance/index state with immutable branch snapshots and fln-amv.7 extension checkpoints. Unchanged Environment maps, module graph, manifest records, both index directions, contribution arrays, payload Arcs, and checkpoint bases remain structurally shared; clone is bounded Arc work, not traversal.

A bare ExtensionCheckpoint does not carry contributor ownership, so define a paired, schema-versioned provenance checkpoint envelope rather than guessing on restore. It binds checkpoint mode and descriptor, expected base logical and provenance roots, epoch/artifact identities, base journal identity, exact captured range, ordered entry identities, contributor ModuleIds/classes, completeness grades, and the Arc-backed payload entries already owned by the committed state. Suffix mode captures only the provenance suffix plus an exact base precondition; full-journal mode captures all contributor rows needed to rebuild the journal. Capture verifies payload bytes against entry identities and never flattens or duplicates opaque payload storage.

Restore is one preflighted immutable transaction over the combined committed state. Suffix and full-journal restore preserve contributor module, exact entry identity/order/range, artifact and epoch binding, completeness, both index directions, provenance root, and the Environment logical-root law. Stale base/logical/provenance/checkpoint roots, descriptor or mode mismatch, missing contributor rows, unequal-value identity collision, range drift/overlap/gap, incomplete checkpoint data, and every resource limit are typed before publication. Incomplete or opaque input remains explicitly graded and never becomes rejection or verified completeness. Failure leaves the original combined state byte-, root-, and pointer-identical; a subsequent clean restore must succeed.

Snapshots are O(1) and isolated. Nested and repeated checkpoints must bind the exact snapshot they came from; replaying a suffix against a sibling or later branch is stale even when lengths coincide. Exact repeated restore may be idempotent only when actual values, identities, roots, and contributor indexes all match. Metadata-only provenance changes never alter logical_root, while restored declaration or extension semantic bytes affect logical_root exactly through existing Environment rules.

Implement the provenance-state three-way merge primitive consumed later by fln-amv.11 without duplicating that bead global Environment merge. Given base, ours, and theirs committed roots, it returns an immutable ProvenanceMergePlan plus typed conflicts; it does not publish a globally merged Environment. Identical and one-sided states preserve identity; disjoint module origins combine canonically; shared identical payload Arcs remain shared; compatible extension-origin rows follow their registered merge/checkpoint contract; conflicting module/declaration/extension origins, stale bases, epoch/schema mismatch, identity collisions, range drift/overlap, descriptor conflicts, and completeness disagreement are atomic typed conflicts, never last-writer-wins. The plan carries exact preconditions and before/after provenance expectations so fln-amv.11 can revalidate immediately before global publication.

Recompute and verify the separate provenance root after restore and plan construction. Tests cover snapshot isolation and clone-cost facts; nested/repeated suffix and full restore; sibling/cross-branch stale checkpoints including equal-length different-root cases; every idempotent, one-sided, disjoint, compatible, and conflict merge case; exact actual-payload and contributor identity; root sensitivity and metadata-only logical-root noninterference; independent checkpoint/restore/merge models; pointer/allocation sharing; insertion and thread-order matrices; all exact resource failures, cancellation/inconclusive behavior, and clean recovery. Named mutants lose checkpoint contributor identity, a graph/root precondition, completeness, one reverse row, payload binding, sharing, or conflict detection. Emit detailed schema-versioned line-oriented unit logs with base/checkpoint/restored/plan roots and ids, modes, contributor/range facts, sharing/allocation facts, operations, limits/usage, grades, authority/publication flags, expected/actual outcomes, and clean final state.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain-registry checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and all predecessor and parent checks. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock integration remains solely in fln-amv.9.4. No global merge duplication, unpaired checkpoint provenance, advisory-only lifecycle metadata, payload flattening, partial publication, stale-root acceptance, or hidden logical-root change.

## Acceptance Criteria

O(1) snapshots and paired provenance checkpoints for both checkpoint modes must preserve actual payload/contributor identities, ranges, both index directions, completeness, sharing, separate roots, and the logical-root law through preflighted atomic restore. The three-way operation must return the exact revalidated ProvenanceMergePlan consumed by fln-amv.11, not duplicate global Environment merge. Every stale/equal-length-sibling/collision/range/descriptor/origin/completeness/resource/cancellation path, independent lifecycle model, positive/negative/idempotence matrix, schedule metamorphic, sharing/allocation fact, named mutant, detailed versioned log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, and predecessor gate is mandatory. Real fixture E2E remains in fln-amv.9.4.
acceptance_criteria: O(1) snapshots and paired provenance checkpoints for both checkpoint modes must preserve actual payload/contributor identities, ranges, both index directions, completeness, sharing, separate roots, and the logical-root law through preflighted atomic restore. The three-way operation must return the exact revalidated ProvenanceMergePlan consumed by fln-amv.11, not duplicate global Environment merge. Every stale/equal-length-sibling/collision/range/descriptor/origin/completeness/resource/cancellation path, independent lifecycle model, positive/negative/idempotence matrix, schedule metamorphic, sharing/allocation fact, named mutant, detailed versioned log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, and predecessor gate is mandatory. Real fixture E2E remains in fln-amv.9.4.

title: fln-kernel K1: recursor-reduction slice — KR-205 dispatch, KR-316 iota, KR-317 K-conversion, KR-955 Quot computation (zht follow-up, closes z6c false-reject families)
description: The z6c kernel replay (tribunal/fixtures/c3/KERNEL_REPLAY_FINDINGS.md) triaged all 522 Init.Prelude false-rejects to named reduction-gap families; ~489 (eliminators, custom eliminators, equation-lemma helpers, decidability instances, brecOn helpers, match auxiliaries) require iota, plus proj-family cases that need iota/structure-eta to expose constructors. Scope, implemented exactly per the vendored pin (KERNEL_CONTRACT.md anchors into vendor/lean4-src/src/kernel/): (1) KR-205 recursor dispatch in whnf_core — quot computation first, then inductive iota, fired when the application head is a stable Const; (2) KR-316 iota: major premise at nparams+nmotives+nminors+nindices, whnf'd, with K conversion (KR-317), Nat-literal-to-constructor conversion, and structure-eta coercion applied before rule matching; matching RecursorRule rhs instantiated with the recursor's levels, applied to params+motives+minors, then the constructor's last nfields args, then trailing args; (3) KR-317 to_cnstr_when_K: k-flagged recursors replace any major whose whnf'd inferred type has the right inductive head with the nullary constructor, gated on type defeq; (4) KR-955 quot_reduce_rec: Quot.lift (mk at 5, f at 3) and Quot.ind (mk at 4, p at 3), mk must whnf to a 3-arg Quot.mk application, trailing args preserved, gated on env quot initialization (QuotVal present). OUT of scope (documented follow-ups, absence only under-accepts, never over-accepts): KR-313 Nat literal acceleration (needs fln-bignum), KR-314 String literal expansion, KR-315 unit-like eta, KR-903 structure eta in defeq, iota RHS admission checks (KR-803 lands with inductive admission). Verification: mutation-killed unit tests through the public surface for every new rule branch (wrong-rule-selection, missing-K, wrong quot positions, dropped trailing args, under-applied recursor no-fire), budget/Inconclusive behavior on reduction loops, covenant guards stay green (12 KLOC, forbid unsafe, dep set unchanged), and the z6c kernel replay re-run as the no-mock E2E with before/after rejection counts recorded on this bead and z6c.
acceptance_criteria: KR-205 dispatch, KR-316 iota, KR-317 K-conversion, and KR-955 Quot computation match the pinned contract, preserve trailing arguments, honor under-application and initialization guards, and return typed budget/cancellation outcomes. Named public-surface tests kill wrong-rule, missing-K, wrong-position, dropped-trailing-argument, and no-fire mutants without broadening authority. The real z6c olean replay records exact before/after rejection-family counts, foreign-witness evidence where applicable, a negative lane, and a clean recovery replay in retained human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts with the fields specified in Notes. At each declared thread profile, two runs of the same fixture with identical epoch, budget, seed, and engine build must produce byte-identical reduced terms, verdicts, diagnostic identities, consumption facts, and canonicalized semantic NDJSON fields; monotonic timings and artifact paths are explicitly excluded from equality. Any repeat divergence is a finding, never normalized away. Covenant, fln-kernel, replay, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, and UBS gates are green. Deferred KR-313/KR-314/KR-315/KR-903/KR-803 work remains explicit and is not claimed by this closure.
notes: Verification evidence is retained in both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON. The no-mock z6c replay records run/scenario/step and KR rule ids, bead/invariant/epoch, exact fixture and module hashes, reference and engine build identity, per-family before/after accepted/rejected/Inconclusive/InternalFault counts, selected-rule and major-premise facts, expected versus actual reduced term/verdict/error/consumption, budgets, thread/seed, mutant id, first divergence, command/exit status, stdout/stderr artifact references, cleanup, and asserted final recovery state. A deliberately invalid, under-applied, wrong-rule, or wrong-Quot-position input is the negative lane; a clean replay of the valid fixture after every failure proves recovery and nonpollution. Log volume is bounded and failures remain diagnosable from retained artifacts alone.
close_reason: Landed 75203bf: KR-205/316/317/955 implemented per pin; 44/44 tests, 6/6 mutants killed (verified by application), no-mock replay E2E green — Prelude census 1233→1412 accepted, 522→343 rejected, 0 Inconclusive, no new rejection class. Residual families decomposed into fln-d4x (KR-903/315 defeq eta) and franken_lean-irm (KR-313/314 literals). Gates: fmt/clippy/UBS/structure-guard green, covenant 1903/12000 LOC.

title: W11: Terminal InfoView client — fln goals over Lantern RPC
description:
Implement the terminal InfoView half of plan §16.4 as a standalone product. fln goals attaches to a real Lantern session over the exact same versioned RPC and immutable ProofStateSnapshot contract used by editor clients; it never maintains a second proof-state truth. Render live goals, hypotheses, hover data, diagnostics, progress grades, and supported widget projections through frankentui for SSH, tmux, narrow terminals, no-color mode, and assistive plain-text use. Consume the OQ-7 widget-fidelity decision from franken_lean-v2p: unsupported or exploratory widget content is explicitly graded and rendered with a useful fallback, never silently dropped or executed with broader authority.

The human surface uses stable ANSI/text projections and diff-based redraw without flicker. The robot surface is decoration-free, line-oriented, schema-versioned JSON/NDJSON with request, snapshot, publication-generation, goal, and evidence identifiers. Reconnect, release, cancellation, stale snapshot, epoch mismatch, daemon restart, terminal resize, Unicode width, large goal, bounded rendering, and backpressure behavior are typed. A stale or provisional goal can never be presented as verified or used as publication authority.

Verification includes rendering goldens across width/color/Unicode matrices; RPC parity properties proving terminal and editor views derive from the same snapshot; deterministic redraw and no-op update tests; session reference/release and resource-boundary tests; and named mutants for skipped generation recheck, dropped diagnostic, reordered hypotheses, unbounded widget rendering, ANSI leakage into robot output, and provisional-to-verified promotion. The real no-mock E2E runs fln goals in a PTY/tmux-style session against a production Lantern daemon, edits a real Lean file, observes progressive goals, exercises stale/disconnect/unsupported-widget failures, reconnects cleanly, and proves final snapshot/session cleanup.

Emit concise human diagnostics and retained bounded NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate, exact redacted command and cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/terminal facts, daemon/session/request/snapshot/generation ids, source and canonical input hashes, expected versus actual goal/hover/widget/diagnostic state, timings, resources, exit/signal, stdout/stderr/transcript references, first divergence, recovery, cleanup, and asserted final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The terminal client is complete only when real Lantern RPC parity, live goal updates, OQ-7 widget behavior, generation safety, resource bounds, deterministic rendering, robot schema, all named unit/property/mutation cases, and the real PTY negative/recovery E2E pass with archived reproducible evidence. No editor-only mock, duplicated proof-state store, stale/provisional authority, ANSI-contaminated robot output, skipped fallback, or deferred listed behavior satisfies closure.

title: W11: WASM Judge browser — offline sealed-capsule verification
description:
Implement the WASM Judge half of plan §16.4b according to the versioned OQ-12 packaging contract. Ship the certified K1 and structurally independent fln-checker seats, bounded sealed-capsule decoder, certificate and receipt verification, transparency-log inclusion/consistency checks, and an integrity-bound stock-browser shell. A user can open the package offline, select or drag a real .flnpack, re-check declarations and dependency closure against the same canonical vectors as native execution, inspect verdicts and graph slices, and export a verifiable result without trusting a network service.

Preserve the trust boundary exactly. Unknown package/capsule/certificate versions fail closed; UI, worker, core WASM, and fixture assets are content-bound by the selected packaging contract; no JavaScript result can manufacture kernel acceptance; disagreement halts; certificate failure falls back to bounded recomputation where the contract permits; resource exhaustion/cancellation is Inconclusive; no filesystem, network, platform libm, native plugin, or hidden oracle participates. Full browser elaboration remains explicitly outside Tier 1 and cannot inflate the Judge claim.

The browser surface is accessible by keyboard and screen reader, gives progressive but honestly graded progress, renders stable typed errors for corrupt/epoch-mismatched/unsupported/oversized inputs, and survives worker restart, refresh, retry, and interrupted capsule streaming without remembering false success. Declaration and dependency browsing is bounded and paginated. Browser-local state is versioned and never treated as attestation without fresh integrity and receipt verification.

Verification includes unit/property/model tests for manifest binding, version negotiation, streaming decode, budgets, certificate fallback, receipt/log consistency, result export/import, worker isolation, accessibility state, and native/WASM vector identity. Named mutants bypass an asset hash, accept an unknown version, trust a UI verdict, skip one engine seat, promote Inconclusive, drop a dependency, ignore a byte flip, reuse stale browser state, or report success before terminal verification. The no-mock E2E runs offline in every declared stock-browser engine against a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule, verifies native-vector identity, rejects tampered capsule and mismatched UI/core assets, exercises budget exhaustion and worker crash, refreshes/retries, and proves clean recovery and final state.

Emit concise accessible human status and retained bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate, browser/platform/epoch/mode/profile/build and asset hashes, package/capsule/receipt/certificate/input/output ids, policy and engine seats, expected versus actual verdict/error/consumption, monotonic timings, memory and decode budgets, worker/exit status, console and artifact references, first divergence, recovery, cleanup, and asserted final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.
acceptance_criteria: The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. For every declared browser engine, two cold-state runs of the same package, capsule, epoch, mode, policy, and budgets must produce byte-identical exported verification payloads and identical canonical semantic records: declaration order, dependency closure, seat verdicts, errors, consumption, receipt/certificate ids, and final state. Timings, browser-generated run ids, and artifact paths are explicitly excluded from equality; worker scheduling, refresh, and retry may not change semantics. Package assembly also repeats from the same locked inputs and must yield identical asset order, hashes, manifest, and distributable bytes. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, nondeterministic semantic/export output, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.

title: fln-env E2E: migrate legacy env_snapshots parent to authoritative evidence schema
description: The shared scripts/e2e/env_snapshots.sh parent still publishes legacy fln-e2e/1 records and treats any nonzero extension-mutant run as a kill. Migrate the outer environment scenario to the authoritative fln.e2e/2 lifecycle instead of relying on a nested child for only fln-amv.10. Add a durable terminal finalizer for setup failures, timeout, cancellation, inconclusive execution, and internal faults; supervise every command with exact wrapper and child classifications; bind the live input and vendor tree; emit separate stdout and stderr artifacts; validate semantic extension-mutant failure signatures so compile errors and unrelated test failures cannot pass; restore from byte-identical pristine input; and publish a manifest plus complete-bundle commitment only after strict validation. Preserve the nested fln-amv.10 child as a separately scoped bounded-model claim or fold it into a composition that retains exact child identity without duplicating detail rows. No deletion of retained historical bundles.

title: fln-kernel K1: defeq-eta slice — KR-903 structure eta + KR-315 unit-like eta (residual eliminator-family probe)
description: Follow-up to franken_lean-5p2. After the recursor-reduction slice, the Init.Prelude replay still false-rejects 343 declarations; the dominant family (244 eliminators) is concentrated in typeclass-structure casesOn/recOn/noConfusionType/below (e.g. Membership.casesOn, HDiv.noConfusionType, Lean.Name.below), all TypeMismatch/DefinitionTypeMismatch. HYPOTHESIS (typed, unverified): these need KR-903 structure eta in defeq (try_eta_struct_core: t ≟ mk as fs for a one-constructor, non-recursive, index-free structure holds when the types agree and every field fi is defeq to t.i, both directions) and possibly KR-315 unit-like eta (two terms of the same one-constructor zero-field structure type are defeq when their types are). FIRST TASK: root-cause ONE case from each residual sub-family from the real olean (decode Membership.casesOn, replay with instrumentation, capture the exact defeq query that fails) — no implementation before the gap is evidence-anchored; if the probe refutes the KR-903 hypothesis, re-triage before coding. Implement per pin anchor vendor/lean4-src/src/kernel/type_checker.cpp:793 (try_eta_struct_core) and :1044 (is_def_eq_unit_like), wired into the is_def_eq ladder at the pin's position. Verification: mutation-killed unit tests per rule branch (eta disabled, one-direction-only, Prop-structure exclusion if pin has one, wrong-field-index), kernel replay re-run with before/after census recorded (expect the 244-family to convert if hypothesis holds), fmt/clippy/UBS/structure-guard green, covenant intact.

title: fln-kernel K1: literal-acceleration slice — KR-313 Nat ops + KR-314 String expansion (wires fln-bignum)
description: Follow-up to franken_lean-5p2. KR-313: on Nat literals the kernel computes succ/add/sub/mul/div/mod/gcd/pow(exp≤2^24)/beq/ble/land/lor/xor/shiftLeft/shiftRight (pin anchor type_checker.cpp:609 reduce_nat; divergence note: no Nat.blt at this pin — beq/ble only), gated on closed terms; requires wiring fln-bignum into fln-kernel (covenant already names fln-bignum as an allowed dependency). Also Nat.zero ⇄ lit 0 normalization so literal-vs-symbolic constructor forms are defeq (the k1_judgments comment in kr316_nat_literal_majors_convert_to_constructor_form marks exactly this boundary). KR-314: a String literal is defeq to its String.ofList constructor form both directions, and projection/recursor machinery expands literals the same way (anchor type_checker.cpp:1030) — closes the inductive_reduce_rec Str-literal early-return documented in tc.rs. Converts the remaining nat-literal replay family (5 in Init.Prelude: UInt*.ofNatLT/Char.ofNatAux) plus any decidability cases blocked on literal arithmetic. Verification: operation-table tests vs known values including the pow cap and the no-blt divergence note, mutation kills (wrong op mapping, dropped cap), replay census before/after, gates + covenant (LOC growth budgeted — bignum calls, not reimplementation).
